Genesis1:1 In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
Genesis1:2 The earth was without form and void
Genesis1:3 And God said
Genesis1:4 And God saw that the light was good; and God separated the light from the darkness.
Genesis1:5 God called the light Day
Genesis1:6 And God said
Genesis1:7 And God made the firmament and separated the waters which were under the firmament from the waters which were above the firmament. And it was so.
Genesis1:8 And God called the firmament Heaven. And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:9 And God said
Genesis1:10 God called the dry land Earth
Genesis1:11 And God said
Genesis1:12 The earth brought forth vegetation
Genesis1:13 And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:14 And God said
Genesis1:15 and let them be lights in the firmament of the heavens to give light upon the earth.” And it was so.
Genesis1:16 And God made the two great lights
Genesis1:17 And God set them in the firmament of the heavens to give light upon the earth
Genesis1:18 to rule over the day and over the night
Genesis1:19 And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:20 And God said
Genesis1:21 So God created the great sea monsters and every living creature that moves
Genesis1:22 And God blessed them
Genesis1:23 And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:24 And God said
Genesis1:25 And God made the beasts of the earth according to their kinds and the cattle according to their kinds
Genesis1:26 Then God said
Genesis1:27 So God created man in his own image
Genesis1:28 And God blessed them
Genesis1:29 And God said
Genesis1:30 And to every beast of the earth
Genesis1:31 And God saw everything that he had made
Genesis2:1 Thus the heavens and the earth were finished
Genesis2:2 And on the seventh day God finished his work which he had done
Genesis2:3 So God blessed the seventh day and hallowed it
Genesis2:4 These are the generations of the heavens and the earth when they were created. In the day that the Lord God made the earth and the heavens
Genesis2:5 when no plant of the field was yet in the earth and no herb of the field had yet sprung up—for the Lord God had not caused it to rain upon the earth
Genesis2:6 but a mist went up from the earth and watered the whole face of the ground—
Genesis2:7 then the Lord God formed man of dust from the ground
Genesis2:8 And the Lord God planted a garden in Eden
Genesis2:9 And out of the ground the Lord God made to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight and good for food
Genesis2:10 A river flowed out of Eden to water the garden
Genesis2:11 The name of the first is Pishon; it is the one which flows around the whole land of Havilah
Genesis2:12 and the gold of that land is good; bdellium and onyx stone are there.
Genesis2:13 The name of the second river is Gihon; it is the one which flows around the whole land of Cush.
Genesis2:14 And the name of the third river is Tigris
Genesis2:15 The Lord God took the man and put him in the garden of Eden to till it and keep it.
Genesis2:16 And the Lord God commanded the man
Genesis2:17 but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat
Genesis2:18 Then the Lord God said
Genesis2:19 So out of the ground the Lord God formed every beast of the field and every bird of the air
Genesis2:20 The man gave names to all cattle
Genesis2:21 So the Lord God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man
Genesis2:22 and the rib which the Lord God had taken from the man he made into a woman and brought her to the man.
Genesis2:23 Then the man said
Genesis2:24 Therefore a man leaves his father and his mother and cleaves to his wife
Genesis2:25 And the man and his wife were both naked

Genesis5:1 This is the book of the generations of Adam. When God created man
Genesis5:2 Male and female he created them
Genesis5:3 When Adam had lived a hundred and thirty years
Genesis5:4 The days of Adam after he became the father of Seth were eight hundred years; and he had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:5 Thus all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and thirty years; and he died.
Genesis5:6 When Seth had lived a hundred and five years
Genesis5:7 Seth lived after the birth of Enosh eight hundred and seven years
Genesis5:8 Thus all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve years; and he died.
Genesis5:9 When Enosh had lived ninety years
Genesis5:10 Enosh lived after the birth of Kenan eight hundred and fifteen years
Genesis5:11 Thus all the days of Enosh were nine hundred and five years; and he died.
Genesis5:12 When Kenan had lived seventy years
Genesis5:13 Kenan lived after the birth of Ma-halalel eight hundred and forty years
Genesis5:14 Thus all the days of Kenan were nine hundred and ten years; and he died.
Genesis5:15 When Ma-halalel had lived sixty-five years
Genesis5:16 Ma-halalel lived after the birth of Jared eight hundred and thirty years
Genesis5:17 Thus all the days of Ma-halalel were eight hundred and ninety-five years; and he died.
Genesis5:18 When Jared had lived a hundred and sixty-two years he became the father of Enoch.
Genesis5:19 Jared lived after the birth of Enoch eight hundred years
Genesis5:20 Thus all the days of Jared were nine hundred and sixty-two years; and he died.
Genesis5:21 When Enoch had lived sixty-five years
Genesis5:22 Enoch walked with God after the birth of Methuselah three hundred years
Genesis5:23 Thus all the days of Enoch were three hundred and sixty-five years.
Genesis5:24 Enoch walked with God; and he was not
Genesis5:25 When Methuselah had lived a hundred and eighty-seven years
Genesis5:26 Methuselah lived after the birth of Lamech seven hundred and eighty-two years
Genesis5:27 Thus all the days of Methuselah were nine hundred and sixty-nine years; and he died.
Genesis5:28 When Lamech had lived a hundred and eighty-two years
Genesis5:29 and called his name Noah
Genesis5:30 Lamech lived after the birth of Noah five hundred and ninety-five years
Genesis5:31 Thus all the days of Lamech were seven hundred and seventy-seven years; and he died.
Genesis5:32 After Noah was five hundred years old

Genesis7:20 the waters prevailed above the mountains

Genesis7:22 everything on the dry land in whose nostrils was the breath of life died.
Genesis7:23 He blotted out every living thing that was upon the face of the ground
Genesis7:24 And the waters prevailed upon the earth a hundred and fifty days.

Genesis8:5 And the waters continued to abate until the tenth month; in the tenth month
Genesis8:6 At the end of forty days Noah opened the window of the ark which he had made

Genesis8:9 but the dove found no place to set her foot

Exodus12:20 You shall eat nothing leavened; in all your dwellings you shall eat unleavened bread.”

Exodus28:16 It shall be square and double
Exodus28:17 And you shall set in it four rows of stones. A row of sardius
Exodus28:18 and the second row an emerald
Exodus28:19 and the third row a jacinth
Exodus28:20 and the fourth row a beryl
Exodus28:21 There shall be twelve stones with their names according to the names of the sons of Israel; they shall be like signets
Exodus28:22 And you shall make for the breastpiece twisted chains like cords
Exodus28:23 and you shall make for the breastpiece two rings of gold
Exodus28:24 And you shall put the two cords of gold in the two rings at the edges of the breastpiece;
Exodus28:25 the two ends of the two cords you shall attach to the two settings of filigree
Exodus28:26 And you shall make two rings of gold
Exodus28:27 And you shall make two rings of gold
Exodus28:28 And they shall bind the breastpiece by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a lace of blue
Exodus28:29 So Aaron shall bear the names of the sons of Israel in the breastpiece of judgment upon his heart
Exodus28:30 And in the breastpiece of judgment you shall put the Urim and the Thummim
Exodus28:31 “And you shall make the robe of the ephod all of blue.
Exodus28:32 It shall have in it an opening for the head
Exodus28:33 On its skirts you shall make pomegranates of blue and purple and scarlet stuff
Exodus28:34 a golden bell and a pomegranate
Exodus28:35 And it shall be upon Aaron when he ministers
Exodus28:36 “And you shall make a plate of pure gold
Exodus28:37 And you shall fasten it on the turban by a lace of blue; it shall be on the front of the turban.
Exodus28:38 It shall be upon Aaron's forehead
Exodus28:39 “And you shall weave the coat in checker work of fine linen
Exodus28:40 “And for Aaron's sons you shall make coats and girdles and caps; you shall make them for glory and beauty.
Exodus28:41 And you shall put them upon Aaron your brother
Exodus28:42 And you shall make for them linen breeches to cover their naked flesh; from the loins to the thighs they shall reach;
Exodus28:43 and they shall be upon Aaron
Exodus29:1 “Now this is what you shall do to them to consecrate them
Exodus29:2 and unleavened bread
Exodus29:3 And you shall put them in one basket and bring them in the basket
Exodus29:4 You shall bring Aaron and his sons to the door of the tent of meeting
Exodus29:5 And you shall take the garments
Exodus29:6 and you shall set the turban on his head
Exodus29:7 And you shall take the anointing oil
Exodus29:8 Then you shall bring his sons
Exodus29:9 and you shall gird them with girdles and bind caps on them; and the priesthood shall be theirs by a perpetual statute. Thus you shall ordain Aaron and his sons.
Exodus29:10 “Then you shall bring the bull before the tent of meeting. Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands upon the head of the bull
Exodus29:11 and you shall kill the bull before the Lord
Exodus29:12 and shall take part of the blood of the bull and put it upon the horns of the altar with your finger
Exodus29:13 And you shall take all the fat that covers the entrails
Exodus29:14 But the flesh of the bull
Exodus29:15 “Then you shall take one of the rams
Exodus29:16 and you shall slaughter the ram
Exodus29:17 Then you shall cut the ram into pieces
Exodus29:18 and burn the whole ram upon the altar; it is a burnt offering to the Lord; it is a pleasing odor
Exodus29:19 “You shall take the other ram; and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands upon the head of the ram
Exodus29:20 and you shall kill the ram
Exodus29:21 Then you shall take part of the blood that is on the altar
Exodus29:22 “You shall also take the fat of the ram
Exodus29:23 and one loaf of bread
Exodus29:24 and you shall put all these in the hands of Aaron and in the hands of his sons
Exodus29:25 Then you shall take them from their hands
Exodus29:26 “And you shall take the breast of the ram of Aaron's ordination and wave if for a wave offering before the Lord; and it shall be your portion.
Exodus29:27 And you shall consecrate the breast of the wave offering
Exodus29:28 It shall be for Aaron and his sons as a perpetual due from the people of Israel
Exodus29:29 “The holy garments of Aaron shall be for his sons after him
Exodus29:30 The son who is priest in his place shall wear them seven days
Exodus29:31 “You shall take the ram of ordination
Exodus29:32 and Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread that is in the basket
Exodus29:33 They shall eat those things with which atonement was made
Exodus29:34 And if any of the flesh for the ordination
Exodus29:35 “Thus you shall do to Aaron and to his sons
Exodus29:36 and every day you shall offer a bull as a sin offering for atonement. Also you shall offer a sin offering for the altar
Exodus29:37 Seven days you shall make atonement for the altar
Exodus29:38 “Now this is what you shall offer upon the altar: two lambs a year old day by day continually.
Exodus29:39 One lamb you shall offer in the morning
Exodus29:40 and with the first lamb a tenth measure of fine flour mingled with a fourth of a hin of beaten oil
Exodus29:41 And the other lamb you shall offer in the evening
Exodus29:42 It shall be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations at the door of the tent of meeting before the Lord
Exodus29:43 There I will meet with the people of Israel
Exodus29:44 I will consecrate the tent of meeting and the altar; Aaron also and his sons I will consecrate
Exodus29:45 And I will dwell among the people of Israel
Exodus29:46 And they shall know that I am the Lord their God
Exodus30:1 “You shall make an altar to burn incense upon; of acacia wood shall you make it.
Exodus30:2 A cubit shall be its length
Exodus30:3 And you shall overlay it with pure gold
Exodus30:4 And two golden rings shall you make for it; under its molding on two opposite sides of it shall you make them
Exodus30:5 You shall make the poles of acacia wood
Exodus30:6 And you shall put it before the veil that is by the ark of the testimony
Exodus30:7 And Aaron shall burn fragrant incense on it; every morning when he dresses the lamps he shall burn it
Exodus30:8 and when Aaron sets up the lamps in the evening
Exodus30:9 You shall offer no unholy incense thereon
Exodus30:10 Aaron shall make atonement upon its horns once a year; with the blood of the sin offering of atonement he shall make atonement for it once in the year throughout your generations; it is most holy to the Lord.”
Exodus30:11 The Lord said to Moses
Exodus30:12 “When you take the census of the people of Israel
Exodus30:13 Each who is numbered in the census shall give this: half a shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary (the shekel is twenty gerahs)
Exodus30:14 Every one who is numbered in the census
Exodus30:15 The rich shall not give more
Exodus30:16 And you shall take the atonement money from the people of Israel
Exodus30:17 The Lord said to Moses

Exodus30:22 Moreover

Exodus30:24 and of cassia five hundred
Exodus30:25 and you shall make of these a sacred anointing oil blended as by the perfumer; a holy anointing oil it shall be.
Exodus30:26 And you shall anoint with it the tent of meeting and the ark of the testimony
Exodus30:27 and the table and all its utensils
Exodus30:28 and the altar of burnt offering with all its utensils and the laver and its base;
Exodus30:29 you shall consecrate them
Exodus30:30 And you shall anoint Aaron and his sons
Exodus30:31 And you shall say to the people of Israel
Exodus30:32 It shall not be poured upon the bodies of ordinary men
Exodus30:33 Whoever compounds any like it or whoever puts any of it on an outsider shall be cut off from his people.’ ”
Exodus30:34 And the Lord said to Moses
Exodus30:35 and make an incense blended as by the perfumer
Exodus30:36 and you shall beat some of it very small
Exodus30:37 And the incense which you shall make according to its composition
Exodus30:38 Whoever makes any like it to use as perfume shall be cut off from his people.”

Exodus40:1 The Lord said to Moses
Exodus40:2 “On the first day of the first month you shall erect the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.
Exodus40:3 And you shall put in it the ark of the testimony
Exodus40:4 And you shall bring in the table
Exodus40:5 And you shall put the golden altar for incense before the ark of the testimony
Exodus40:6 You shall set the altar of burnt offering before the door of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting
Exodus40:7 and place the laver between the tent of meeting and the altar
Exodus40:8 And you shall set up the court round about
Exodus40:9 Then you shall take the anointing oil
Exodus40:10 You shall also anoint the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils
Exodus40:11 You shall also anoint the laver and its base
Exodus40:12 Then you shall bring Aaron and his sons to the door of the tent of meeting
Exodus40:13 and put upon Aaron the holy garments
Exodus40:14 You shall bring his sons also and put coats on them
Exodus40:15 and anoint them
Exodus40:16 Thus did Moses; according to all that the Lord commanded him
Exodus40:17 And in the first month in the second year
Exodus40:18 Moses erected the tabernacle; he laid its bases
Exodus40:19 and he spread the tent over the tabernacle
Exodus40:20 And he took the testimony and put it into the ark
Exodus40:21 and he brought the ark into the tabernacle
Exodus40:22 And he put the table in the tent of meeting
Exodus40:23 and set the bread in order on it before the Lord; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
Exodus40:24 And he put the lampstand in the tent of meeting
Exodus40:25 and set up the lamps before the Lord; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
Exodus40:26 And he put the golden altar in the tent of meeting before the veil
Exodus40:27 and burnt fragrant incense upon it; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
Exodus40:28 And he put in place the screen for the door of the tabernacle.
Exodus40:29 And he set the altar of burnt offering at the door of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting
Exodus40:30 And he set the laver between the tent of meeting and the altar
Exodus40:31 with which Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet;
Exodus40:32 when they went into the tent of meeting
Exodus40:33 And he erected the court round the tabernacle and the altar
Exodus40:34 Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting
Exodus40:35 And Moses was not able to enter the tent of meeting
Exodus40:36 Throughout all their journeys
Exodus40:37 but if the cloud was not taken up
Exodus40:38 For throughout all their journeys the cloud of the Lord was upon the tabernacle by day
Leviticus1:1 The Lord called Moses
Leviticus1:2 “Speak to the people of Israel
Leviticus1:3 “If his offering is a burnt offering from the herd
Leviticus1:4 he shall lay his hand upon the head of the burnt offering
Leviticus1:5 Then he shall kill the bull before the Lord; and Aaron's sons the priests shall present the blood
Leviticus1:6 And he shall flay the burnt offering and cut it into pieces;
Leviticus1:7 and the sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire on the altar
Leviticus1:8 and Aaron's sons the priests shall lay the pieces
Leviticus1:9 but its entrails and its legs he shall wash with water. And the priest shall burn the whole on the altar
Leviticus1:10 “If his gift for a burnt offering is from the flock
Leviticus1:11 and he shall kill it on the north side of the altar before the Lord
Leviticus1:12 And he shall cut it into pieces
Leviticus1:13 but the entrails and the legs he shall wash with water. And the priest shall offer the whole
Leviticus1:14 “If his offering to the Lord is a burnt offering of birds
Leviticus1:15 And the priest shall bring it to the altar and wring off its head
Leviticus1:16 and he shall take away its crop with the feathers
Leviticus1:17 he shall tear it by its wings
Leviticus2:1 “When any one brings a cereal offering as an offering to the Lord
Leviticus2:2 and bring it to Aaron's sons the priests. And he shall take from it a handful of the fine flour and oil
Leviticus2:3 And what is left of the cereal offering shall be for Aaron and his sons; it is a most holy part of the offerings by fire to the Lord.
Leviticus2:4 “When you bring a cereal offering baked in the oven as an offering
Leviticus2:5 And if your offering is a cereal offering baked on a griddle
Leviticus2:6 you shall break it in pieces
Leviticus2:7 And if your offering is a cereal offering cooked in a pan
Leviticus2:8 And you shall bring the cereal offering that is made of these things to the Lord; and when it is presented to the priest
Leviticus2:9 And the priest shall take from the cereal offering its memorial portion and burn this on the altar
Leviticus2:10 And what is left of the cereal offering shall be for Aaron and his sons; it is a most holy part of the offerings by fire to the Lord.
Leviticus2:11 “No cereal offering which you bring to the Lord shall be made with leaven; for you shall burn no leaven nor any honey as an offering by fire to the Lord.
Leviticus2:12 As an offering of first fruits you may bring them to the Lord
Leviticus2:13 You shall season all your cereal offerings with salt; you shall not let the salt of the covenant with your God be lacking from your cereal offering; with all your offerings you shall offer salt.
Leviticus2:14 “If you offer a cereal offering of first fruits to the Lord
Leviticus2:15 And you shall put oil upon it
Leviticus2:16 And the priest shall burn as its memorial portion part of the crushed grain and of the oil with all of its frankincense; it is an offering by fire to the Lord.

Leviticus3:3 And from the sacrifice of the peace offering
Leviticus3:4 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins
Leviticus3:5 Then Aaron's sons shall burn it on the altar upon the burnt offering
Leviticus3:6 “If his offering for a sacrifice of peace offering to the Lord is an animal from the flock
Leviticus3:7 If he offers a lamb for his offering

Leviticus3:9 Then from the sacrifice of the peace offering as an offering by fire to the Lord he shall offer its fat
Leviticus3:10 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins
Leviticus3:11 And the priest shall burn it on the altar as food offered by fire to the Lord.
Leviticus3:12 “If his offering is a goat

Leviticus3:14 Then he shall offer from it
Leviticus3:15 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins
Leviticus3:16 And the priest shall burn them on the altar as food offered by fire for a pleasing odor. All fat is the Lord 's.
Leviticus3:17 It shall be a perpetual statute throughout your generations
Leviticus4:1 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus4:2 “Say to the people of Israel
Leviticus4:3 if it is the anointed priest who sins
Leviticus4:4 He shall bring the bull to the door of the tent of meeting before the Lord
Leviticus4:5 And the anointed priest shall take some of the blood of the bull and bring it to the tent of meeting;
Leviticus4:6 and the priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle part of the blood seven times before the Lord in front of the veil of the sanctuary.
Leviticus4:7 And the priest shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar of fragrant incense before the Lord which is in the tent of meeting
Leviticus4:8 And all the fat of the bull of the sin offering he shall take from it
Leviticus4:9 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins
Leviticus4:10 (just as these are taken from the ox of the sacrifice of the peace offerings)
Leviticus4:11 But the skin of the bull and all its flesh
Leviticus4:12 the whole bull he shall carry forth outside the camp to a clean place
Leviticus4:13 “If the whole congregation of Israel commits a sin unwittingly and the thing is hidden from the eyes of the assembly
Leviticus4:14 when the sin which they have committed becomes known
Leviticus4:15 and the elders of the congregation shall lay their hands upon the head of the bull before the Lord
Leviticus4:16 Then the anointed priest shall bring some of the blood of the bull to the tent of meeting
Leviticus4:17 and the priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle it seven times before the Lord in front of the veil.
Leviticus4:18 And he shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar which is in the tent of meeting before the Lord; and the rest of the blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering which is at the door of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus4:19 And all its fat he shall take from it and burn upon the altar.
Leviticus4:20 Thus shall he do with the bull; as he did with the bull of the sin offering
Leviticus4:21 And he shall carry forth the bull outside the camp
Leviticus4:22 “When a ruler sins
Leviticus4:23 if the sin which he has committed is made known to him
Leviticus4:24 and shall lay his hand upon the head of the goat
Leviticus4:25 Then the priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering
Leviticus4:26 And all its fat he shall burn on the altar
Leviticus4:27 “If any one of the common people sins unwittingly in doing any one of the things which the Lord has commanded not to be done
Leviticus4:28 when the sin which he has committed is made known to him he shall bring for his offering a goat
Leviticus4:29 And he shall lay his hand on the head of the sin offering
Leviticus4:30 And the priest shall take some of its blood with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering
Leviticus4:31 And all its fat he shall remove
Leviticus4:32 “If he brings a lamb as his offering for a sin offering
Leviticus4:33 and lay his hand upon the head of the sin offering
Leviticus4:34 Then the priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering
Leviticus4:35 And all its fat he shall remove as the fat of the lamb is removed from the sacrifice of peace offerings
Leviticus5:1 “If any one sins in that he hears a public adjuration to testify and though he is a witness
Leviticus5:2 Or if any one touches an unclean thing
Leviticus5:3 Or if he touches human uncleanness
Leviticus5:4 Or if any one utters with his lips a rash oath to do evil or to do good

Leviticus5:7 “But if he cannot afford a lamb
Leviticus5:8 He shall bring them to the priest

Leviticus5:11 “But if he cannot afford two turtledoves or two young pigeons

Leviticus5:13 Thus the priest shall make atonement for him for the sin which he has committed in any one of these things

Leviticus5:17 “If any one sins
Leviticus5:18 He shall bring to the priest a ram without blemish out of the flock
Leviticus5:19 It is a guilt offering; he is guilty before the Lord.”

Leviticus6:29 Every male among the priests may eat of it; it is most holy.
Leviticus6:30 But no sin offering shall be eaten from which any blood is brought into the tent of meeting to make atonement in the holy place; it shall be burned with fire.

Leviticus11:1 And the Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus11:2 “Say to the people of Israel
Leviticus11:3 Whatever parts the hoof and is cloven-footed and chews the cud
Leviticus11:4 Nevertheless among those that chew the cud or part the hoof
Leviticus11:5 And the rock badger
Leviticus11:6 And the hare
Leviticus11:7 And the swine
Leviticus11:8 Of their flesh you shall not eat
Leviticus11:9 “These you may eat
Leviticus11:10 But anything in the seas or the rivers that has not fins and scales
Leviticus11:11 They shall remain an abomination to you; of their flesh you shall not eat
Leviticus11:12 Everything in the waters that has not fins and scales is an abomination to you.
Leviticus11:13 “And these you shall have in abomination among the birds
Leviticus11:14 the kite
Leviticus11:15 every raven according to its kind
Leviticus11:16 the ostrich
Leviticus11:17 the owl
Leviticus11:18 the water hen
Leviticus11:19 the stork
Leviticus11:20 “All winged insects that go upon all fours are an abomination to you.
Leviticus11:21 Yet among the winged insects that go on all fours you may eat those which have legs above their feet
Leviticus11:22 Of them you may eat: the locust according to its kind
Leviticus11:23 But all other winged insects which have four feet are an abomination to you.
Leviticus11:24 “And by these you shall become unclean; whoever touches their carcass shall be unclean until the evening
Leviticus11:25 and whoever carries any part of their carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus11:26 Every animal which parts the hoof but is not cloven-footed or does not chew the cud is unclean to you; every one who touches them shall be unclean.
Leviticus11:27 And all that go on their paws
Leviticus11:28 and he who carries their carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening; they are unclean to you.
Leviticus11:29 “And these are unclean to you among the swarming things that swarm upon the earth: the weasel
Leviticus11:30 the gecko
Leviticus11:31 These are unclean to you among all that swarm; whoever touches them when they are dead shall be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus11:32 And anything upon which any of them falls when they are dead shall be unclean
Leviticus11:33 And if any of them falls into any earthen vessel
Leviticus11:34 Any food in it which may be eaten
Leviticus11:35 And everything upon which any part of their carcass falls shall be unclean; whether oven or stove
Leviticus11:36 Nevertheless a spring or a cistern holding water shall be clean; but whatever touches their carcass shall be unclean.
Leviticus11:37 And if any part of their carcass falls upon any seed for sowing that is to be sown
Leviticus11:38 but if water is put on the seed and any part of their carcass falls on it
Leviticus11:39 “And if any animal of which you may eat dies
Leviticus11:40 and he who eats of its carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening; he also who carries the carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus11:41 “Every swarming thing that swarms upon the earth is an abomination; it shall not be eaten.
Leviticus11:42 Whatever goes on its belly
Leviticus11:43 You shall not make yourselves abominable with any swarming thing that swarms; and you shall not defile yourselves with them
Leviticus11:44 For I am the Lord your God; consecrate yourselves therefore
Leviticus11:45 For I am the Lord who brought you up out of the land of Egypt
Leviticus11:46 This is the law pertaining to beast and bird and every living creature that moves through the waters and every creature that swarms upon the earth
Leviticus11:47 to make a distinction between the unclean and the clean and between the living creature that may be eaten and the living creature that may not be eaten.

Leviticus12:2 “Say to the people of Israel

Leviticus12:5 But if she bears a female child
Leviticus12:6 “And when the days of her purifying are completed
Leviticus12:7 and he shall offer it before the Lord
Leviticus12:8 And if she cannot afford a lamb
Leviticus13:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus13:2 “When a man has on the skin of his body a swelling or an eruption or a spot
Leviticus13:3 and the priest shall examine the diseased spot on the skin of his body; and if the hair in the diseased spot has turned white and the disease appears to be deeper than the skin of his body
Leviticus13:4 But if the spot is white in the skin of his body
Leviticus13:5 and the priest shall examine him on the seventh day
Leviticus13:6 and the priest shall examine him again on the seventh day
Leviticus13:7 But if the eruption spreads in the skin
Leviticus13:8 and the priest shall make an examination
Leviticus13:9 “When a man is afflicted with leprosy
Leviticus13:10 and the priest shall make an examination
Leviticus13:11 it is a chronic leprosy in the skin of his body
Leviticus13:12 And if the leprosy breaks out in the skin
Leviticus13:13 then the priest shall make an examination
Leviticus13:14 But when raw flesh appears on him
Leviticus13:15 And the priest shall examine the raw flesh
Leviticus13:16 But if the raw flesh turns again and is changed to white
Leviticus13:17 and the priest shall examine him
Leviticus13:18 “And when there is in the skin of one's body a boil that has healed
Leviticus13:19 and in the place of the boil there comes a white swelling or a reddish-white spot
Leviticus13:20 and the priest shall make an examination
Leviticus13:21 But if the priest examines it
Leviticus13:22 and if it spreads in the skin
Leviticus13:23 But if the spot remains in one place and does not spread
Leviticus13:24 “Or
Leviticus13:25 the priest shall examine it
Leviticus13:26 But if the priest examines it
Leviticus13:27 and the priest shall examine him the seventh day; if it is spreading in the skin
Leviticus13:28 But if the spot remains in one place and does not spread in the skin
Leviticus13:29 “When a man or woman has a disease on the head or the beard
Leviticus13:30 the priest shall examine the disease; and if it appears deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:31 And if the priest examines the itching disease
Leviticus13:32 and on the seventh day the priest shall examine the disease; and if the itch has not spread
Leviticus13:33 then he shall shave himself
Leviticus13:34 and on the seventh day the priest shall examine the itch
Leviticus13:35 But if the itch spreads in the skin after his cleansing
Leviticus13:36 then the priest shall examine him
Leviticus13:37 But if in his eyes the itch is checked
Leviticus13:38 “When a man or a woman has spots on the skin of the body
Leviticus13:39 the priest shall make an examination
Leviticus13:40 “If a man's hair has fallen from his head
Leviticus13:41 And if a man's hair has fallen from his forehead and temples
Leviticus13:42 But if there is on the bald head or the bald forehead a reddish-white diseased spot
Leviticus13:43 Then the priest shall examine him
Leviticus13:44 he is a leprous man
Leviticus13:45 “The leper who has the disease shall wear torn clothes and let the hair of his head hang loose
Leviticus13:46 He shall remain unclean as long as he has the disease; he is unclean; he shall dwell alone in a habitation outside the camp.
Leviticus13:47 “When there is a leprous disease in a garment
Leviticus13:48 in warp or woof of linen or wool
Leviticus13:49 if the disease shows greenish or reddish in the garment
Leviticus13:50 And the priest shall examine the disease
Leviticus13:51 then he shall examine the disease on the seventh day. If the disease has spread in the garment
Leviticus13:52 And he shall burn the garment
Leviticus13:53 “And if the priest examines
Leviticus13:54 then the priest shall command that they wash the thing in which is the disease
Leviticus13:55 and the priest shall examine the diseased thing after it has been washed. And if the diseased spot has not changed color
Leviticus13:56 “But if the priest examines
Leviticus13:57 then if it appears again in the garment
Leviticus13:58 But the garment
Leviticus13:59 This is the law for a leprous disease in a garment of wool or linen
Leviticus14:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus14:2 “This shall be the law of the leper for the day of his cleansing. He shall be brought to the priest;
Leviticus14:3 and the priest shall go out of the camp
Leviticus14:4 the priest shall command them to take for him who is to be cleansed two living clean birds and cedarwood and scarlet stuff and hyssop;
Leviticus14:5 and the priest shall command them to kill one of the birds in an earthen vessel over running water.
Leviticus14:6 He shall take the living bird with the cedarwood and the scarlet stuff and the hyssop
Leviticus14:7 and he shall sprinkle it seven times upon him who is to be cleansed of leprosy; then he shall pronounce him clean
Leviticus14:8 And he who is to be cleansed shall wash his clothes
Leviticus14:9 And on the seventh day he shall shave all his hair off his head; he shall shave off his beard and his eyebrows
Leviticus14:10 “And on the eighth day he shall take two male lambs without blemish
Leviticus14:11 And the priest who cleanses him shall set the man who is to be cleansed and these things before the Lord
Leviticus14:12 And the priest shall take one of the male lambs
Leviticus14:13 and he shall kill the lamb in the place where they kill the sin offering and the burnt offering
Leviticus14:14 The priest shall take some of the blood of the guilt offering
Leviticus14:15 Then the priest shall take some of the log of oil
Leviticus14:16 and dip his right finger in the oil that is in his left hand
Leviticus14:17 And some of the oil that remains in his hand the priest shall put on the tip of the right ear of him who is to be cleansed
Leviticus14:18 and the rest of the oil that is in the priest's hand he shall put on the head of him who is to be cleansed. Then the priest shall make atonement for him before the Lord.
Leviticus14:19 The priest shall offer the sin offering
Leviticus14:20 and the priest shall offer the burnt offering and the cereal offering on the altar. Thus the priest shall make atonement for him
Leviticus14:21 “But if he is poor and cannot afford so much
Leviticus14:22 also two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus14:23 And on the eighth day he shall bring them for his cleansing to the priest
Leviticus14:24 and the priest shall take the lamb of the guilt offering
Leviticus14:25 And he shall kill the lamb of the guilt offering; and the priest shall take some of the blood of the guilt offering
Leviticus14:26 And the priest shall pour some of the oil into the palm of his own left hand;
Leviticus14:27 and shall sprinkle with his right finger some of the oil that is in his left hand seven times before the Lord;
Leviticus14:28 and the priest shall put some of the oil that is in his hand on the tip of the right ear of him who is to be cleansed
Leviticus14:29 and the rest of the oil that is in the priest's hand he shall put on the head of him who is to be cleansed
Leviticus14:30 And he shall offer
Leviticus14:31 one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering
Leviticus14:32 This is the law for him in whom is a leprous disease
Leviticus14:33 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus14:34 “When you come into the land of Canaan
Leviticus14:35 then he who owns the house shall come and tell the priest
Leviticus14:36 Then the priest shall command that they empty the house before the priest goes to examine the disease
Leviticus14:37 And he shall examine the disease; and if the disease is in the walls of the house with greenish or reddish spots
Leviticus14:38 then the priest shall go out of the house to the door of the house
Leviticus14:39 And the priest shall come again on the seventh day
Leviticus14:40 then the priest shall command that they take out the stones in which is the disease and throw them into an unclean place outside the city;
Leviticus14:41 and he shall cause the inside of the house to be scraped round about
Leviticus14:42 then they shall take other stones and put them in the place of those stones
Leviticus14:43 “If the disease breaks out again in the house
Leviticus14:44 then the priest shall go and look; and if the disease has spread in the house
Leviticus14:45 And he shall break down the house
Leviticus14:46 Moreover he who enters the house while it is shut up shall be unclean until the evening;
Leviticus14:47 and he who lies down in the house shall wash his clothes; and he who eats in the house shall wash his clothes.
Leviticus14:48 “But if the priest comes and makes an examination
Leviticus14:49 And for the cleansing of the house he shall take two small birds
Leviticus14:50 and shall kill one of the birds in an earthen vessel over running water
Leviticus14:51 and shall take the cedarwood and the hyssop and the scarlet stuff
Leviticus14:52 Thus he shall cleanse the house with the blood of the bird
Leviticus14:53 and he shall let the living bird go out of the city into the open field; so he shall make atonement for the house
Leviticus14:54 This is the law for any leprous disease: for an itch
Leviticus14:55 for leprosy in a garment or in a house
Leviticus14:56 and for a swelling or an eruption or a spot
Leviticus14:57 to show when it is unclean and when it is clean. This is the law for leprosy.
Leviticus15:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus15:2 “Say to the people of Israel
Leviticus15:3 And this is the law of his uncleanness for a discharge: whether his body runs with his discharge
Leviticus15:4 Every bed on which he who has the discharge lies shall be unclean; and everything on which he sits shall be unclean.
Leviticus15:5 And any one who touches his bed shall wash his clothes
Leviticus15:6 And whoever sits on anything on which he who has the discharge has sat shall wash his clothes
Leviticus15:7 And whoever touches the body of him who has the discharge shall wash his clothes
Leviticus15:8 And if he who has the discharge spits on one who is clean
Leviticus15:9 And any saddle on which he who has the discharge rides shall be unclean.
Leviticus15:10 And whoever touches anything that was under him shall be unclean until the evening; and he who carries such a thing shall wash his clothes
Leviticus15:11 Any one whom he that has the discharge touches without having rinsed his hands in water shall wash his clothes
Leviticus15:12 And the earthen vessel which he who has the discharge touches shall be broken; and every vessel of wood shall be rinsed in water.
Leviticus15:13 “And when he who has a discharge is cleansed of his discharge
Leviticus15:14 And on the eighth day he shall take two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus15:15 and the priest shall offer them
Leviticus15:16 “And if a man has an emission of semen
Leviticus15:17 And every garment and every skin on which the semen comes shall be washed with water
Leviticus15:18 If a man lies with a woman and has an emission of semen
Leviticus15:19 “When a woman has a discharge of blood which is her regular discharge from her body
Leviticus15:20 And everything upon which she lies during her impurity shall be unclean; everything also upon which she sits shall be unclean.
Leviticus15:21 And whoever touches her bed shall wash his clothes
Leviticus15:22 And whoever touches anything upon which she sits shall wash his clothes
Leviticus15:23 whether it is the bed or anything upon which she sits
Leviticus15:24 And if any man lies with her
Leviticus15:25 “If a woman has a discharge of blood for many days
Leviticus15:26 Every bed on which she lies
Leviticus15:27 And whoever touches these things shall be unclean
Leviticus15:28 But if she is cleansed of her discharge
Leviticus15:29 And on the eighth day she shall take two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus15:30 And the priest shall offer one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering; and the priest shall make atonement for her before the Lord for her unclean discharge.
Leviticus15:31 “Thus you shall keep the people of Israel separate from their uncleanness
Leviticus15:32 This is the law for him who has a discharge and for him who has an emission of semen
Leviticus15:33 also for her who is sick with her impurity; that is

Leviticus16:2 and the Lord said to Moses

Leviticus16:4 He shall put on the holy linen coat
Leviticus16:5 And he shall take from the congregation of the people of Israel two male goats for a sin offering
Leviticus16:6 “And Aaron shall offer the bull as a sin offering for himself
Leviticus16:7 Then he shall take the two goats
Leviticus16:8 and Aaron shall cast lots upon the two goats
Leviticus16:9 And Aaron shall present the goat on which the lot fell for the Lord
Leviticus16:10 but the goat on which the lot fell for Azazel shall be presented alive before the Lord to make atonement over it
Leviticus16:11 “Aaron shall present the bull as a sin offering for himself
Leviticus16:12 And he shall take a censer full of coals of fire from the altar before the Lord
Leviticus16:13 and put the incense on the fire before the Lord
Leviticus16:14 and he shall take some of the blood of the bull
Leviticus16:15 “Then he shall kill the goat of the sin offering which is for the people
Leviticus16:16 thus he shall make atonement for the holy place
Leviticus16:17 There shall be no man in the tent of meeting when he enters to make atonement in the holy place until he comes out and has made atonement for himself and for his house and for all the assembly of Israel.
Leviticus16:18 Then he shall go out to the altar which is before the Lord and make atonement for it
Leviticus16:19 And he shall sprinkle some of the blood upon it with his finger seven times
Leviticus16:20 “And when he has made an end of atoning for the holy place and the tent of meeting and the altar
Leviticus16:21 and Aaron shall lay both his hands upon the head of the live goat
Leviticus16:22 The goat shall bear all their iniquities upon him to a solitary land; and he shall let the goat go in the wilderness.
Leviticus16:23 “Then Aaron shall come into the tent of meeting
Leviticus16:24 and he shall bathe his body in water in a holy place
Leviticus16:25 And the fat of the sin offering he shall burn upon the altar.

Leviticus16:30 for on this day shall atonement be made for you
Leviticus16:31 It is a sabbath of solemn rest to you
Leviticus16:32 And the priest who is anointed and consecrated as priest in his father's place shall make atonement
Leviticus16:33 he shall make atonement for the sanctuary
Leviticus16:34 And this shall be an everlasting statute for you

Leviticus17:4 and does not bring it to the door of the tent of meeting
Leviticus17:5 This is to the end that the people of Israel may bring their sacrifices which they slay in the open field

Leviticus17:12 Therefore I have said to the people of Israel

Leviticus18:1 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus18:2 “Say to the people of Israel
Leviticus18:3 You shall not do as they do in the land of Egypt
Leviticus18:4 You shall do my ordinances and keep my statutes and walk in them. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus18:5 You shall therefore keep my statutes and my ordinances
Leviticus18:6 “None of you shall approach any one near of kin to him to uncover nakedness. I am the Lord.
Leviticus18:7 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father
Leviticus18:8 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's wife; it is your father's nakedness.
Leviticus18:9 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your sister
Leviticus18:10 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your son's daughter or of your daughter's daughter
Leviticus18:11 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's wife's daughter
Leviticus18:12 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's sister; she is your father's near kinswoman.
Leviticus18:13 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother's sister
Leviticus18:14 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's brother
Leviticus18:15 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your daughter-in-law; she is your son's wife
Leviticus18:16 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your brother's wife; she is your brother's nakedness.
Leviticus18:17 You shall not uncover the nakedness of a woman and of her daughter
Leviticus18:18 And you shall not take a woman as a rival wife to her sister
Leviticus18:19 “You shall not approach a woman to uncover her nakedness while she is in her menstrual uncleanness.
Leviticus18:20 And you shall not lie carnally with your neighbor's wife
Leviticus18:21 You shall not give any of your children to devote them by fire to Molech
Leviticus18:22 You shall not lie with a male as with a woman; it is an abomination.
Leviticus18:23 And you shall not lie with any beast and defile yourself with it
Leviticus18:24 “Do not defile yourselves by any of these things
Leviticus18:25 and the land became defiled
Leviticus18:26 But you shall keep my statutes and my ordinances and do none of these abominations
Leviticus18:27 (for all of these abominations the men of the land did
Leviticus18:28 lest the land vomit you out
Leviticus18:29 For whoever shall do any of these abominations
Leviticus18:30 So keep my charge never to practice any of these abominable customs which were practiced before you
Leviticus19:1 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus19:2 “Say to all the congregation of the people of Israel
Leviticus19:3 Every one of you shall revere his mother and his father
Leviticus19:4 Do not turn to idols or make for yourselves molten gods: I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:5 “When you offer a sacrifice of peace offerings to the Lord
Leviticus19:6 It shall be eaten the same day you offer it
Leviticus19:7 If it is eaten at all on the third day
Leviticus19:8 and every one who eats it shall bear his iniquity
Leviticus19:9 “When you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus19:10 And you shall not strip your vineyard bare
Leviticus19:11 “You shall not steal
Leviticus19:12 And you shall not swear by my name falsely
Leviticus19:13 “You shall not oppress your neighbor or rob him. The wages of a hired servant shall not remain with you all night until the morning.
Leviticus19:14 You shall not curse the deaf or put a stumbling block before the blind
Leviticus19:15 “You shall do no injustice in judgment; you shall not be partial to the poor or defer to the great
Leviticus19:16 You shall not go up and down as a slanderer among your people
Leviticus19:17 “You shall not hate your brother in your heart
Leviticus19:18 You shall not take vengeance or bear any grudge against the sons of your own people
Leviticus19:19 “You shall keep my statutes. You shall not let your cattle breed with a different kind; you shall not sow your field with two kinds of seed; nor shall there come upon you a garment of cloth made of two kinds of stuff.
Leviticus19:20 “If a man lies carnally with a woman who is a slave
Leviticus19:21 but he shall bring a guilt offering for himself to the Lord
Leviticus19:22 And the priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of the guilt offering before the Lord for his sin which he has committed; and the sin which he has committed shall be forgiven him.
Leviticus19:23 “When you come into the land and plant all kinds of trees for food
Leviticus19:24 And in the fourth year all their fruit shall be holy
Leviticus19:25 But in the fifth year you may eat of their fruit
Leviticus19:26 “You shall not eat any flesh with the blood in it. You shall not practice augury or witchcraft.
Leviticus19:27 You shall not round off the hair on you temples or mar the edges of your beard.
Leviticus19:28 You shall not make any cuttings in your flesh on account of the dead or tattoo any marks upon you: I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:29 “Do not profane your daughter by making her a harlot
Leviticus19:30 You shall keep my sabbaths and reverence my sanctuary: I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:31 “Do not turn to mediums or wizards; do not seek them out
Leviticus19:32 “You shall rise up before the hoary head
Leviticus19:33 “When a stranger sojourns with you in your land
Leviticus19:34 The stranger who sojourns with you shall be to you as the native among you
Leviticus19:35 “You shall do no wrong in judgment
Leviticus19:36 You shall have just balances
Leviticus19:37 And you shall observe all my statutes and all my ordinances

Leviticus20:7 Consecrate yourselves therefore
Leviticus20:8 Keep my statutes

Leviticus20:12 If a man lies with his daughter-in-law
Leviticus20:13 If a man lies with a male as with a woman

Leviticus20:21 If a man takes his brother's wife

Leviticus20:24 But I have said to you
Leviticus20:25 You shall therefore make a distinction between the clean beast and the unclean
Leviticus20:26 You shall be holy to me; for I the Lord am holy
Leviticus20:27 “A man or a woman who is a medium or a wizard shall be put to death; they shall be stoned with stones
