Leviticus22:1 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus22:2 “Tell Aaron and his sons to keep away from the holy things of the people of Israel
Leviticus22:3 Say to them
Leviticus22:4 None of the line of Aaron who is a leper or suffers a discharge may eat of the holy things until he is clean. Whoever touches anything that is unclean through contact with the dead or a man who has had an emission of semen
Leviticus22:5 and whoever touches a creeping thing by which he may be made unclean or a man from whom he may take uncleanness
Leviticus22:6 the person who touches any such shall be unclean until the evening and shall not eat of the holy things unless he has bathed his body in water.
Leviticus22:7 When the sun is down he shall be clean; and afterward he may eat of the holy things
Leviticus22:8 That which dies of itself or is torn by beasts he shall not eat
Leviticus22:9 They shall therefore keep my charge
Leviticus22:10 “An outsider shall not eat of a holy thing. A sojourner of the priest's or a hired servant shall not eat of a holy thing;
Leviticus22:11 but if a priest buys a slave as his property for money
Leviticus22:12 If a priest's daughter is married to an outsider she shall not eat of the offering of the holy things.
Leviticus22:13 But if a priest's daughter is a widow or divorced
Leviticus22:14 And if a man eats of a holy thing unwittingly
Leviticus22:15 The priests shall not profane the holy things of the people of Israel
Leviticus22:16 and so cause them to bear iniquity and guilt
Leviticus22:17 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus22:18 “Say to Aaron and his sons and all the people of Israel
Leviticus22:19 to be accepted you shall offer a male without blemish
Leviticus22:20 You shall not offer anything that has a blemish
Leviticus22:21 And when any one offers a sacrifice of peace offerings to the Lord
Leviticus22:22 Animals blind or disabled or mutilated or having a discharge or an itch or scabs
Leviticus22:23 A bull or a lamb which has a part too long or too short you may present for a freewill offering; but for a votive offering it cannot be accepted.
Leviticus22:24 Any animal which has its testicles bruised or crushed or torn or cut
Leviticus22:25 neither shall you offer as the bread of your God any such animals gotten from a foreigner. Since there is a blemish in them
Leviticus22:26 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus22:27 “When a bull or sheep or goat is born
Leviticus22:28 And whether the mother is a cow or a ewe
Leviticus22:29 And when you sacrifice a sacrifice of thanksgiving to the Lord
Leviticus22:30 It shall be eaten on the same day
Leviticus22:31 “So you shall keep my commandments and do them: I am the Lord.
Leviticus22:32 And you shall not profane my holy name
Leviticus22:33 who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God: I am the Lord.”
Leviticus23:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:2 “Say to the people of Israel
Leviticus23:3 Six days shall work be done; but on the seventh day is a sabbath of solemn rest
Leviticus23:4 “These are the appointed feasts of the Lord
Leviticus23:5 In the first month
Leviticus23:6 And on the fifteenth day of the same month is the feast of unleavened bread to the Lord; seven days you shall eat unleavened bread.
Leviticus23:7 On the first day you shall have a holy convocation; you shall do no laborious work.
Leviticus23:8 But you shall present an offering by fire to the Lord seven days; on the seventh day is a holy convocation; you shall do no laborious work.”
Leviticus23:9 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:10 “Say to the people of Israel
Leviticus23:11 and he shall wave the sheaf before the Lord
Leviticus23:12 And on the day when you wave the sheaf
Leviticus23:13 And the cereal offering with it shall be two tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil
Leviticus23:14 And you shall eat neither bread nor grain parched or fresh until this same day
Leviticus23:15 “And you shall count from the morrow after the sabbath
Leviticus23:16 counting fifty days to the morrow after the seventh sabbath; then you shall present a cereal offering of new grain to the Lord.
Leviticus23:17 You shall bring from your dwellings two loaves of bread to be waved
Leviticus23:18 And you shall present with the bread seven lambs a year old without blemish
Leviticus23:19 And you shall offer one male goat for a sin offering
Leviticus23:20 And the priest shall wave them with the bread of the first fruits as a wave offering before the Lord
Leviticus23:21 And you shall make proclamation on the same day; you shall hold a holy convocation; you shall do no laborious work: it is a statute for ever in all your dwellings throughout your generations.
Leviticus23:22 “And when you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus23:23 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:24 “Say to the people of Israel
Leviticus23:25 You shall do no laborious work; and you shall present an offering by fire to the Lord.”
Leviticus23:26 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:27 “On the tenth day of this seventh month is the day of atonement; it shall be for you a time of holy convocation
Leviticus23:28 And you shall do no work on this same day; for it is a day of atonement
Leviticus23:29 For whoever is not afflicted on this same day shall be cut off from his people.
Leviticus23:30 And whoever does any work on this same day
Leviticus23:31 You shall do no work: it is a statute for ever throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
Leviticus23:32 It shall be to you a sabbath of solemn rest
Leviticus23:33 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:34 “Say to the people of Israel
Leviticus23:35 On the first day shall be a holy convocation; you shall do no laborious work.
Leviticus23:36 Seven days you shall present offerings by fire to the Lord; on the eighth day you shall hold a holy convocation and present an offering by fire to the Lord; it is a solemn assembly; you shall do no laborious work.
Leviticus23:37 “These are the appointed feasts of the Lord
Leviticus23:38 besides the sabbaths of the Lord
Leviticus23:39 “On the fifteenth day of the seventh month
Leviticus23:40 And you shall take on the first day the fruit of goodly trees
Leviticus23:41 You shall keep it as a feast to the Lord seven days in the year; it is a statute for ever throughout your generations; you shall keep it in the seventh month.
Leviticus23:42 You shall dwell in booths for seven days; all that are native in Israel shall dwell in booths
Leviticus23:43 that your generations may know that I made the people of Israel dwell in booths when I brought them out of the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.”
Leviticus23:44 Thus Moses declared to the people of Israel the appointed feasts of the Lord.
Leviticus24:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus24:2 “Command the people of Israel to bring you pure oil from beaten olives for the lamp
Leviticus24:3 Outside the veil of the testimony
Leviticus24:4 He shall keep the lamps in order upon the lampstand of pure gold before the Lord continually.
Leviticus24:5 “And you shall take fine flour
Leviticus24:6 And you shall set them in two rows
Leviticus24:7 And you shall put pure frankincense with each row
Leviticus24:8 Every sabbath day Aaron shall set it in order before the Lord continually on behalf of the people of Israel as a covenant for ever.
Leviticus24:9 And it shall be for Aaron and his sons
Leviticus24:10 Now an Israelite woman's son
Leviticus24:11 and the Israelite woman's son blasphemed the Name
Leviticus24:12 And they put him in custody
Leviticus24:13 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus24:14 “Bring out of the camp him who cursed; and let all who heard him lay their hands upon his head
Leviticus24:15 And say to the people of Israel
Leviticus24:16 He who blasphemes the name of the Lord shall be put to death; all the congregation shall stone him; the sojourner as well as the native
Leviticus24:17 He who kills a man shall be put to death.
Leviticus24:18 He who kills a beast shall make it good
Leviticus24:19 When a man causes a disfigurement in his neighbor
Leviticus24:20 fracture for fracture
Leviticus24:21 He who kills a beast shall make it good; and he who kills a man shall be put to death.
Leviticus24:22 You shall have one law for the sojourner and for the native; for I am the Lord your God.”
Leviticus24:23 So Moses spoke to the people of Israel; and they brought him who had cursed out of the camp
Leviticus25:1 The Lord said to Moses on Mount Sinai
Leviticus25:2 “Say to the people of Israel
Leviticus25:3 Six years you shall sow your field
Leviticus25:4 but in the seventh year there shall be a sabbath of solemn rest for the land
Leviticus25:5 What grows of itself in your harvest you shall not reap
Leviticus25:6 The sabbath of the land shall provide food for you
Leviticus25:7 for your cattle also and for the beasts that are in your land all its yield shall be for food.
Leviticus25:8 “And you shall count seven weeks of years
Leviticus25:9 Then you shall send abroad the loud trumpet on the tenth day of the seventh month; on the day of atonement you shall send abroad the trumpet throughout all your land.
Leviticus25:10 And you shall hallow the fiftieth year
Leviticus25:11 A jubilee shall that fiftieth year be to you; in it you shall neither sow
Leviticus25:12 For it is a jubilee; it shall be holy to you; you shall eat what it yields out of the field.
Leviticus25:13 “In this year of jubilee each of you shall return to his property.
Leviticus25:14 And if you sell to your neighbor or buy from your neighbor
Leviticus25:15 According to the number of years after the jubilee
Leviticus25:16 If the years are many you shall increase the price
Leviticus25:17 You shall not wrong one another
Leviticus25:18 “Therefore you shall do my statutes
Leviticus25:19 The land will yield its fruit
Leviticus25:20 And if you say
Leviticus25:21 I will command my blessing upon you in the sixth year
Leviticus25:22 When you sow in the eighth year
Leviticus25:23 The land shall not be sold in perpetuity
Leviticus25:24 And in all the country you possess
Leviticus25:25 “If your brother becomes poor
Leviticus25:26 If a man has no one to redeem it
Leviticus25:27 let him reckon the years since he sold it and pay back the overpayment to the man to whom he sold it; and he shall return to his property.
Leviticus25:28 But if he has not sufficient means to get it back for himself
Leviticus25:29 “If a man sells a dwelling house in a walled city
Leviticus25:30 If it is not redeemed within a full year
Leviticus25:31 But the houses of the villages which have no wall around them shall be reckoned with the fields of the country; they may be redeemed
Leviticus25:32 Nevertheless the cities of the Levites
Leviticus25:33 And if one of the Levites does not exercise his right of redemption
Leviticus25:34 But the fields of common land belonging to their cities may not be sold; for that is their perpetual possession.
Leviticus25:35 “And if your brother becomes poor
Leviticus25:36 Take no interest from him or increase
Leviticus25:37 You shall not lend him your money at interest
Leviticus25:38 I am the Lord your God
Leviticus25:39 “And if your brother becomes poor beside you
Leviticus25:40 he shall be with you as a hired servant and as a sojourner. He shall serve with you until the year of the jubilee;
Leviticus25:41 then he shall go out from you
Leviticus25:42 For they are my servants
Leviticus25:43 You shall not rule over him with harshness
Leviticus25:44 As for your male and female slaves whom you may have: you may buy male and female slaves from among the nations that are round about you.
Leviticus25:45 You may also buy from among the strangers who sojourn with you and their families that are with you
Leviticus25:46 You may bequeath them to your sons after you
Leviticus25:47 “If a stranger or sojourner with you becomes rich
Leviticus25:48 then after he is sold he may be redeemed; one of his brothers may redeem him
Leviticus25:49 or his uncle
Leviticus25:50 He shall reckon with him who bought him from the year when he sold himself to him until the year of jubilee
Leviticus25:51 If there are still many years
Leviticus25:52 If there remain but a few years until the year of jubilee
Leviticus25:53 As a servant hired year by year shall he be with him; he shall not rule with harshness over him in your sight.
Leviticus25:54 And if he is not redeemed by these means
Leviticus25:55 For to me the people of Israel are servants
Leviticus26:1 “You shall make for yourselves no idols and erect no graven image or pillar
Leviticus26:2 You shall keep my sabbaths and reverence my sanctuary: I am the Lord.
Leviticus26:3 “If you walk in my statutes and observe my commandments and do them
Leviticus26:4 then I will give you your rains in their season
Leviticus26:5 And your threshing shall last to the time of vintage
Leviticus26:6 And I will give peace in the land
Leviticus26:7 And you shall chase your enemies
Leviticus26:8 Five of you shall chase a hundred
Leviticus26:9 And I will have regard for you and make you fruitful and multiply you
Leviticus26:10 And you shall eat old store long kept
Leviticus26:11 And I will make my abode among you
Leviticus26:12 And I will walk among you
Leviticus26:13 I am the Lord your God
Leviticus26:14 “But if you will not hearken to me
Leviticus26:15 if you spurn my statutes
Leviticus26:16 I will do this to you: I will appoint over you sudden terror
Leviticus26:17 I will set my face against you
Leviticus26:18 And if in spite of this you will not hearken to me
Leviticus26:19 and I will break the pride of your power
Leviticus26:20 and your strength shall be spent in vain
Leviticus26:21 “Then if you walk contrary to me
Leviticus26:22 And I will let loose the wild beasts among you
Leviticus26:23 “And if by this discipline you are not turned to me
Leviticus26:24 then I also will walk contrary to you
Leviticus26:25 And I will bring a sword upon you
Leviticus26:26 When I break your staff of bread
Leviticus26:27 “And if in spite of this you will not hearken to me
Leviticus26:28 then I will walk contrary to you in fury
Leviticus26:29 You shall eat the flesh of your sons
Leviticus26:30 And I will destroy your high places
Leviticus26:31 And I will lay your cities waste
Leviticus26:32 And I will devastate the land
Leviticus26:33 And I will scatter you among the nations
Leviticus26:34 “Then the land shall enjoy its sabbaths as long as it lies desolate
Leviticus26:35 As long as it lies desolate it shall have rest
Leviticus26:36 And as for those of you that are left
Leviticus26:37 They shall stumble over one another
Leviticus26:38 And you shall perish among the nations
Leviticus26:39 And those of you that are left shall pine away in your enemies' lands because of their iniquity; and also because of the iniquities of their fathers they shall pine away like them.
Leviticus26:40 “But if they confess their iniquity and the iniquity of their fathers in their treachery which they committed against me
Leviticus26:41 so that I walked contrary to them and brought them into the land of their enemies; if then their uncircumcised heart is humbled and they make amends for their iniquity;
Leviticus26:42 then I will remember my covenant with Jacob
Leviticus26:43 But the land shall be left by them
Leviticus26:44 Yet for all that
Leviticus26:45 but I will for their sake remember the covenant with their forefathers
Leviticus26:46 These are the statutes and ordinances and laws which the Lord made between him and the people of Israel on Mount Sinai by Moses.
Leviticus27:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus27:2 “Say to the people of Israel
Leviticus27:3 then your valuation of a male from twenty years old up to sixty years old shall be fifty shekels of silver
Leviticus27:4 If the person is a female
Leviticus27:5 If the person is from five years old up to twenty years old
Leviticus27:6 If the person is from a month old up to five years old
Leviticus27:7 And if the person is sixty years old and upward
Leviticus27:8 And if a man is too poor to pay your valuation
Leviticus27:9 “If it is an animal such as men offer as an offering to the Lord
Leviticus27:10 He shall not substitute anything for it or exchange it
Leviticus27:11 And if it is an unclean animal such as is not offered as an offering to the Lord
Leviticus27:12 and the priest shall value it as either good or bad; as you
Leviticus27:13 But if he wishes to redeem it
Leviticus27:14 “When a man dedicates his house to be holy to the Lord
Leviticus27:15 And if he who dedicates it wishes to redeem his house
Leviticus27:16 “If a man dedicates to the Lord part of the land which is his by inheritance
Leviticus27:17 If he dedicates his field from the year of jubilee
Leviticus27:18 but if he dedicates his field after the jubilee
Leviticus27:19 And if he who dedicates the field wishes to redeem it
Leviticus27:20 But if he does not wish to redeem the field
Leviticus27:21 but the field
Leviticus27:22 If he dedicates to the Lord a field which he has bought
Leviticus27:23 then the priest shall compute the valuation for it up to the year of jubilee
Leviticus27:24 In the year of jubilee the field shall return to him from whom it was bought
Leviticus27:25 Every valuation shall be according to the shekel of the sanctuary: twenty gerahs shall make a shekel.
Leviticus27:26 “But a firstling of animals
Leviticus27:27 And if it is an unclean animal
Leviticus27:28 “But no devoted thing that a man devotes to the Lord
Leviticus27:29 No one devoted
Leviticus27:30 “All the tithe of the land
Leviticus27:31 If a man wishes to redeem any of his tithe
Leviticus27:32 And all the tithe of herds and flocks
Leviticus27:33 A man shall not inquire whether it is good or bad
Leviticus27:34 These are the commandments which the Lord commanded Moses for the people of Israel on Mount Sinai.
Numbers1:1 The Lord spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai
Numbers1:2 “Take a census of all the congregation of the people of Israel
Numbers1:3 from twenty years old and upward
Numbers1:4 And there shall be with you a man from each tribe
Numbers1:5 And these are the names of the men who shall attend you. From Reuben
Numbers1:6 from Simeon
Numbers1:7 from Judah
Numbers1:8 from Issachar
Numbers1:9 from Zebulun
Numbers1:10 from the sons of Joseph
Numbers1:11 from Benjamin
Numbers1:12 from Dan
Numbers1:13 from Asher
Numbers1:14 from Gad
Numbers1:15 from Naphtali
Numbers1:16 These were the ones chosen from the congregation
Numbers1:17 Moses and Aaron took these men who have been named
Numbers1:18 and on the first day of the second month
Numbers1:19 as the Lord commanded Moses. So he numbered them in the wilderness of Sinai.
Numbers1:20 The people of Reuben
Numbers1:21 the number of the tribe of Reuben was forty-six thousand five hundred.
Numbers1:22 Of the people of Simeon
Numbers1:23 the number of the tribe of Simeon was fifty-nine thousand three hundred.
Numbers1:24 Of the people of Gad
Numbers1:25 the number of the tribe of Gad was forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty.
Numbers1:26 Of the people of Judah
Numbers1:27 the number of the tribe of Judah was seventy-four thousand six hundred.
Numbers1:28 Of the people of Issachar
Numbers1:29 the number of the tribe of Issachar was fifty-four thousand four hundred.
Numbers1:30 Of the people of Zebulun
Numbers1:31 the number of the tribe of Zebulun was fifty-seven thousand four hundred.
Numbers1:32 Of the people of Joseph
Numbers1:33 the number of the tribe of Ephraim was forty thousand five hundred.
Numbers1:34 Of the people of Manasseh
Numbers1:35 the number of the tribe of Manasseh was thirty-two thousand two hundred.
Numbers1:36 Of the people of Benjamin
Numbers1:37 the number of the tribe of Benjamin was thirty-five thousand four hundred.
Numbers1:38 Of the people of Dan
Numbers1:39 the number of the tribe of Dan was sixty-two thousand seven hundred.
Numbers1:40 Of the people of Asher
Numbers1:41 the number of the tribe of Asher was forty-one thousand five hundred.
Numbers1:42 Of the people of Naphtali
Numbers1:43 the number of the tribe of Naphtali was fifty-three thousand four hundred.
Numbers1:44 These are those who were numbered
Numbers1:45 So the whole number of the people of Israel
Numbers1:46 their whole number was six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty.
Numbers1:47 But the Levites were not numbered by their ancestral tribe along with them.
Numbers1:48 For the Lord said to Moses
Numbers1:49 “Only the tribe of Levi you shall not number
Numbers1:50 but appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the testimony
Numbers1:51 When the tabernacle is to set out
Numbers1:52 The people of Israel shall pitch their tents by their companies
Numbers1:53 but the Levites shall encamp around the tabernacle of the testimony
Numbers1:54 Thus did the people of Israel; they did according to all that the Lord commanded Moses.
Numbers2:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers2:2 “The people of Israel shall encamp each by his own standard
Numbers2:3 Those to encamp on the east side toward the sunrise shall be of the standard of the camp of Judah by their companies
Numbers2:4 his host as numbered being seventy-four thousand six hundred.
Numbers2:5 Those to encamp next to him shall be the tribe of Issachar
Numbers2:6 his host as numbered being fifty-four thousand four hundred.
Numbers2:7 Then the tribe of Zebulun
Numbers2:8 his host as numbered being fifty-seven thousand four hundred.
Numbers2:9 The whole number of the camp of Judah
Numbers2:10 “On the south side shall be the standard of the camp of Reuben by their companies
Numbers2:11 his host as numbered being forty-six thousand five hundred.
Numbers2:12 And those to encamp next to him shall be the tribe of Simeon
Numbers2:13 his host as numbered being fifty-nine thousand three hundred.
Numbers2:14 Then the tribe of Gad
Numbers2:15 his host as numbered being forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty.
Numbers2:16 The whole number of the camp of Reuben
Numbers2:17 “Then the tent of meeting shall set out
Numbers2:18 “On the west side shall be the standard of the camp of Ephraim by their companies
Numbers2:19 his host as numbered being forty thousand five hundred.
Numbers2:20 And next to him shall be the tribe of Manasseh
Numbers2:21 his host as numbered being thirty-two thousand two hundred.
Numbers2:22 Then the tribe of Benjamin
Numbers2:23 his host as numbered being thirty-five thousand four hundred.
Numbers2:24 The whole number of the camp of Ephraim
Numbers2:25 “On the north side shall be the standard of the camp of Dan by their companies
Numbers2:26 his host as numbered being sixty-two thousand seven hundred.
Numbers2:27 And those to encamp next to him shall be the tribe of Asher
Numbers2:28 his host as numbered being forty-one thousand five hundred.
Numbers2:29 Then the tribe of Naphtali
Numbers2:30 his host as numbered being fifty-three thousand four hundred.
Numbers2:31 The whole number of the camp of Dan is a hundred and fifty-seven thousand six hundred. They shall set out last
Numbers2:32 These are the people of Israel as numbered by their fathers' houses; all in the camps who were numbered by their companies were six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty.
Numbers2:33 But the Levites were not numbered among the people of Israel
Numbers2:34 Thus did the people of Israel. According to all that the Lord commanded Moses
Numbers3:1 These are the generations of Aaron and Moses at the time when the Lord spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.
Numbers3:2 These are the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab the first-born
Numbers3:3 these are the names of the sons of Aaron
Numbers3:4 But Nadab and Abihu died before the Lord when they offered unholy fire before the Lord in the wilderness of Sinai; and they had no children. So Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests in the lifetime of Aaron their father.
Numbers3:5 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers3:6 “Bring the tribe of Levi near
Numbers3:7 They shall perform duties for him and for the whole congregation before the tent of meeting
Numbers3:8 they shall have charge of all the furnishings of the tent of meeting
Numbers3:9 And you shall give the Levites to Aaron and his sons; they are wholly given to him from among the people of Israel.
Numbers3:10 And you shall appoint Aaron and his sons
Numbers3:11 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers3:12 “Behold
Numbers3:13 for all the first-born are mine; on the day that I slew all the first-born in the land of Egypt
Numbers3:14 And the Lord said to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai
Numbers3:15 “Number the sons of Levi
Numbers3:16 So Moses numbered them according to the word of the Lord
Numbers3:17 And these were the sons of Levi by their names: Gershon and Kohath and Merari.
Numbers3:18 And these are the names of the sons of Gershon by their families: Libni and Shime-i.
Numbers3:19 And the sons of Kohath by their families: Amram
Numbers3:20 And the sons of Merari by their families: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites
Numbers3:21 Of Gershon were the family of the Libnites and the family of the Shimeites; these were the families of the Gershonites.
Numbers3:22 Their number according to the number of all the males from a month old and upward was seven thousand five hundred.
Numbers3:23 The families of the Gershonites were to encamp behind the tabernacle on the west
Numbers3:24 with Eliasaph
Numbers3:25 And the charge of the sons of Gershon in the tent of meeting was to be the tabernacle
Numbers3:26 the hangings of the court
Numbers3:27 Of Kohath were the family of the Amramites
Numbers3:28 According to the number of all the males
Numbers3:29 The families of the sons of Kohath were to encamp on the south side of the tabernacle
Numbers3:30 with Eli-zaphan the son of Uzziel as head of the fathers' house of the families of the Kohathites.
Numbers3:31 And their charge was to be the ark
Numbers3:32 And Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest was to be chief over the leaders of the Levites
Numbers3:33 Of Merari were the family of the Mahlites and the family of the Mushites: these are the families of Merari.
Numbers3:34 Their number according to the number of all the males from a month old and upward was six thousand two hundred.
Numbers3:35 And the head of the fathers' house of the families of Merari was Zuriel the son of Abihail; they were to encamp on the north side of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:36 And the appointed charge of the sons of Merari was to be the frames of the tabernacle
Numbers3:37 also the pillars of the court round about
Numbers3:38 And those to encamp before the tabernacle on the east
Numbers3:39 All who were numbered of the Levites
Numbers3:40 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers3:41 And you shall take the Levites for me—I am the Lord —instead of all the first-born among the people of Israel
Numbers3:42 So Moses numbered all the first-born among the people of Israel
Numbers3:43 And all the first-born males
Numbers3:44 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers3:45 “Take the Levites instead of all the first-born among the people of Israel
Numbers3:46 And for the redemption of the two hundred and seventy-three of the first-born of the people of Israel
Numbers3:47 you shall take five shekels apiece; reckoning by the shekel of the sanctuary
Numbers3:48 and give the money by which the excess number of them is redeemed to Aaron and his sons.”
Numbers3:49 So Moses took the redemption money from those who were over and above those redeemed by the Levites;
Numbers3:50 from the first-born of the people of Israel he took the money
Numbers3:51 and Moses gave the redemption money to Aaron and his sons
Numbers4:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:2 “Take a census of the sons of Kohath from among the sons of Levi
Numbers4:3 from thirty years old up to fifty years old
Numbers4:4 This is the service of the sons of Kohath in the tent of meeting: the most holy things.
Numbers4:5 When the camp is to set out
Numbers4:6 then they shall put on it a covering of goatskin
Numbers4:7 And over the table of the bread of the Presence they shall spread a cloth of blue
Numbers4:8 then they shall spread over them a cloth of scarlet
Numbers4:9 And they shall take a cloth of blue
Numbers4:10 and they shall put it with all its utensils in a covering of goatskin and put it upon the carrying frame.
Numbers4:11 And over the golden altar they shall spread a cloth of blue
Numbers4:12 and they shall take all the vessels of the service which are used in the sanctuary
Numbers4:13 And they shall take away the ashes from the altar
Numbers4:14 and they shall put on it all the utensils of the altar
Numbers4:15 And when Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary and all the furnishings of the sanctuary
Numbers4:16 “And Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest shall have charge of the oil for the light
Numbers4:17 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:18 “Let not the tribe of the families of the Kohathites be destroyed from among the Levites;
Numbers4:19 but deal thus with them
Numbers4:20 but they shall not go in to look upon the holy things even for a moment
Numbers4:21 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers4:22 “Take a census of the sons of Gershon also
Numbers4:23 from thirty years old up to fifty years old
Numbers4:24 This is the service of the families of the Gershonites
Numbers4:25 they shall carry the curtains of the tabernacle
Numbers4:26 and the hangings of the court
Numbers4:27 All the service of the sons of the Gershonites shall be at the command of Aaron and his sons
Numbers4:28 This is the service of the families of the sons of the Gershonites in the tent of meeting
Numbers4:29 “As for the sons of Merari
Numbers4:30 from thirty years old up to fifty years old
Numbers4:31 And this is what they are charged to carry
Numbers4:32 and the pillars of the court round about with their bases
Numbers4:33 This is the service of the families of the sons of Merari
Numbers4:34 And Moses and Aaron and the leaders of the congregation numbered the sons of the Kohathites
Numbers4:35 from thirty years old up to fifty years old
Numbers4:36 and their number by families was two thousand seven hundred and fifty.
Numbers4:37 This was the number of the families of the Kohathites
Numbers4:38 The number of the sons of Gershon
Numbers4:39 from thirty years old up to fifty years old
Numbers4:40 their number by their families and their fathers' houses was two thousand six hundred and thirty.
Numbers4:41 This was the number of the families of the sons of Gershon
Numbers4:42 The number of the families of the sons of Merari
Numbers4:43 from thirty years old up to fifty years old
Numbers4:44 their number by families was three thousand two hundred.
Numbers4:45 These are those who were numbered of the families of the sons of Merari
Numbers4:46 All those who were numbered of the Levites
Numbers4:47 from thirty years old up to fifty years old
Numbers4:48 those who were numbered of them were eight thousand five hundred and eighty.
Numbers4:49 According to the commandment of the Lord through Moses they were appointed
Numbers5:1 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers5:2 “Command the people of Israel that they put out of the camp every leper
Numbers5:3 you shall put out both male and female
Numbers5:4 And the people of Israel did so
Numbers5:5 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers5:6 “Say to the people of Israel
Numbers5:7 he shall confess his sin which he has committed; and he shall make full restitution for his wrong
Numbers5:8 But if the man has no kinsman to whom restitution may be made for the wrong
Numbers5:9 And every offering
Numbers5:10 and every man's holy things shall be his; whatever any man gives to the priest shall be his.”
Numbers5:11 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers5:12 “Say to the people of Israel
Numbers5:13 if a man lies with her carnally
Numbers5:14 and if the spirit of jealousy comes upon him
Numbers5:15 then the man shall bring his wife to the priest
Numbers5:16 “And the priest shall bring her near
Numbers5:17 and the priest shall take holy water in an earthen vessel
Numbers5:18 And the priest shall set the woman before the Lord
Numbers5:19 Then the priest shall make her take an oath
Numbers5:20 But if you have gone astray
Numbers5:21 then’ (let the priest make the woman take the oath of the curse
Numbers5:22 may this water that brings the curse pass into your bowels and make your body swell and your thigh fall away.’ And the woman shall say
Numbers5:23 “Then the priest shall write these curses in a book
Numbers5:24 and he shall make the woman drink the water of bitterness that brings the curse
Numbers5:25 And the priest shall take the cereal offering of jealousy out of the woman's hand
Numbers5:26 and the priest shall take a handful of the cereal offering
Numbers5:27 And when he has made her drink the water
Numbers5:28 But if the woman has not defiled herself and is clean
Numbers5:29 “This is the law in cases of jealousy
Numbers5:30 or when the spirit of jealousy comes upon a man and he is jealous of his wife; then he shall set the woman before the Lord
Numbers5:31 The man shall be free from iniquity
Numbers6:1 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers6:2 “Say to the people of Israel
Numbers6:3 he shall separate himself from wine and strong drink; he shall drink no vinegar made from wine or strong drink
Numbers6:4 All the days of his separation he shall eat nothing that is produced by the grapevine
Numbers6:5 “All the days of his vow of separation no razor shall come upon his head; until the time is completed for which he separates himself to the Lord
Numbers6:6 “All the days that he separates himself to the Lord he shall not go near a dead body.
Numbers6:7 Neither for his father nor for his mother
Numbers6:8 All the days of his separation he is holy to the Lord.
Numbers6:9 “And if any man dies very suddenly beside him
Numbers6:10 On the eighth day he shall bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest to the door of the tent of meeting
Numbers6:11 and the priest shall offer one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering
Numbers6:12 and separate himself to the Lord for the days of his separation
Numbers6:13 “And this is the law for the Nazirite
Numbers6:14 and he shall offer his gift to the Lord
Numbers6:15 and a basket of unleavened bread
Numbers6:16 And the priest shall present them before the Lord and offer his sin offering and his burnt offering
Numbers6:17 and he shall offer the ram as a sacrifice of peace offering to the Lord
Numbers6:18 And the Nazirite shall shave his consecrated head at the door of the tent of meeting
Numbers6:19 And the priest shall take the shoulder of the ram
Numbers6:20 and the priest shall wave them for a wave offering before the Lord; they are a holy portion for the priest
Numbers6:21 “This is the law for the Nazirite who takes a vow. His offering to the Lord shall be according to his vow as a Nazirite
Numbers6:22 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers6:23 “Say to Aaron and his sons
Numbers6:24 “The Lord bless you and keep you:
Numbers6:25 The Lord make his face to shine upon you
Numbers6:26 The Lord lift up his countenance upon you
Numbers6:27 “So shall they put my name upon the people of Israel
Numbers7:1 On the day when Moses had finished setting up the tabernacle
Numbers7:2 the leaders of Israel
Numbers7:3 offered and brought their offerings before the Lord
Numbers7:4 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers7:5 “Accept these from them
Numbers7:6 So Moses took the wagons and the oxen
Numbers7:7 Two wagons and four oxen he gave to the sons of Gershon
Numbers7:8 and four wagons and eight oxen he gave to the sons of Merari
Numbers7:9 But to the sons of Kohath he gave none
Numbers7:10 And the leaders offered offerings for the dedication of the altar on the day it was anointed; and the leaders offered their offering before the altar.
Numbers7:11 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers7:12 He who offered his offering the first day was Nahshon the son of Amminadab
Numbers7:13 and his offering was one silver plate whose weight was a hundred and thirty shekels
Numbers7:14 one golden dish of ten shekels
Numbers7:15 one young bull
Numbers7:16 one male goat for a sin offering;
Numbers7:17 and for the sacrifice of peace offerings
Numbers7:18 On the second day Nethanel the son of Zuar

Numbers8:1 Now the Lord said to Moses
Numbers8:2 “Say to Aaron
Numbers8:3 And Aaron did so; he set up its lamps to give light in front of the lampstand
Numbers8:4 And this was the workmanship of the lampstand
Numbers8:5 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers8:6 “Take the Levites from among the people of Israel
Numbers8:7 And thus you shall do to them
Numbers8:8 Then let them take a young bull and its cereal offering of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers8:9 And you shall present the Levites before the tent of meeting
Numbers8:10 When you present the Levites before the Lord
Numbers8:11 and Aaron shall offer the Levites before the Lord as a wave offering from the people of Israel
Numbers8:12 Then the Levites shall lay their hands upon the heads of the bulls; and you shall offer the one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering to the Lord
Numbers8:13 And you shall cause the Levites to attend Aaron and his sons
Numbers8:14 “Thus you shall separate the Levites from among the people of Israel
Numbers8:15 And after that the Levites shall go in to do service at the tent of meeting
Numbers8:16 For they are wholly given to me from among the people of Israel; instead of all that open the womb
Numbers8:17 For all the first-born among the people of Israel are mine
Numbers8:18 and I have taken the Levites instead of all the first-born among the people of Israel.
Numbers8:19 And I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and his sons from among the people of Israel

Numbers8:21 And the Levites purified themselves from sin

Numbers8:23 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers8:24 “This is what pertains to the Levites: from twenty-five years old and upward they shall go in to perform the work in the service of the tent of meeting;

Numbers8:26 but minister to their brethren in the tent of meeting

Numbers19:1 Now the Lord said to Moses and to Aaron
Numbers19:2 “This is the statute of the law which the Lord has commanded: Tell the people of Israel to bring you a red heifer without defect
Numbers19:3 And you shall give her to Eleazar the priest
Numbers19:4 and Eleazar the priest shall take some of her blood with his finger
Numbers19:5 And the heifer shall be burned in his sight; her skin
Numbers19:6 and the priest shall take cedarwood and hyssop and scarlet stuff
Numbers19:7 Then the priest shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water
Numbers19:8 He who burns the heifer shall wash his clothes in water and bathe his body in water
Numbers19:9 And a man who is clean shall gather up the ashes of the heifer
Numbers19:10 And he who gathers the ashes of the heifer shall wash his clothes
Numbers19:11 “He who touches the dead body of any person shall be unclean seven days;
Numbers19:12 he shall cleanse himself with the water on the third day and on the seventh day
Numbers19:13 Whoever touches a dead person
Numbers19:14 “This is the law when a man dies in a tent: every one who comes into the tent
Numbers19:15 And every open vessel
Numbers19:16 Whoever in the open field touches one who is slain with a sword
Numbers19:17 For the unclean they shall take some ashes of the burnt sin offering
Numbers19:18 then a clean person shall take hyssop
Numbers19:19 and the clean person shall sprinkle upon the unclean on the third day and on the seventh day; thus on the seventh day he shall cleanse him
Numbers19:20 “But the man who is unclean and does not cleanse himself
Numbers19:21 And it shall be a perpetual statute for them. He who sprinkles the water for impurity shall wash his clothes; and he who touches the water for impurity shall be unclean until evening.
Numbers19:22 And whatever the unclean person touches shall be unclean; and any one who touches it shall be unclean until evening.”

Numbers33:52 then you shall drive out all the inhabitants of the land from before you
Numbers33:53 and you shall take possession of the land and settle in it
Numbers33:54 You shall inherit the land by lot according to your families; to a large tribe you shall give a large inheritance
Numbers33:55 But if you do not drive out the inhabitants of the land from before you
Numbers33:56 And I will do to you as I thought to do to them.”

Numbers35:1 The Lord said to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho
Numbers35:2 “Command the people of Israel
Numbers35:3 The cities shall be theirs to dwell in
Numbers35:4 The pasture lands of the cities
Numbers35:5 And you shall measure
Numbers35:6 The cities which you give to the Levites shall be the six cities of refuge
Numbers35:7 All the cities which you give to the Levites shall be forty-eight
Numbers35:8 And as for the cities which you shall give from the possession of the people of Israel
Numbers35:9 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers35:10 “Say to the people of Israel
Numbers35:11 then you shall select cities to be cities of refuge for you
Numbers35:12 The cities shall be for you a refuge from the avenger
Numbers35:13 And the cities which you give shall be your six cities of refuge.
Numbers35:14 You shall give three cities beyond the Jordan
Numbers35:15 These six cities shall be for refuge for the people of Israel
Numbers35:16 “But if he struck him down with an instrument of iron
Numbers35:17 And if he struck him down with a stone in the hand
Numbers35:18 Or if he struck him down with a weapon of wood in the hand
Numbers35:19 The avenger of blood shall himself put the murderer to death; when he meets him
Numbers35:20 And if he stabbed him from hatred
Numbers35:21 or in enmity struck him down with his hand
Numbers35:22 “But if he stabbed him suddenly without enmity
Numbers35:23 or used a stone
Numbers35:24 then the congregation shall judge between the manslayer and the avenger of blood
Numbers35:25 and the congregation shall rescue the manslayer from the hand of the avenger of blood
Numbers35:26 But if the manslayer shall at any time go beyond the bounds of his city of refuge to which he fled
Numbers35:27 and the avenger of blood finds him outside the bounds of his city of refuge
Numbers35:28 For the man must remain in his city of refuge until the death of the high priest; but after the death of the high priest the manslayer may return to the land of his possession.
Numbers35:29 “And these things shall be for a statute and ordinance to you throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
Numbers35:30 If any one kills a person
Numbers35:31 Moreover you shall accept no ransom for the life of a murderer
Numbers35:32 And you shall accept no ransom for him who has fled to his city of refuge
Numbers35:33 You shall not thus pollute the land in which you live; for blood pollutes the land
Numbers35:34 You shall not defile the land in which you live

Deuteronomy11:5 and what he did to you in the wilderness
Deuteronomy11:6 and what he did to Dathan and Abiram the sons of Eliab
Deuteronomy11:7 for your eyes have seen all the great work of the Lord which he did.
Deuteronomy11:8 “You shall therefore keep all the commandment which I command you this day
Deuteronomy11:9 and that you may live long in the land which the Lord swore to your fathers to give to them and to their descendants
Deuteronomy11:10 For the land which you are entering to take possession of it is not like the land of Egypt
Deuteronomy11:11 but the land which you are going over to possess is a land of hills and valleys
Deuteronomy11:12 a land which the Lord your God cares for; the eyes of the Lord your God are always upon it
Deuteronomy11:13 “And if you will obey my commandments which I command you this day
Deuteronomy11:14 he will give the rain for your land in its season
Deuteronomy11:15 And he will give grass in your fields for your cattle
Deuteronomy11:16 Take heed lest your heart be deceived
Deuteronomy11:17 and the anger of the Lord be kindled against you
Deuteronomy11:18 “You shall therefore lay up these words of mine in your heart and in your soul; and you shall bind them as a sign upon your hand
Deuteronomy11:19 And you shall teach them to your children
Deuteronomy11:20 And you shall write them upon the doorposts of your house and upon your gates
Deuteronomy11:21 that your days and the days of your children may be multiplied in the land which the Lord swore to your fathers to give them
Deuteronomy11:22 For if you will be careful to do all this commandment which I command you to do
Deuteronomy11:23 then the Lord will drive out all these nations before you
Deuteronomy11:24 Every place on which the sole of your foot treads shall be yours; your territory shall be from the wilderness and Lebanon and from the River
Deuteronomy11:25 No man shall be able to stand against you; the Lord your God will lay the fear of you and the dread of you upon all the land that you shall tread
Deuteronomy11:26 “Behold
Deuteronomy11:27 the blessing
Deuteronomy11:28 and the curse
Deuteronomy11:29 And when the Lord your God brings you into the land which you are entering to take possession of it
Deuteronomy11:30 Are they not beyond the Jordan
Deuteronomy11:31 For you are to pass over the Jordan to go in to take possession of the land which the Lord your God gives you; and when you possess it and live in it
Deuteronomy11:32 you shall be careful to do all the statutes and the ordinances which I set before you this day.
Deuteronomy12:1 “These are the statutes and ordinances which you shall be careful to do in the land which the Lord
Deuteronomy12:2 You shall surely destroy all the places where the nations whom you shall dispossess served their gods
Deuteronomy12:3 you shall tear down their altars
Deuteronomy12:4 You shall not do so to the Lord your God.
Deuteronomy12:5 But you shall seek the place which the Lord your God will choose out of all your tribes to put his name and make his habitation there; thither you shall go
Deuteronomy12:6 and thither you shall bring your burnt offerings and your sacrifices
Deuteronomy12:7 and there you shall eat before the Lord your God
Deuteronomy12:8 You shall not do according to all that we are doing here this day
Deuteronomy12:9 for you have not as yet come to the rest and to the inheritance which the Lord your God gives you.
Deuteronomy12:10 But when you go over the Jordan
Deuteronomy12:11 then to the place which the Lord your God will choose
Deuteronomy12:12 And you shall rejoice before the Lord your God
Deuteronomy12:13 Take heed that you do not offer your burnt offerings at every place that you see;
Deuteronomy12:14 but at the place which the Lord will choose in one of your tribes
Deuteronomy12:15 “However
Deuteronomy12:16 Only you shall not eat the blood; you shall pour it out upon the earth like water.
Deuteronomy12:17 You may not eat within your towns the tithe of your grain or of your wine or of your oil
Deuteronomy12:18 but you shall eat them before the Lord your God in the place which the Lord your God will choose
Deuteronomy12:19 Take heed that you do not forsake the Levite as long as you live in your land.
Deuteronomy12:20 “When the Lord your God enlarges your territory
Deuteronomy12:21 If the place which the Lord your God will choose to put his name there is too far from you
Deuteronomy12:22 Just as the gazelle or the hart is eaten
Deuteronomy12:23 Only be sure that you do not eat the blood; for the blood is the life
Deuteronomy12:24 You shall not eat it; you shall pour it out upon the earth like water.
Deuteronomy12:25 You shall not eat it; that all may go well with you and with your children after you
Deuteronomy12:26 But the holy things which are due from you
Deuteronomy12:27 and offer your burnt offerings
Deuteronomy12:28 Be careful to heed all these words which I command you

Deuteronomy12:30 take heed that you be not ensnared to follow them
Deuteronomy12:31 You shall not do so to the Lord your God; for every abominable thing which the Lord hates they have done for their gods; for they even burn their sons and their daughters in the fire to their gods.
Deuteronomy12:32 “Everything that I command you shall be careful to do; you shall not add to it or take from it.

Deuteronomy15:2 And this is the manner of the release: every creditor shall release what he has lent to his neighbor; he shall not exact it of his neighbor
Deuteronomy15:3 Of a foreigner you may exact it; but whatever of yours is with your brother your hand shall release.

Deuteronomy16:1 “Observe the month of Abib

Deuteronomy16:4 No leaven shall be seen with you in all your territory for seven days; nor shall any of the flesh which you sacrifice on the evening of the first day remain all night until morning.
Deuteronomy16:5 You may not offer the passover sacrifice within any of your towns which the Lord your God gives you;
Deuteronomy16:6 but at the place which the Lord your God will choose
Deuteronomy16:7 And you shall boil it and eat it at the place which the Lord your God will choose; and in the morning you shall turn and go to your tents.

2Samuel22:30 Yea

1Kings6:1 In the four hundred and eightieth year after the people of Israel came out of the land of Egypt
1Kings6:2 The house which King Solomon built for the Lord was sixty cubits long
1Kings6:3 The vestibule in front of the nave of the house was twenty cubits long
1Kings6:4 And he made for the house windows with recessed frames.
1Kings6:5 He also built a structure against the wall of the house
1Kings6:6 The lowest story was five cubits broad
1Kings6:7 When the house was built
1Kings6:8 The entrance for the lowest story was on the south side of the house; and one went up by stairs to the middle story
1Kings6:9 So he built the house
1Kings6:10 He built the structure against the whole house
1Kings6:11 Now the word of the Lord came to Solomon
1Kings6:12 “Concerning this house which you are building

1Kings6:14 So Solomon built the house
1Kings6:15 He lined the walls of the house on the inside with boards of cedar; from the floor of the house to the rafters of the ceiling
1Kings6:16 He built twenty cubits of the rear of the house with boards of cedar from the floor to the rafters
1Kings6:17 The house
1Kings6:18 The cedar within the house was carved in the form of gourds and open flowers; all was cedar
1Kings6:19 The inner sanctuary he prepared in the innermost part of the house
1Kings6:20 The inner sanctuary was twenty cubits long
1Kings6:21 And Solomon overlaid the inside of the house with pure gold
1Kings6:22 And he overlaid the whole house with gold
1Kings6:23 In the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim of olivewood
1Kings6:24 Five cubits was the length of one wing of the cherub
1Kings6:25 The other cherub also measured ten cubits; both cherubim had the same measure and the same form.
1Kings6:26 The height of one cherub was ten cubits
1Kings6:27 He put the cherubim in the innermost part of the house; and the wings of the cherubim were spread out so that a wing of one touched the one wall
1Kings6:28 And he overlaid the cherubim with gold.
1Kings6:29 He carved all the walls of the house round about with carved figures of cherubim and palm trees and open flowers
1Kings6:30 The floor of the house he overlaid with gold in the inner and outer rooms.
1Kings6:31 For the entrance to the inner sanctuary he made doors of olivewood; the lintel and the doorposts formed a pentagon.
1Kings6:32 He covered the two doors of olivewood with carvings of cherubim
1Kings6:33 So also he made for the entrance to the nave doorposts of olivewood
1Kings6:34 and two doors of cypress wood; the two leaves of the one door were folding
1Kings6:35 On them he carved cherubim and palm trees and open flowers; and he overlaid them with gold evenly applied upon the carved work.
1Kings6:36 He built the inner court with three courses of hewn stone and one course of cedar beams.
1Kings6:37 In the fourth year the foundation of the house of the Lord was laid
1Kings6:38 And in the eleventh year

1Kings7:15 He cast two pillars of bronze. Eighteen cubits was the height of one pillar
1Kings7:16 He also made two capitals of molten bronze
1Kings7:17 Then he made two nets of checker work with wreaths of chain work for the capitals upon the tops of the pillars; a net for the one capital
1Kings7:18 Likewise he made pomegranates; in two rows round about upon the one network
1Kings7:19 Now the capitals that were upon the tops of the pillars in the vestibule were of lily-work
1Kings7:20 The capitals were upon the two pillars and also above the rounded projection which was beside the network; there were two hundred pomegranates
1Kings7:21 He set up the pillars at the vestibule of the temple; he set up the pillar on the south and called its name Jachin; and he set up the pillar on the north and called its name Boaz.
1Kings7:22 And upon the tops of the pillars was lily-work. Thus the work of the pillars was finished.
1Kings7:23 Then he made the molten sea; it was round
1Kings7:24 Under its brim were gourds
1Kings7:25 It stood upon twelve oxen
1Kings7:26 Its thickness was a handbreadth; and its brim was made like the brim of a cup
1Kings7:27 He also made the ten stands of bronze; each stand was four cubits long
1Kings7:28 This was the construction of the stands: they had panels
1Kings7:29 and on the panels that were set in the frames were lions
1Kings7:30 Moreover each stand had four bronze wheels and axles of bronze; and at the four corners were supports for a laver. The supports were cast
1Kings7:31 Its opening was within a crown which projected upward one cubit; its opening was round
1Kings7:32 And the four wheels were underneath the panels; the axles of the wheels were of one piece with the stands; and the height of a wheel was a cubit and a half.
1Kings7:33 The wheels were made like a chariot wheel; their axles
1Kings7:34 There were four supports at the four corners of each stand; the supports were of one piece with the stands.
1Kings7:35 And on the top of the stand there was a round band half a cubit high; and on the top of the stand its stays and its panels were of one piece with it.
1Kings7:36 And on the surfaces of its stays and on its panels
1Kings7:37 After this manner he made the ten stands; all of them were cast alike
1Kings7:38 And he made ten lavers of bronze; each laver held forty baths
1Kings7:39 And he set the stands
1Kings7:40 Hiram also made the pots
1Kings7:41 the two pillars
1Kings7:42 and the four hundred pomegranates for the two networks
1Kings7:43 the ten stands
1Kings7:44 and the one sea
1Kings7:45 Now the pots
1Kings7:46 In the plain of the Jordan the king cast them
1Kings7:47 And Solomon left all the vessels unweighed
1Kings7:48 So Solomon made all the vessels that were in the house of the Lord: the golden altar
1Kings7:49 the lampstands of pure gold
1Kings7:50 the cups
1Kings7:51 Thus all the work that King Solomon did on the house of the Lord was finished. And Solomon brought in the things which David his father had dedicated
1Kings8:1 Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel and all the heads of the tribes
1Kings8:2 And all the men of Israel assembled to King Solomon at the feast in the month Ethanim
1Kings8:3 And all the elders of Israel came
1Kings8:4 And they brought up the ark of the Lord
1Kings8:5 And King Solomon and all the congregation of Israel
1Kings8:6 Then the priests brought the ark of the covenant of the Lord to its place
1Kings8:7 For the cherubim spread out their wings over the place of the ark
1Kings8:8 And the poles were so long that the ends of the poles were seen from the holy place before the inner sanctuary; but they could not be seen from outside; and they are there to this day.
1Kings8:9 There was nothing in the ark except the two tables of stone which Moses put there at Horeb
1Kings8:10 And when the priests came out of the holy place
1Kings8:11 so that the priests could not stand to minister because of the cloud; for the glory of the Lord filled the house of the Lord.
1Kings8:12 Then Solomon said
1Kings8:13 I have built thee an exalted house
1Kings8:14 Then the king faced about
1Kings8:15 And he said
1Kings8:16 ‘Since the day that I brought my people Israel out of Egypt
1Kings8:17 Now it was in the heart of David my father to build a house for the name of the Lord
1Kings8:18 But the Lord said to David my father
1Kings8:19 nevertheless you shall not build the house
1Kings8:20 Now the Lord has fulfilled his promise which he made; for I have risen in the place of David my father
1Kings8:21 And there I have provided a place for the ark
1Kings8:22 Then Solomon stood before the altar of the Lord in the presence of all the assembly of Israel
1Kings8:23 and said
1Kings8:24 who hast kept with thy servant David my father what thou didst declare to him; yea
1Kings8:25 Now therefore
1Kings8:26 Now therefore
1Kings8:27 “But will God indeed dwell on the earth? Behold
1Kings8:28 Yet have regard to the prayer of thy servant and to his supplication
1Kings8:29 that thy eyes may be open night and day toward this house
1Kings8:30 And hearken thou to the supplication of thy servant and of thy people Israel
1Kings8:31 “If a man sins against his neighbor and is made to take an oath
1Kings8:32 then hear thou in heaven
1Kings8:33 “When thy people Israel are defeated before the enemy because they have sinned against thee
1Kings8:34 then hear thou in heaven
1Kings8:35 “When heaven is shut up and there is no rain because they have sinned against thee
1Kings8:36 then hear thou in heaven
1Kings8:37 “If there is famine in the land
1Kings8:38 whatever prayer
1Kings8:39 then hear thou in heaven thy dwelling place
1Kings8:40 that they may fear thee all the days that they live in the land which thou gavest to our fathers.
1Kings8:41 “Likewise when a foreigner
1Kings8:42 (for they shall hear of thy great name
1Kings8:43 hear thou in heaven thy dwelling place
1Kings8:44 “If thy people go out to battle against their enemy
1Kings8:45 then hear thou in heaven their prayer and their supplication
1Kings8:46 “If they sin against thee—for there is no man who does not sin—and thou art angry with them
1Kings8:47 yet if they lay it to heart in the land to which they have been carried captive
1Kings8:48 if they repent with all their mind and with all their heart in the land of their enemies
1Kings8:49 then hear thou in heaven thy dwelling place their prayer and their supplication
1Kings8:50 and forgive thy people who have sinned against thee
1Kings8:51 (for they are thy people
1Kings8:52 Let thy eyes be open to the supplication of thy servant
1Kings8:53 For thou didst separate them from among all the peoples of the earth
1Kings8:54 Now as Solomon finished offering all this prayer and supplication to the Lord
1Kings8:55 and he stood
1Kings8:56 “Blessed be the Lord who has given rest to his people Israel
1Kings8:57 The Lord our God be with us
1Kings8:58 that he may incline our hearts to him
1Kings8:59 Let these words of mine
1Kings8:60 that all the peoples of the earth may know that the Lord is God; there is no other.
1Kings8:61 Let your heart therefore be wholly true to the Lord our God
1Kings8:62 Then the king
1Kings8:63 Solomon offered as peace offerings to the Lord twenty-two thousand oxen and a hundred and twenty thousand sheep. So the king and all the people of Israel dedicated the house of the Lord.
1Kings8:64 The same day the king consecrated the middle of the court that was before the house of the Lord; for there he offered the burnt offering and the cereal offering and the fat pieces of the peace offerings
1Kings8:65 So Solomon held the feast at that time
1Kings8:66 On the eighth day he sent the people away; and they blessed the king
1Kings9:1 When Solomon had finished building the house of the Lord and the king's house and all that Solomon desired to build
1Kings9:2 the Lord appeared to Solomon a second time
1Kings9:3 And the Lord said to him
1Kings9:4 And as for you
1Kings9:5 then I will establish your royal throne over Israel for ever
1Kings9:6 But if you turn aside from following me
1Kings9:7 then I will cut off Israel from the land which I have given them; and the house which I have consecrated for my name I will cast out of my sight; and Israel will become a proverb and a byword among all peoples.
1Kings9:8 And this house will become a heap of ruins; everyone passing by it will be astonished
1Kings9:9 Then they will say
1Kings9:10 At the end of twenty years
1Kings9:11 and Hiram king of Tyre had supplied Solomon with cedar and cypress timber and gold
1Kings9:12 But when Hiram came from Tyre to see the cities which Solomon had given him
1Kings9:13 Therefore he said
1Kings9:14 Hiram had sent to the king one hundred and twenty talents of gold.
1Kings9:15 And this is the account of the forced labor which King Solomon levied to build the house of the Lord and his own house and the Millo and the wall of Jerusalem and Hazor and Megiddo and Gezer
1Kings9:16 (Pharaoh king of Egypt had gone up and captured Gezer and burnt it with fire
1Kings9:17 so Solomon rebuilt Gezer) and Lower Beth-horon
1Kings9:18 and Baalath and Tamar in the wilderness
1Kings9:19 and all the store-cities that Solomon had
1Kings9:20 All the people who were left of the Amorites
1Kings9:21 their descendants who were left after them in the land
1Kings9:22 But of the people of Israel Solomon made no slaves; they were the soldiers
1Kings9:23 These were the chief officers who were over Solomon's work: five hundred and fifty
1Kings9:24 But Pharaoh's daughter went up from the city of David to her own house which Solomon had built for her; then he built the Millo.
1Kings9:25 Three times a year Solomon used to offer up burnt offerings and peace offerings upon the altar which he built to the Lord
1Kings9:26 King Solomon built a fleet of ships at Ezion-geber
1Kings9:27 And Hiram sent with the fleet his servants

1Kings10:1 Now when the queen of Sheba heard of the fame of Solomon concerning the name of the Lord
1Kings10:2 She came to Jerusalem with a very great retinue
1Kings10:3 And Solomon answered all her questions; there was nothing hidden from the king which he could not explain to her.
1Kings10:4 And when the queen of Sheba had seen all the wisdom of Solomon
1Kings10:5 the food of his table
1Kings10:6 And she said to the king
1Kings10:7 but I did not believe the reports until I came and my own eyes had seen it; and behold
1Kings10:8 Happy are your wives! Happy are these your servants
1Kings10:9 Blessed be the Lord your God
1Kings10:10 Then she gave the king a hundred and twenty talents of gold
1Kings10:11 Moreover the fleet of Hiram
1Kings10:12 And the king made of the almug wood supports for the house of the Lord
1Kings10:13 And King Solomon gave to the queen of Sheba all that she desired
1Kings10:14 Now the weight of gold that came to Solomon in one year was six hundred and sixty-six talents of gold
1Kings10:15 besides that which came from the traders and from the traffic of the merchants
1Kings10:16 King Solomon made two hundred large shields of beaten gold; six hundred shekels of gold went into each shield.
1Kings10:17 And he made three hundred shields of beaten gold; three minas of gold went into each shield; and the king put them in the House of the Forest of Lebanon.
1Kings10:18 The king also made a great ivory throne
1Kings10:19 The throne had six steps
1Kings10:20 while twelve lions stood there
1Kings10:21 All King Solomon's drinking vessels were of gold
1Kings10:22 For the king had a fleet of ships of Tarshish at sea with the fleet of Hiram. Once every three years the fleet of ships of Tarshish used to come bringing gold
1Kings10:23 Thus King Solomon excelled all the kings of the earth in riches and in wisdom.
1Kings10:24 And the whole earth sought the presence of Solomon to hear his wisdom
1Kings10:25 Every one of them brought his present
1Kings10:26 And Solomon gathered together chariots and horsemen; he had fourteen hundred chariots and twelve thousand horsemen
1Kings10:27 And the king made silver as common in Jerusalem as stone
1Kings10:28 And Solomon's import of horses was from Egypt and Kue
1Kings10:29 A chariot could be imported from Egypt for six hundred shekels of silver
1Kings11:1 Now King Solomon loved many foreign women: the daughter of Pharaoh
1Kings11:2 from the nations concerning which the Lord had said to the people of Israel
1Kings11:3 He had seven hundred wives
1Kings11:4 For when Solomon was old his wives turned away his heart after other gods; and his heart was not wholly true to the Lord his God
1Kings11:5 For Solomon went after Ashtoreth the goddess of the Sidonians
1Kings11:6 So Solomon did what was evil in the sight of the Lord
1Kings11:7 Then Solomon built a high place for Chemosh the abomination of Moab
1Kings11:8 And so he did for all his foreign wives
1Kings11:9 And the Lord was angry with Solomon
1Kings11:10 and had commanded him concerning this thing
1Kings11:11 Therefore the Lord said to Solomon

1Kings11:13 However I will not tear away all the kingdom; but I will give one tribe to your son

1Kings11:15 For when David was in Edom

1Kings11:20 And the sister of Tahpenes bore him Genubath his son

1Kings11:22 But Pharaoh said to him
1Kings11:23 God also raised up as an adversary to him
1Kings11:24 And he gathered men about him and became leader of a marauding band
1Kings11:25 He was an adversary of Israel all the days of Solomon
1Kings11:26 Jeroboam the son of Nebat
1Kings11:27 And this was the reason why he lifted up his hand against the king. Solomon built the Millo
1Kings11:28 The man Jeroboam was very able
1Kings11:29 And at that time

1Kings11:35 but I will take the kingdom out of his son's hand
1Kings11:36 Yet to his son I will give one tribe

1Kings15:1 Now in the eighteenth year of King Jeroboam the son of Nebat
1Kings15:2 He reigned for three years in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Maacah the daughter of Abishalom.

1Kings15:4 Nevertheless for David's sake the Lord his God gave him a lamp in Jerusalem
1Kings15:5 because David did what was right in the eyes of the Lord

1Kings15:14 But the high places were not taken away. Nevertheless the heart of Asa was wholly true to the Lord all his days.
1Kings15:15 And he brought into the house of the Lord the votive gifts of his father and his own votive gifts

2Kings21:19 Amon was twenty-two years old when he began to reign
2Kings21:20 And he did what was evil in the sight of the Lord
2Kings21:21 He walked in all the way in which his father walked
2Kings21:22 he forsook the Lord
2Kings21:23 And the servants of Amon conspired against him
2Kings21:24 But the people of the land slew all those who had conspired against King Amon
2Kings21:25 Now the rest of the acts of Amon which he did
2Kings21:26 And he was buried in his tomb in the garden of Uzza; and Josiah his son reigned in his stead.
2Kings22:1 Josiah was eight years old when he began to reign
2Kings22:2 And he did what was right in the eyes of the Lord
2Kings22:3 In the eighteenth year of King Josiah
2Kings22:4 “Go up to Hilkiah the high priest
2Kings22:5 and let it be given into the hand of the workmen who have the oversight of the house of the Lord; and let them give it to the workmen who are at the house of the Lord
2Kings22:6 that is
2Kings22:7 But no accounting shall be asked from them for the money which is delivered into their hand
2Kings22:8 And Hilkiah the high priest said to Shaphan the secretary
2Kings22:9 And Shaphan the secretary came to the king
2Kings22:10 Then Shaphan the secretary told the king
2Kings22:11 And when the king heard the words of the book of the law
2Kings22:12 And the king commanded Hilkiah the priest
2Kings22:13 “Go
2Kings22:14 So Hilkiah the priest
2Kings22:15 And she said to them
2Kings22:16 Thus says the Lord
2Kings22:17 Because they have forsaken me and have burned incense to other gods
2Kings22:18 But as to the king of Judah
2Kings22:19 because your heart was penitent
2Kings22:20 Therefore
2Kings23:1 Then the king sent
2Kings23:2 And the king went up to the house of the Lord
2Kings23:3 And the king stood by the pillar and made a covenant before the Lord
2Kings23:4 And the king commanded Hilkiah
2Kings23:5 And he deposed the idolatrous priests whom the kings of Judah had ordained to burn incense in the high places at the cities of Judah and round about Jerusalem; those also who burned incense to Baal
2Kings23:6 And he brought out the Asherah from the house of the Lord
2Kings23:7 And he broke down the houses of the male cult prostitutes which were in the house of the Lord
2Kings23:8 And he brought all the priests out of the cities of Judah
2Kings23:9 However
2Kings23:10 And he defiled Topheth
2Kings23:11 And he removed the horses that the kings of Judah had dedicated to the sun
2Kings23:12 And the altars on the roof of the upper chamber of Ahaz
2Kings23:13 And the king defiled the high places that were east of Jerusalem
2Kings23:14 And he broke in pieces the pillars
2Kings23:15 Moreover the altar at Bethel
2Kings23:16 And as Josiah turned
2Kings23:17 Then he said
2Kings23:18 And he said
2Kings23:19 And all the shrines also of the high places that were in the cities of Samaria
2Kings23:20 And he slew all the priests of the high places who were there
2Kings23:21 And the king commanded all the people
2Kings23:22 For no such passover had been kept since the days of the judges who judged Israel
2Kings23:23 but in the eighteenth year of King Josiah this passover was kept to the Lord in Jerusalem.
2Kings23:24 Moreover Josiah put away the mediums and the wizards and the teraphim and the idols and all the abominations that were seen in the land of Judah and in Jerusalem
2Kings23:25 Before him there was no king like him
2Kings23:26 Still the Lord did not turn from the fierceness of his great wrath
2Kings23:27 And the Lord said
2Kings23:28 Now the rest of the acts of Josiah
2Kings23:29 In his days Pharaoh Neco king of Egypt went up to the king of Assyria to the river Euphrates. King Josiah went to meet him; and Pharaoh Neco slew him at Megiddo
2Kings23:30 And his servants carried him dead in a chariot from Megiddo
2Kings23:31 Jehoahaz was twenty-three years old when he began to reign
2Kings23:32 And he did what was evil in the sight of the Lord
2Kings23:33 And Pharaoh Neco put him in bonds at Riblah in the land of Hamath
2Kings23:34 And Pharaoh Neco made Eliakim the son of Josiah king in the place of Josiah his father
2Kings23:35 And Jehoiakim gave the silver and the gold to Pharaoh
2Kings23:36 Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he began to reign
2Kings23:37 And he did what was evil in the sight of the Lord
2Kings24:1 In his days Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up
2Kings24:2 And the Lord sent against him bands of the Chaldeans
2Kings24:3 Surely this came upon Judah at the command of the Lord
2Kings24:4 and also for the innocent blood that he had shed; for he filled Jerusalem with innocent blood
2Kings24:5 Now the rest of the deeds of Jehoiakim
2Kings24:6 So Jehoiakim slept with his fathers
2Kings24:7 And the king of Egypt did not come again out of his land
2Kings24:8 Jehoiachin was eighteen years old when he became king
2Kings24:9 And he did what was evil in the sight of the Lord
2Kings24:10 At that time the servants of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up to Jerusalem
2Kings24:11 And Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came to the city
2Kings24:12 and Jehoiachin the king of Judah gave himself up to the king of Babylon
2Kings24:13 and carried off all the treasures of the house of the Lord
2Kings24:14 He carried away all Jerusalem
2Kings24:15 And he carried away Jehoiachin to Babylon; the king's mother
2Kings24:16 And the king of Babylon brought captive to Babylon all the men of valor
2Kings24:17 And the king of Babylon made Mattaniah
2Kings24:18 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king
2Kings24:19 And he did what was evil in the sight of the Lord
2Kings24:20 For because of the anger of the Lord it came to the point in Jerusalem and Judah that he cast them out from his presence. And Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.
2Kings25:1 And in the ninth year of his reign
2Kings25:2 So the city was besieged till the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.
2Kings25:3 On the ninth day of the fourth month the famine was so severe in the city that there was no food for the people of the land.
2Kings25:4 Then a breach was made in the city; the king with all the men of war fled by night by the way of the gate between the two walls
2Kings25:5 But the army of the Chaldeans pursued the king
2Kings25:6 Then they captured the king
2Kings25:7 They slew the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes
2Kings25:8 In the fifth month
2Kings25:9 And he burned the house of the Lord
2Kings25:10 And all the army of the Chaldeans
2Kings25:11 And the rest of the people who were left in the city and the deserters who had deserted to the king of Babylon
2Kings25:12 But the captain of the guard left some of the poorest of the land to be vinedressers and plowmen.
2Kings25:13 And the pillars of bronze that were in the house of the Lord
2Kings25:14 And they took away the pots
2Kings25:15 the firepans also
2Kings25:16 As for the two pillars
2Kings25:17 The height of the one pillar was eighteen cubits
2Kings25:18 And the captain of the guard took Seraiah the chief priest
2Kings25:19 and from the city he took an officer who had been in command of the men of war
2Kings25:20 And Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took them
2Kings25:21 And the king of Babylon smote them
2Kings25:22 And over the people who remained in the land of Judah
2Kings25:23 Now when all the captains of the forces in the open country and their men heard that the king of Babylon had appointed Gedaliah governor
2Kings25:24 And Gedaliah swore to them and their men
2Kings25:25 But in the seventh month
2Kings25:26 Then all the people

2Kings25:28 and he spoke kindly to him
2Kings25:29 So Jehoiachin put off his prison garments. And every day of his life he dined regularly at the king's table;

2Chronicles3:1 Then Solomon began to build the house of the Lord in Jerusalem on Mount Moriah
2Chronicles3:2 He began to build in the second month of the fourth year of his reign.
2Chronicles3:3 These are Solomon's measurements for building the house of God: the length
2Chronicles3:4 The vestibule in front of the nave of the house was twenty cubits long
2Chronicles3:5 The nave he lined with cypress
2Chronicles3:6 He adorned the house with settings of precious stones. The gold was gold of Parvaim.
2Chronicles3:7 So he lined the house with gold—its beams
2Chronicles3:8 And he made the most holy place; its length
2Chronicles3:9 The weight of the nails was one shekel to fifty shekels of gold. And he overlaid the upper chambers with gold.
2Chronicles3:10 In the most holy place he made two cherubim of wood and overlaid them with gold.

2Chronicles3:12 and of this cherub

2Chronicles3:14 And he made the veil of blue and purple and crimson fabrics and fine linen
2Chronicles3:15 In front of the house he made two pillars thirty-five cubits high
2Chronicles3:16 He made chains like a necklace and put them on the tops of the pillars; and he made a hundred pomegranates
2Chronicles3:17 He set up the pillars in front of the temple

2Chronicles6:29 whatever prayer

2Chronicles6:33 hear thou from heaven thy dwelling place
2Chronicles6:34 “If thy people go out to battle against their enemies
2Chronicles6:35 then hear thou from heaven their prayer and their supplication
2Chronicles6:36 “If they sin against thee—for there is no man who does not sin—and thou art angry with them
2Chronicles6:37 yet if they lay it to heart in the land to which they have been carried captive

2Chronicles6:39 then hear thou from heaven thy dwelling place their prayer and their supplications
2Chronicles6:40 Now

2Chronicles6:42 O Lord God
2Chronicles7:1 When Solomon had ended his prayer
2Chronicles7:2 And the priests could not enter the house of the Lord
2Chronicles7:3 When all the children of Israel saw the fire come down and the glory of the Lord upon the temple
2Chronicles7:4 Then the king and all the people offered sacrifice before the Lord.
2Chronicles7:5 King Solomon offered as a sacrifice twenty-two thousand oxen and a hundred and twenty thousand sheep. So the king and all the people dedicated the house of God.
2Chronicles7:6 The priests stood at their posts; the Levites also
2Chronicles7:7 And Solomon consecrated the middle of the court that was before the house of the Lord; for there he offered the burnt offering and the fat of the peace offerings
2Chronicles7:8 At that time Solomon held the feast for seven days
2Chronicles7:9 And on the eighth day they held a solemn assembly; for they had kept the dedication of the altar seven days and the feast seven days.
2Chronicles7:10 On the twenty-third day of the seventh month he sent the people away to their homes
2Chronicles7:11 Thus Solomon finished the house of the Lord and the king's house; all that Solomon had planned to do in the house of the Lord and in his own house he successfully accomplished.

2Chronicles24:17 Now after the death of Jehoiada the princes of Judah came and did obeisance to the king; then the king harkened to them.
2Chronicles24:18 And they forsook the house of the Lord
2Chronicles24:19 Yet he sent prophets among them to bring them back to the Lord; these testified against them
2Chronicles24:20 Then the Spirit of God took possession of Zechariah the son of Jehoiada the priest; and he stood above the people
2Chronicles24:21 But they conspired against him
2Chronicles24:22 Thus Joash the king did not remember the kindness which Jehoiada
2Chronicles24:23 At the end of the year the army of the Syrians came up against Joash. They came to Judah and Jerusalem
2Chronicles24:24 Though the army of the Syrians had come with few men

2Chronicles25:1 Amaziah was twenty-five years old when he began to reign
2Chronicles25:2 And he did what was right in the eyes of the Lord
2Chronicles25:3 And as soon as the royal power was firmly in his hand he killed his servants who had slain the king his father.
2Chronicles25:4 But he did not put their children to death
