2Chronicles32:1 After these things and these acts of faithfulness Sennacherib king of Assyria came and invaded Judah and encamped against the fortified cities
2Chronicles32:2 And when Hezekiah saw that Sennacherib had come and intended to fight against Jerusalem
2Chronicles32:3 he planned with his officers and his mighty men to stop the water of the springs that were outside the city; and they helped him.
2Chronicles32:4 A great many people were gathered
2Chronicles32:5 He set to work resolutely and built up all the wall that was broken down
2Chronicles32:6 And he set combat commanders over the people
2Chronicles32:7 “Be strong and of good courage. Do not be afraid or dismayed before the king of Assyria and all the horde that is with him; for there is one greater with us than with him.
2Chronicles32:8 With him is an arm of flesh; but with us is the Lord our God
2Chronicles32:9 After this Sennacherib king of Assyria
2Chronicles32:10 “Thus says Sennacherib king of Assyria
2Chronicles32:11 Is not Hezekiah misleading you
2Chronicles32:12 Has not this same Hezekiah taken away his high places and his altars and commanded Judah and Jerusalem
2Chronicles32:13 Do you not know what I and my fathers have done to all the peoples of other lands? Were the gods of the nations of those lands at all able to deliver their lands out of my hand?
2Chronicles32:14 Who among all the gods of those nations which my fathers utterly destroyed was able to deliver his people from my hand
2Chronicles32:15 Now therefore do not let Hezekiah deceive you or mislead you in this fashion
2Chronicles32:16 And his servants said still more against the Lord God and against his servant Hezekiah.
2Chronicles32:17 And he wrote letters to cast contempt on the Lord the God of Israel and to speak against him
2Chronicles32:18 And they shouted it with a loud voice in the language of Judah to the people of Jerusalem who were upon the wall
2Chronicles32:19 And they spoke of the God of Jerusalem as they spoke of the gods of the peoples of the earth
2Chronicles32:20 Then Hezekiah the king and Isaiah the prophet
2Chronicles32:21 And the Lord sent an angel
2Chronicles32:22 So the Lord saved Hezekiah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem from the hand of Sennacherib king of Assyria and from the hand of all his enemies; and he gave them rest on every side.
2Chronicles32:23 And many brought gifts to the Lord to Jerusalem and precious things to Hezekiah king of Judah
2Chronicles32:24 In those days Hezekiah became sick and was at the point of death
2Chronicles32:25 But Hezekiah did not make return according to the benefit done to him
2Chronicles32:26 But Hezekiah humbled himself for the pride of his heart
2Chronicles32:27 And Hezekiah had very great riches and honor; and he made for himself treasuries for silver
2Chronicles32:28 storehouses also for the yield of grain
2Chronicles32:29 He likewise provided cities for himself
2Chronicles32:30 This same Hezekiah closed the upper outlet of the waters of Gihon and directed them down to the west side of the city of David. And Hezekiah prospered in all his works.
2Chronicles32:31 And so in the matter of the envoys of the princes of Babylon
2Chronicles32:32 Now the rest of the acts of Hezekiah

Ezra3:1 When the seventh month came
Ezra3:2 Then arose Jeshua the son of Jozadak

Ezra4:1 Now when the adversaries of Judah and Benjamin heard that the returned exiles were building a temple to the Lord
Ezra4:2 they approached Zerubbabel and the heads of fathers' houses and said to them
Ezra4:3 But Zerubbabel
Ezra4:4 Then the people of the land discouraged the people of Judah
Ezra4:5 and hired counselors against them to frustrate their purpose
Ezra4:6 And in the reign of Ahasu-erus
Ezra4:7 And in the days of Ar-ta-xerxes
Ezra4:8 Rehum the commander and Shimshai the scribe wrote a letter against Jerusalem to Ar-ta-xerxes the king as follows—
Ezra4:9 then wrote Rehum the commander
Ezra4:10 and the rest of the nations whom the great and noble Osnappar deported and settled in the cities of Samaria and in the rest of the province Beyond the River
Ezra4:11 this is a copy of the letter that they sent—“To Ar-ta-xerxes the king: Your servants
Ezra4:12 be it known to the king that the Jews who came up from you to us have gone to Jerusalem. They are rebuilding that rebellious and wicked city; they are finishing the walls and repairing the foundations.
Ezra4:13 Now be it known to the king that
Ezra4:14 Now because we eat the salt of the palace and it is not fitting for us to witness the king's dishonor
Ezra4:15 in order that search may be made in the book of the records of your fathers. You will find in the book of the records and learn that this city is a rebellious city
Ezra4:16 We make known to the king that
Ezra4:17 The king sent an answer: “To Rehum the commander and Shimshai the scribe and the rest of their associates who live in Samaria and in the rest of the province Beyond the River
Ezra4:18 the letter which you sent to us has been plainly read before me.
Ezra4:19 And I made a decree
Ezra4:20 And mighty kings have been over Jerusalem
Ezra4:21 Therefore make a decree that these men be made to cease
Ezra4:22 And take care not to be slack in this matter; why should damage grow to the hurt of the king?”
Ezra4:23 Then
Ezra4:24 Then the work on the house of God which is in Jerusalem stopped; and it ceased until the second year of the reign of Darius king of Persia.

Ezra6:2 And in Ecbatana
Ezra6:3 In the first year of Cyrus the king
Ezra6:4 with three courses of great stones and one course of timber; let the cost be paid from the royal treasury.

Ezra6:6 “Now therefore
Ezra6:7 let the work on this house of God alone; let the governor of the Jews and the elders of the Jews rebuild this house of God on its site.
Ezra6:8 Moreover I make a decree regarding what you shall do for these elders of the Jews for the rebuilding of this house of God; the cost is to be paid to these men in full and without delay from the royal revenue
Ezra6:9 And whatever is needed—young bulls
Ezra6:10 that they may offer pleasing sacrifices to the God of heaven
Ezra6:11 Also I make a decree that if any one alters this edict
Ezra6:12 May the God who has caused his name to dwell there overthrow any king or people that shall put forth a hand to alter this
Ezra6:13 Then
Ezra6:14 And the elders of the Jews built and prospered

Ezra6:16 And the people of Israel

Ezra6:18 And they set the priests in their divisions and the Levites in their courses
Ezra6:19 On the fourteenth day of the first month the returned exiles kept the passover.
Ezra6:20 For the priests and the Levites had purified themselves together; all of them were clean. So they killed the passover lamb for all the returned exiles
Ezra6:21 it was eaten by the people of Israel who had returned from exile
Ezra6:22 And they kept the feast of unleavened bread seven days with joy; for the Lord had made them joyful

Ezra9:2 For they have taken some of their daughters to be wives for themselves and for their sons; so that the holy race has mixed itself with the peoples of the lands. And in this faithlessness the hand of the officials and chief men has been foremost.”

Ezra9:4 Then all who trembled at the words of the God of Israel

Ezra9:11 which thou didst command by thy servants the prophets

Ezra9:13 And after all that has come upon us for our evil deeds and for our great guilt
Ezra9:14 shall we break thy commandments again and intermarry with the peoples who practice these abominations? Wouldst thou not be angry with us till thou wouldst consume us
Ezra9:15 O Lord the God of Israel

Nehemiah2:15 Then I went up in the night by the valley and inspected the wall; and I turned back and entered by the Valley Gate

Nehemiah3:1 Then Eliashib the high priest rose up with his brethren the priests and they built the Sheep Gate. They consecrated it and set its doors; they consecrated it as far as the Tower of the Hundred
Nehemiah3:2 And next to him the men of Jericho built. And next to them Zaccur the son of Imri built.
Nehemiah3:3 And the sons of Hassenaah built the Fish Gate; they laid its beams and set its doors
Nehemiah3:4 And next to them Meremoth the son of Uriah
Nehemiah3:5 And next to them the Tekoites repaired; but their nobles did not put their necks to the work of their Lord.
Nehemiah3:6 And Joiada the son of Paseah and Meshullam the son of Besodeiah repaired the Old Gate; they laid its beams and set its doors
Nehemiah3:7 And next to them repaired Melatiah the Gibeonite and Jadon the Meronothite
Nehemiah3:8 Next to them Uzziel the son of Harhaiah
Nehemiah3:9 Next to them Rephaiah the son of Hur
Nehemiah3:10 Next to them Jedaiah the son of Harumaph repaired opposite his house; and next to him Hattush the son of Hashabneiah repaired.
Nehemiah3:11 Malchijah the son of Harim and Hasshub the son of Pahath-moab repaired another section and the Tower of the Ovens.
Nehemiah3:12 Next to him Shallum the son of Hallohesh
Nehemiah3:13 Hanun and the inhabitants of Zanoah repaired the Valley Gate; they rebuilt it and set its doors
Nehemiah3:14 Malchijah the son of Rechab
Nehemiah3:15 And Shallum the son of Colhozeh
Nehemiah3:16 After him Nehemiah the son of Azbuk
Nehemiah3:17 After him the Levites repaired: Rehum the son of Bani; next to him Hashabiah
Nehemiah3:18 After him their brethren repaired: Bavvai the son of Henadad
Nehemiah3:19 next to him Ezer the son of Jeshua
Nehemiah3:20 After him Baruch the son of Zabbai repaired another section from the Angle to the door of the house of Eliashib the high priest.
Nehemiah3:21 After him Meremoth the son of Uriah
Nehemiah3:22 After him the priests
Nehemiah3:23 After them Benjamin and Hasshub repaired opposite their house. After them Azariah the son of Ma-aseiah
Nehemiah3:24 After him Binnui the son of Henadad repaired another section
Nehemiah3:25 and to the corner. Palal the son of Uzai repaired opposite the Angle and the tower projecting from the upper house of the king at the court of the guard. After him Pedaiah the son of Parosh
Nehemiah3:26 and the temple servants living on Ophel repaired to a point opposite the Water Gate on the east and the projecting tower.
Nehemiah3:27 After him the Tekoites repaired another section opposite the great projecting tower as far as the wall of Ophel.
Nehemiah3:28 Above the Horse Gate the priests repaired
Nehemiah3:29 After them Zadok the son of Immer repaired opposite his own house. After him Shemaiah the son of Shecaniah
Nehemiah3:30 After him Hananiah the son of Shelemiah and Hanun the sixth son of Zalaph repaired another section. After him Meshullam the son of Berechiah repaired opposite his chamber.
Nehemiah3:31 After him Malchijah
Nehemiah3:32 And between the upper chamber of the corner and the Sheep Gate the goldsmiths and the merchants repaired.
Nehemiah4:1 Now when Sanballat heard that we were building the wall
Nehemiah4:2 And he said in the presence of his brethren and of the army of Samaria
Nehemiah4:3 Tobiah the Ammonite was by him
Nehemiah4:4 Hear
Nehemiah4:5 Do not cover their guilt
Nehemiah4:6 So we built the wall; and all the wall was joined together to half its height. For the people had a mind to work.
Nehemiah4:7 But when Sanballat and Tobiah and the Arabs and the Ammonites and the Ashdodites heard that the repairing of the walls of Jerusalem was going forward and that the breaches were beginning to be closed
Nehemiah4:8 and they all plotted together to come and fight against Jerusalem and to cause confusion in it.
Nehemiah4:9 And we prayed to our God
Nehemiah4:10 But Judah said
Nehemiah4:11 And our enemies said
Nehemiah4:12 When the Jews who lived by them came they said to us ten times
Nehemiah4:13 So in the lowest parts of the space behind the wall
Nehemiah4:14 And I looked
Nehemiah4:15 When our enemies heard that it was known to us and that God had frustrated their plan
Nehemiah4:16 From that day on
Nehemiah4:17 who were building on the wall. Those who carried burdens were laden in such a way that each with one hand labored on the work and with the other held his weapon.
Nehemiah4:18 And each of the builders had his sword girded at his side while he built. The man who sounded the trumpet was beside me.
Nehemiah4:19 And I said to the nobles and to the officials and to the rest of the people
Nehemiah4:20 In the place where you hear the sound of the trumpet
Nehemiah4:21 So we labored at the work
Nehemiah4:22 I also said to the people at that time
Nehemiah4:23 So neither I nor my brethren nor my servants nor the men of the guard who followed me
Nehemiah5:1 Now there arose a great outcry of the people and of their wives against their Jewish brethren.
Nehemiah5:2 For there were those who said
Nehemiah5:3 There were also those who said
Nehemiah5:4 And there were those who said
Nehemiah5:5 Now our flesh is as the flesh of our brethren
Nehemiah5:6 I was very angry when I heard their outcry and these words.
Nehemiah5:7 I took counsel with myself
Nehemiah5:8 and said to them
Nehemiah5:9 So I said
Nehemiah5:10 Moreover I and my brethren and my servants are lending them money and grain. Let us leave off this interest.
Nehemiah5:11 Return to them this very day their fields
Nehemiah5:12 Then they said
Nehemiah5:13 I also shook out my lap and said
Nehemiah5:14 Moreover from the time that I was appointed to be their governor in the land of Judah
Nehemiah5:15 The former governors who were before me laid heavy burdens upon the people
Nehemiah5:16 I also held to the work on this wall
Nehemiah5:17 Moreover there were at my table a hundred and fifty men
Nehemiah5:18 Now that which was prepared for one day was one ox and six choice sheep; fowls likewise were prepared for me
Nehemiah5:19 Remember for my good
Nehemiah6:1 Now when it was reported to Sanballat and Tobiah and to Geshem the Arab and to the rest of our enemies that I had built the wall and that there was no breach left in it (although up to that time I had not set up the doors in the gates)
Nehemiah6:2 Sanballat and Geshem sent to me
Nehemiah6:3 And I sent messengers to them
Nehemiah6:4 And they sent to me four times in this way and I answered them in the same manner.
Nehemiah6:5 In the same way Sanballat for the fifth time sent his servant to me with an open letter in his hand.
Nehemiah6:6 In it was written
Nehemiah6:7 And you have also set up prophets to proclaim concerning you in Jerusalem
Nehemiah6:8 Then I sent to him
Nehemiah6:9 For they all wanted to frighten us
Nehemiah6:10 Now when I went into the house of Shemaiah the son of Delaiah
Nehemiah6:11 But I said
Nehemiah6:12 And I understood
Nehemiah6:13 For this purpose he was hired
Nehemiah6:14 Remember Tobiah and Sanballat

Nehemiah6:16 And when all our enemies heard of it
Nehemiah6:17 Moreover in those days the nobles of Judah sent many letters to Tobiah
Nehemiah6:18 For many in Judah were bound by oath to him
Nehemiah6:19 Also they spoke of his good deeds in my presence
Nehemiah7:1 Now when the wall had been built and I had set up the doors
Nehemiah7:2 I gave my brother Hanani and Hananiah the governor of the castle charge over Jerusalem
Nehemiah7:3 And I said to them
Nehemiah7:4 The city was wide and large
Nehemiah7:5 Then God put it into my mind to assemble the nobles and the officials and the people to be enrolled by genealogy. And I found the book of the genealogy of those who came up at the first
Nehemiah7:6 These were the people of the province who came up out of the captivity of those exiles whom Nebuchadnezzar the king of Babylon had carried into exile; they returned to Jerusalem and Judah

Nehemiah8:1 And all the people gathered as one man into the square before the Water Gate; and they told Ezra the scribe to bring the book of the law of Moses which the Lord had given to Israel.
Nehemiah8:2 And Ezra the priest brought the law before the assembly
Nehemiah8:3 And he read from it facing the square before the Water Gate from early morning until midday
Nehemiah8:4 And Ezra the scribe stood on a wooden pulpit which they had made for the purpose; and beside him stood Mattithiah
Nehemiah8:5 And Ezra opened the book in the sight of all the people
Nehemiah8:6 And Ezra blessed the Lord
Nehemiah8:7 Also Jeshua
Nehemiah8:8 And they read from the book
Nehemiah8:9 And Nehemiah
Nehemiah8:10 Then he said to them
Nehemiah8:11 So the Levites stilled all the people
Nehemiah8:12 And all the people went their way to eat and drink and to send portions and to make great rejoicing
Nehemiah8:13 On the second day the heads of fathers' houses of all the people
Nehemiah8:14 And they found it written in the law that the Lord had commanded by Moses that the people of Israel should dwell in booths during the feast of the seventh month
Nehemiah8:15 and that they should publish and proclaim in all their towns and in Jerusalem
Nehemiah8:16 So the people went out and brought them and made booths for themselves
Nehemiah8:17 And all the assembly of those who had returned from the captivity made booths and dwelt in the booths; for from the days of Jeshua the son of Nun to that day the people of Israel had not done so. And there was very great rejoicing.

Nehemiah9:1 Now on the twenty-fourth day of this month the people of Israel were assembled with fasting and in sackcloth
Nehemiah9:2 And the Israelites separated themselves from all foreigners
Nehemiah9:3 And they stood up in their place and read from the book of the law of the Lord their God for a fourth of the day; for another fourth of it they made confession and worshiped the Lord their God.
Nehemiah9:4 Upon the stairs of the Levites stood Jeshua
Nehemiah9:5 Then the Levites
Nehemiah9:6 And Ezra said: “Thou art the Lord
Nehemiah9:7 Thou art the Lord
Nehemiah9:8 and thou didst find his heart faithful before thee
Nehemiah9:9 “And thou didst see the affliction of our fathers in Egypt and hear their cry at the Red Sea
Nehemiah9:10 and didst perform signs and wonders against Pharaoh and all his servants and all the people of his land
Nehemiah9:11 And thou didst divide the sea before them
Nehemiah9:12 By a pillar of cloud thou didst lead them in the day
Nehemiah9:13 Thou didst come down upon Mount Sinai
Nehemiah9:14 and thou didst make known to them thy holy sabbath and command them commandments and statutes and a law by Moses thy servant.
Nehemiah9:15 Thou didst give them bread from heaven for their hunger and bring forth water for them from the rock for their thirst
Nehemiah9:16 “But they and our fathers acted presumptuously and stiffened their neck and did not obey thy commandments;
Nehemiah9:17 they refused to obey
Nehemiah9:18 Even when they had made for themselves a molten calf and said
Nehemiah9:19 thou in thy great mercies didst not forsake them in the wilderness; the pillar of cloud which led them in the way did not depart from them by day
Nehemiah9:20 Thou gavest thy good Spirit to instruct them
Nehemiah9:21 Forty years didst thou sustain them in the wilderness
Nehemiah9:22 And thou didst give them kingdoms and peoples
Nehemiah9:23 Thou didst multiply their descendants as the stars of heaven
Nehemiah9:24 So the descendants went in and possessed the land
Nehemiah9:25 And they captured fortified cities and a rich land
Nehemiah9:26 “Nevertheless they were disobedient and rebelled against thee and cast thy law behind their back and killed thy prophets
Nehemiah9:27 Therefore thou didst give them into the hand of their enemies
Nehemiah9:28 But after they had rest they did evil again before thee
Nehemiah9:29 And thou didst warn them in order to turn them back to thy law. Yet they acted presumptuously and did not obey thy commandments
Nehemiah9:30 Many years thou didst bear with them
Nehemiah9:31 Nevertheless in thy great mercies thou didst not make an end of them or forsake them; for thou art a gracious and merciful God.
Nehemiah9:32 “Now therefore
Nehemiah9:33 Yet thou hast been just in all that has come upon us
Nehemiah9:34 our kings
Nehemiah9:35 They did not serve thee in their kingdom
Nehemiah9:36 Behold
Nehemiah9:37 And its rich yield goes to the kings whom thou hast set over us because of our sins; they have power also over our bodies and over our cattle at their pleasure
Nehemiah9:38 Because of all this we make a firm covenant and write it

Esther1:1 In the days of Ahasu-erus
Esther1:2 in those days when King Ahasu-erus sat on his royal throne in Susa the capital
Esther1:3 in the third year of his reign he gave a banquet for all his princes and servants
Esther1:4 while he showed the riches of his royal glory and the splendor and pomp of his majesty for many days
Esther1:5 And when these days were completed
Esther1:6 There were white cotton curtains and blue hangings caught up with cords of fine linen and purple to silver rings and marble pillars
Esther1:7 Drinks were served in golden goblets
Esther1:8 And drinking was according to the law
Esther1:9 Queen Vashti also gave a banquet for the women in the palace which belonged to King Ahasu-erus.
Esther1:10 On the seventh day
Esther1:11 to bring Queen Vashti before the king with her royal crown
Esther1:12 But Queen Vashti refused to come at the king's command conveyed by the eunuchs. At this the king was enraged
Esther1:13 Then the king said to the wise men who knew the times—for this was the king's procedure toward all who were versed in law and judgment
Esther1:14 the men next to him being Carshena
Esther1:15 “According to the law
Esther1:16 Then Memucan said in presence of the king and the princes
Esther1:17 For this deed of the queen will be made known to all women
Esther1:18 This very day the ladies of Persia and Media who have heard of the queen's behavior will be telling it to all the king's princes
Esther1:19 If it please the king
Esther1:20 So when the decree made by the king is proclaimed throughout all his kingdom
Esther1:21 This advice pleased the king and the princes
Esther1:22 he sent letters to all the royal provinces
Esther2:1 After these things
Esther2:2 Then the king's servants who attended him said
Esther2:3 And let the king appoint officers in all the provinces of his kingdom to gather all the beautiful young virgins to the harem in Susa the capital
Esther2:4 And let the maiden who pleases the king be queen instead of Vashti.” This pleased the king
Esther2:5 Now there was a Jew in Susa the capital whose name was Mordecai
Esther2:6 who had been carried away from Jerusalem among the captives carried away with Jeconiah king of Judah
Esther2:7 He had brought up Hadassah
Esther2:8 So when the king's order and his edict were proclaimed
Esther2:9 And the maiden pleased him and won his favor; and he quickly provided her with her ointments and her portion of food
Esther2:10 Esther had not made known her people or kindred
Esther2:11 And every day Mordecai walked in front of the court of the harem
Esther2:12 Now when the turn came for each maiden to go in to King Ahasu-erus
Esther2:13 when the maiden went in to the king in this way she was given whatever she desired to take with her from the harem to the king's palace.
Esther2:14 In the evening she went
Esther2:15 When the turn came for Esther the daughter of Abihail the uncle of Mordecai
Esther2:16 And when Esther was taken to King Ahasu-erus into his royal palace in the tenth month
Esther2:17 the king loved Esther more than all the women
Esther2:18 Then the king gave a great banquet to all his princes and servants; it was Esther's banquet. He also granted a remission of taxes to the provinces
Esther2:19 When the virgins were gathered together the second time
Esther2:20 Now Esther had not made known her kindred or her people

Esther2:22 And this came to the knowledge of Mordecai

Esther3:1 After these things King Ahasu-erus promoted Haman the Agagite
Esther3:2 And all the king's servants who were at the king's gate bowed down and did obeisance to Haman; for the king had so commanded concerning him. But Mordecai did not bow down or do obeisance.
Esther3:3 Then the king's servants who were at the king's gate said to Mordecai
Esther3:4 And when they spoke to him day after day and he would not listen to them
Esther3:5 And when Haman saw that Mordecai did not bow down or do obeisance to him

Esther9:1 Now in the twelfth month
Esther9:2 the Jews gathered in their cities throughout all the provinces of King Ahasu-erus to lay hands on such as sought their hurt. And no one could make a stand against them
Esther9:3 All the princes of the provinces and the satraps and the governors and the royal officials also helped the Jews
Esther9:4 For Mordecai was great in the king's house

Esther9:13 And Esther said
Esther9:14 So the king commanded this to be done; a decree was issued in Susa
Esther9:15 The Jews who were in Susa gathered also on the fourteenth day of the month of Adar and they slew three hundred men in Susa; but they laid no hands on the plunder.
Esther9:16 Now the other Jews who were in the king's provinces also gathered to defend their lives
Esther9:17 This was on the thirteenth day of the month of Adar
Esther9:18 But the Jews who were in Susa gathered on the thirteenth day and on the fourteenth
Esther9:19 Therefore the Jews of the villages
Esther9:20 And Mordecai recorded these things
Esther9:21 enjoining them that they should keep the fourteenth day of the month Adar and also the fifteenth day of the same
Esther9:22 as the days on which the Jews got relief from their enemies
Esther9:23 So the Jews undertook to do as they had begun
Esther9:24 For Haman the Agagite
Esther9:25 but when Esther came before the king
Esther9:26 Therefore they called these days Purim
Esther9:27 the Jews ordained and took it upon themselves and their descendants and all who joined them
Esther9:28 that these days should be remembered and kept throughout every generation
Esther9:29 Then Queen Esther
Esther9:30 Letters were sent to all the Jews
Esther9:31 that these days of Purim should be observed at their appointed seasons
Esther9:32 The command of Queen Esther fixed these practices of Purim

Esther10:3 For Mordecai the Jew was next in rank to King Ahasu-erus
Job1:1 There was a man in the land of Uz
Job1:2 There were born to him seven sons and three daughters.
Job1:3 He had seven thousand sheep
Job1:4 His sons used to go and hold a feast in the house of each on his day; and they would send and invite their three sisters to eat and drink with them.
Job1:5 And when the days of the feast had run their course
Job1:6 Now there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the Lord

Job1:12 And the Lord said to Satan

Job3:1 After this Job opened his mouth and cursed the day of his birth.
Job3:2 And Job said:
Job3:3 “Let the day perish wherein I was born
Job3:4 Let that day be darkness! May God above not seek it
Job3:5 Let gloom and deep darkness claim it. Let clouds dwell upon it; let the blackness of the day terrify it.
Job3:6 That night—let thick darkness seize it! let it not rejoice among the days of the year
Job3:7 Yea
Job3:8 Let those curse it who curse the day
Job3:9 Let the stars of its dawn be dark; let it hope for light
Job3:10 because it did not shut the doors of my mother's womb
Job3:11 “Why did I not die at birth
Job3:12 Why did the knees receive me? Or why the breasts
Job3:13 For then I should have lain down and been quiet; I should have slept; then I should have been at rest

Job3:15 or with princes who had gold
Job3:16 Or why was I not as a hidden untimely birth

Job3:21 who long for death

Job3:23 Why is light given to a man whose way is hid
Job3:24 For my sighing comes as my bread
Job3:25 For the thing that I fear comes upon me

Job4:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered:

Job5:1 “Call now; is there any one who will answer you? To which of the holy ones will you turn?
Job5:2 Surely vexation kills the fool
Job5:3 I have seen the fool taking root
Job5:4 His sons are far from safety
Job5:5 His harvest the hungry eat
Job5:6 For affliction does not come from the dust
Job5:7 but man is born to trouble as the sparks fly upward.
Job5:8 “As for me
Job5:9 who does great things and unsearchable
Job5:10 he gives rain upon the earth and sends waters upon the fields;
Job5:11 he sets on high those who are lowly
Job5:12 He frustrates the devices of the crafty
Job5:13 He takes the wise in their own craftiness; and the schemes of the wily are brought to a quick end.
Job5:14 They meet with darkness in the daytime
Job5:15 But he saves the fatherless from their mouth
Job5:16 So the poor have hope
Job5:17 “Behold
Job5:18 For he wounds
Job5:19 He will deliver you from six troubles; in seven there shall no evil touch you.
Job5:20 In famine he will redeem you from death
Job5:21 You shall be hid from the scourge of the tongue
Job5:22 At destruction and famine you shall laugh
Job5:23 For you shall be in league with the stones of the field
Job5:24 You shall know that your tent is safe
Job5:25 You shall know also that your descendants shall be many
Job5:26 You shall come to your grave in ripe old age

Job6:1 Then Job answered:
Job6:2 “O that my vexation were weighed
Job6:3 For then it would be heavier than the sand of the sea; therefore my words have been rash.
Job6:4 For the arrows of the Almighty are in me; my spirit drinks their poison; the terrors of God are arrayed against me.
Job6:5 Does the wild ass bray when he has grass
Job6:6 Can that which is tasteless be eaten without salt
Job6:7 My appetite refuses to touch them; they are as food that is loathsome to me.
Job6:8 “O that I might have my request
Job6:9 that it would please God to crush me
Job6:10 This would be my consolation; I would even exult in pain unsparing; for I have not denied the words of the Holy One.

Job6:12 Is my strength the strength of stones
Job6:13 In truth I have no help in me

Job6:16 which are dark with ice

Job6:19 The caravans of Tema look

Job20:12 “Though wickedness is sweet in his mouth

Job20:14 yet his food is turned in his stomach; it is the gall of asps within him.
Job20:15 He swallows down riches and vomits them up again; God casts them out of his belly.
Job20:16 He will suck the poison of asps; the tongue of a viper will kill him.
Job20:17 He will not look upon the rivers
Job20:18 He will give back the fruit of his toil
Job20:19 For he has crushed and abandoned the poor
Job20:20 “Because his greed knew no rest
Job20:21 There was nothing left after he had eaten; therefore his prosperity will not endure.
Job20:22 In the fulness of his sufficiency he will be in straits; all the force of misery will come upon him.
Job20:23 To fill his belly to the full God will send his fierce anger into him
Job20:24 He will flee from an iron weapon; a bronze arrow will strike him through.
Job20:25 It is drawn forth and comes out of his body
Job20:26 Utter darkness is laid up for his treasures; a fire not blown upon will devour him; what is left in his tent will be consumed.
Job20:27 The heavens will reveal his iniquity
Job20:28 The possessions of his house will be carried away
Job20:29 This is the wicked man's portion from God

Job21:3 Bear with me
Job21:4 As for me
Job21:5 Look at me
Job21:6 When I think of it I am dismayed
Job21:7 Why do the wicked live
Job21:8 Their children are established in their presence
Job21:9 Their houses are safe from fear
Job21:10 Their bull breeds without fail; their cow calves
Job21:11 They send forth their little ones like a flock
Job21:12 They sing to the tambourine and the lyre
Job21:13 They spend their days in prosperity
Job21:14 They say to God
Job21:15 What is the Almighty
Job21:16 Behold
Job21:17 “How often is it that the lamp of the wicked is put out? That their calamity comes upon them? That God distributes pains in his anger?
Job21:18 That they are like straw before the wind
Job21:19 You say
Job21:20 Let their own eyes see their destruction
Job21:21 For what do they care for their houses after them
Job21:22 Will any teach God knowledge
Job21:23 One dies in full prosperity
Job21:24 his body full of fat and the marrow of his bones moist.
Job21:25 Another dies in bitterness of soul
Job21:26 They lie down alike in the dust
Job21:27 “Behold
Job21:28 For you say
Job21:29 Have you not asked those who travel the roads
Job21:30 that the wicked man is spared in the day of calamity
Job21:31 Who declares his way to his face
Job21:32 When he is borne to the grave
Job21:33 The clods of the valley are sweet to him; all men follow after him
Job21:34 How then will you comfort me with empty nothings? There is nothing left of your answers but falsehood.”
Job22:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered:
Job22:2 “Can a man be profitable to God? Surely he who is wise is profitable to himself.
Job22:3 Is it any pleasure to the Almighty if you are righteous
Job22:4 Is it for your fear of him that he reproves you
Job22:5 Is not your wickedness great? There is no end to your iniquities.
Job22:6 For you have exacted pledges of your brothers for nothing
Job22:7 You have given no water to the weary to drink
Job22:8 The man with power possessed the land
Job22:9 You have sent widows away empty
Job22:10 Therefore snares are round about you
Job22:11 your light is darkened
Job22:12 “Is not God high in the heavens? See the highest stars
Job22:13 Therefore you say
Job22:14 Thick clouds enwrap him
Job22:15 Will you keep to the old way which wicked men have trod?
Job22:16 They were snatched away before their time; their foundation was washed away.
Job22:17 They said to God
Job22:18 Yet he filled their houses with good things— but the counsel of the wicked is far from me.
Job22:19 The righteous see it and are glad; the innocent laugh them to scorn
Job22:20 saying
Job22:21 “Agree with God
Job22:22 Receive instruction from his mouth
Job22:23 If you return to the Almighty and humble yourself
Job22:24 if you lay gold in the dust
Job22:25 and if the Almighty is your gold
Job22:26 then you will delight yourself in the Almighty
Job22:27 You will make your prayer to him
Job22:28 You will decide on a matter
Job22:29 For God abases the proud
Job22:30 He delivers the innocent man; you will be delivered through the cleanness of your hands.”
Job23:1 Then Job answered:
Job23:2 “Today also my complaint is bitter
Job23:3 Oh
Job23:4 I would lay my case before him and fill my mouth with arguments.
Job23:5 I would learn what he would answer me
Job23:6 Would he contend with me in the greatness of his power? No; he would give heed to me.
Job23:7 There an upright man could reason with him
Job23:8 “Behold
Job23:9 on the left hand I seek him
Job23:10 But he knows the way that I take; when he has tried me
Job23:11 My foot has held fast to his steps; I have kept his way and have not turned aside.
Job23:12 I have not departed from the commandment of his lips; I have treasured in my bosom the words of his mouth.
Job23:13 But he is unchangeable and who can turn him? What he desires
Job23:14 For he will complete what he appoints for me; and many such things are in his mind.
Job23:15 Therefore I am terrified at his presence; when I consider
Job23:16 God has made my heart faint; the Almighty has terrified me;
Job23:17 for I am hemmed in by darkness

Job24:2 Men remove landmarks; they seize flocks and pasture them.
Job24:3 They drive away the ass of the fatherless; they take the widow's ox for a pledge.
Job24:4 They thrust the poor off the road; the poor of the earth all hide themselves.
Job24:5 Behold

Job24:13 “There are those who rebel against the light
Job24:14 The murderer rises in the dark
Job24:15 The eye of the adulterer also waits for the twilight
Job24:16 In the dark they dig through houses; by day they shut themselves up; they do not know the light.
Job24:17 For deep darkness is morning to all of them; for they are friends with the terrors of deep darkness.
Job24:18 “You say
Job24:19 Drought and heat snatch away the snow waters; so does Sheol those who have sinned.

Job26:1 Then Job answered:
Job26:2 “How you have helped him who has no power! How you have saved the arm that has no strength!
Job26:3 How you have counseled him who has no wisdom
Job26:4 With whose help have you uttered words

Job26:6 Sheol is naked before God

Job26:11 The pillars of heaven tremble
Job26:12 By his power he stilled the sea; by his understanding he smote Rahab.
Job26:13 By his wind the heavens were made fair; his hand pierced the fleeing serpent.

Job27:1 And Job again took up his discourse
Job27:2 “As God lives
Job27:3 as long as my breath is in me
Job27:4 my lips will not speak falsehood
Job27:5 Far be it from me to say that you are right; till I die I will not put away my integrity from me.
Job27:6 I hold fast my righteousness
Job27:7 “Let my enemy be as the wicked
Job27:8 For what is the hope of the godless when God cuts him off
Job27:9 Will God hear his cry
Job27:10 Will he take delight in the Almighty? Will he call upon God at all times?
Job27:11 I will teach you concerning the hand of God; what is with the Almighty I will not conceal.
Job27:12 Behold
Job27:13 “This is the portion of a wicked man with God
Job27:14 If his children are multiplied
Job27:15 Those who survive him the pestilence buries
Job27:16 Though he heap up silver like dust
Job27:17 he may pile it up
Job27:18 The house which he builds is like a spider's web
Job27:19 He goes to bed rich
Job27:20 Terrors overtake him like a flood; in the night a whirlwind carries him off.
Job27:21 The east wind lifts him up and he is gone; it sweeps him out of his place.
Job27:22 It hurls at him without pity; he flees from its power in headlong flight.
Job27:23 It claps its hands at him
Job28:1 “Surely there is a mine for silver
Job28:2 Iron is taken out of the earth
Job28:3 Men put an end to darkness
Job28:4 They open shafts in a valley away from where men live; they are forgotten by travelers
Job28:5 As for the earth
Job28:6 Its stones are the place of sapphires
Job28:7 “That path no bird of prey knows
Job28:8 The proud beasts have not trodden it; the lion has not passed over it.
Job28:9 “Man puts his hand to the flinty rock
Job28:10 He cuts out channels in the rocks
Job28:11 He binds up the streams so that they do not trickle
Job28:12 “But where shall wisdom be found? And where is the place of understanding?
Job28:13 Man does not know the way to it
Job28:14 The deep says
Job28:15 It cannot be gotten for gold
Job28:16 It cannot be valued in the gold of Ophir
Job28:17 Gold and glass cannot equal it
Job28:18 No mention shall be made of coral or of crystal; the price of wisdom is above pearls.
Job28:19 The topaz of Ethiopia cannot compare with it
Job28:20 “Whence then comes wisdom? And where is the place of understanding?
Job28:21 It is hid from the eyes of all living
Job28:22 Abaddon and Death say
Job28:23 “God understands the way to it
Job28:24 For he looks to the ends of the earth

Job28:26 when he made a decree for the rain
Job28:27 then he saw it and declared it; he established it

Job29:2 “Oh

Job29:4 as I was in my autumn days
Job29:5 when the Almighty was yet with me

Job29:20 my glory fresh with me

Job30:5 They are driven out from among men; they shout after them as after a thief.
Job30:6 In the gullies of the torrents they must dwell
Job30:7 Among the bushes they bray; under the nettles they huddle together.

Job30:9 “And now I have become their song

Job30:16 “And now my soul is poured out within me; days of affliction have taken hold of me.

Job30:20 I cry to thee and thou dost not answer me; I stand
Job30:21 Thou hast turned cruel to me; with the might of thy hand thou dost persecute me.

Job30:23 Yea

Job31:11 For that would be a heinous crime; that would be an iniquity to be punished by the judges;
Job31:12 for that would be a fire which consumes unto Abaddon
Job31:13 “If I have rejected the cause of my manservant or my maidservant
Job31:14 what then shall I do when God rises up? When he makes inquiry
Job31:15 Did not he who made me in the womb make him? And did not one fashion us in the womb?
Job31:16 “If I have withheld anything that the poor desired
Job31:17 or have eaten my morsel alone
Job31:18 (for from his youth I reared him as a father

Job31:20 if his loins have not blessed me

Job31:23 For I was in terror of calamity from God
Job31:24 “If I have made gold my trust
Job31:25 if I have rejoiced because my wealth was great
Job31:26 if I have looked at the sun when it shone
Job31:27 and my heart has been secretly enticed
Job31:28 this also would be an iniquity to be punished by the judges

Job31:30 (I have not let my mouth sin by asking for his life with a curse);
Job31:31 if the men of my tent have not said
Job31:32 (the sojourner has not lodged in the street; I have opened my doors to the wayfarer);

Job31:34 because I stood in great fear of the multitude
Job31:35 Oh

Job31:39 if I have eaten its yield without payment

Job38:1 Then the Lord answered Job out of the whirlwind:
Job38:2 “Who is this that darkens counsel by words without knowledge?
Job38:3 Gird up your loins like a man
Job38:4 “Where were you when I laid the foundation of the earth? Tell me
Job38:5 Who determined its measurements—surely you know! Or who stretched the line upon it?
Job38:6 On what were its bases sunk
Job38:7 when the morning stars sang together
Job38:8 “Or who shut in the sea with doors
Job38:9 when I made clouds its garment
Job38:10 and prescribed bounds for it
Job38:11 and said
Job38:12 “Have you commanded the morning since your days began
Job38:13 that it might take hold of the skirts of the earth
Job38:14 It is changed like clay under the seal
Job38:15 From the wicked their light is withheld
Job38:16 “Have you entered into the springs of the sea
Job38:17 Have the gates of death been revealed to you
Job38:18 Have you comprehended the expanse of the earth? Declare
Job38:19 “Where is the way to the dwelling of light
Job38:20 that you may take it to its territory and that you may discern the paths to its home?
Job38:21 You know
Job38:22 “Have you entered the storehouses of the snow
Job38:23 which I have reserved for the time of trouble
Job38:24 What is the way to the place where the light is distributed
Job38:25 “Who has cleft a channel for the torents of rain
Job38:26 to bring rain on a land where no man is
Job38:27 to satisfy the waste and desolate land
Job38:28 “Has the rain a father
Job38:29 From whose womb did the ice come forth
Job38:30 The waters become hard like stone
Job38:31 “Can you bind the chains of the Pleiades
Job38:32 Can you lead forth the Mazzaroth in their season
Job38:33 Do you know the ordinances of the heavens? Can you establish their rule on the earth?
Job38:34 “Can you lift up your voice to the clouds
Job38:35 Can you send forth lightnings
Job38:36 Who has put wisdom in the clouds
Job38:37 Who can number the clouds by wisdom? Or who can tilt the waterskins of the heavens

Job38:39 “Can you hunt the prey for the lion
Job38:40 when they crouch in their dens
Job38:41 Who provides for the raven its prey
Job39:1 “Do you know when the mountain goats bring forth? Do you observe the calving of the hinds?
Job39:2 Can you number the months that they fulfil
Job39:3 when they crouch
Job39:4 Their young ones become strong
Job39:5 “Who has let the wild ass go free? Who has loosed the bonds of the swift ass
Job39:6 to whom I have given the steppe for his home
Job39:7 He scorns the tumult of the city; he hears not the shouts of the driver.
Job39:8 He ranges the mountains as his pasture
Job39:9 “Is the wild ox willing to serve you? Will he spend the night at your crib?

Job39:11 Will you depend on him because his strength is great
Job39:12 Do you have faith in him that he will return

Job39:14 For she leaves her eggs to the earth
Job39:15 forgetting that a foot may crush them

Job39:17 because God has made her forget wisdom
Job39:18 When she rouses herself to flee

Job39:22 He laughs at fear
Job39:23 Upon him rattle the quiver
Job39:24 With fierceness and rage he swallows the ground; he cannot stand still at the sound of the trumpet.

Job39:29 Thence he spies out the prey; his eyes behold it afar off.

Job40:8 Will you even put me in the wrong? Will you condemn me that you may be justified?
Job40:9 Have you an arm like God
Job40:10 “Deck yourself with majesty and dignity; clothe yourself with glory and splendor.
Job40:11 Pour forth the overflowings of your anger
Job40:12 Look on every one that is proud
Job40:13 Hide them all in the dust together; bind their faces in the world below.
Job40:14 Then will I also acknowledge to you
Job40:15 “Behold
Job40:16 Behold
Job40:17 He makes his tail stiff like a cedar; the sinews of his thighs are knit together.
Job40:18 His bones are tubes of bronze
Job40:19 “He is the first of the works of God; let him who made him bring near his sword!
Job40:20 For the mountains yield food for him where all the wild beasts play.
Job40:21 Under the lotus plants he lies
Job40:22 For his shade the lotus trees cover him; the willows of the brook surround him.
Job40:23 Behold
Job40:24 Can one take him with hooks
Job41:1 “Can you draw out Leviathan with a fishhook
Job41:2 Can you put a rope in his nose
Job41:3 Will he make many supplications to you? Will he speak to you soft words?
Job41:4 Will he make a covenant with you to take him for your servant for ever?
Job41:5 Will you play with him as with a bird
Job41:6 Will traders bargain over him? Will they divide him up among the merchants?
Job41:7 Can you fill his skin with harpoons
Job41:8 Lay hands on him; think of the battle; you will not do it again!
Job41:9 Behold
Job41:10 No one is so fierce that he dares to stir him up. Who then is he that can stand before me?
Job41:11 Who has given to me
Job41:12 “I will not keep silence concerning his limbs
Job41:13 Who can strip off his outer garment? Who can penetrate his double coat of mail?
Job41:14 Who can open the doors of his face? Round about his teeth is terror.
Job41:15 His back is made of rows of shields
Job41:16 One is so near to another that no air can come between them.
Job41:17 They are joined one to another; they clasp each other and cannot be separated.
Job41:18 His sneezings flash forth light
Job41:19 Out of his mouth go flaming torches; sparks of fire leap forth.
Job41:20 Out of his nostrils comes forth smoke
Job41:21 His breath kindles coals
Job41:22 In his neck abides strength
Job41:23 The folds of his flesh cleave together
Job41:24 His heart is hard as a stone
Job41:25 When he raises himself up the mighty are afraid; at the crashing they are beside themselves.
Job41:26 Though the sword reaches him
Job41:27 He counts iron as straw
Job41:28 The arrow cannot make him flee; for him slingstones are turned to stubble.
Job41:29 Clubs are counted as stubble; he laughs at the rattle of javelins.
Job41:30 His underparts are like sharp potsherds; he spreads himself like a threshing sledge on the mire.
Job41:31 He makes the deep boil like a pot; he makes the sea like a pot of ointment.
Job41:32 Behind him he leaves a shining wake; one would think the deep to be hoary.
Job41:33 Upon earth there is not his like
Job41:34 He beholds everything that is high; he is king over all the sons of pride.”
Job42:1 Then Job answered the Lord:
Job42:2 “I know that thou canst do all things
Job42:3 ‘Who is this that hides counsel without knowledge?’ Therefore I have uttered what I did not understand
Job42:4 ‘Hear
Job42:5 I had heard of thee by the hearing of the ear
Job42:6 therefore I despise myself
Job42:7 After the Lord had spoken these words to Job
Job42:8 Now therefore take seven bulls and seven rams
Job42:9 So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went and did what the Lord had told them; and the Lord accepted Job's prayer.
Job42:10 And the Lord restored the fortunes of Job
Job42:11 Then came to him all his brothers and sisters and all who had known him before
Job42:12 And the Lord blessed the latter days of Job more than his beginning; and he had fourteen thousand sheep
Job42:13 He had also seven sons and three daughters.
Job42:14 And he called the name of the first Jemimah; and the name of the second Keziah; and the name of the third Keren-happuch.
Job42:15 And in all the land there were no women so fair as Job's daughters; and their father gave them inheritance among their brothers.
Job42:16 And after this Job lived a hundred and forty years
Job42:17 And Job died

Psalms72:20 The prayers of David
Psalms73:1 Truly God is good to the upright
Psalms73:2 But as for me
Psalms73:3 For I was envious of the arrogant
Psalms73:4 For they have no pangs; their bodies are sound and sleek.

Psalms73:6 Therefore pride is their necklace; violence covers them as a garment.
Psalms73:7 Their eyes swell out with fatness
Psalms73:8 They scoff and speak with malice; loftily they threaten oppression.

Psalms73:19 How they are destroyed in a moment
Psalms73:20 They are like a dream when one awakes
Psalms73:21 When my soul was embittered
Psalms73:22 I was stupid and ignorant
Psalms73:23 Nevertheless I am continually with thee; thou dost hold my right hand.

Psalms73:25 Whom have I in heaven but thee? And there is nothing upon earth that I desire besides thee.

Psalms73:27 For lo
Psalms73:28 But for me it is good to be near God; I have made the Lord God my refuge

Psalms74:6 And then all its carved wood they broke down with hatchets and hammers.
Psalms74:7 They set thy sanctuary on fire; to the ground they desecrated the dwelling place of thy name.
Psalms74:8 They said to themselves

Psalms77:1 I cry aloud to God

Psalms77:5 I consider the days of old

Psalms78:1 Give ear

Psalms78:6 that the next generation might know them
Psalms78:7 so that they should set their hope in God
Psalms78:8 and that they should not be like their fathers
Psalms78:9 The Ephraimites
Psalms78:10 They did not keep God's covenant
Psalms78:11 They forgot what he had done
Psalms78:12 In the sight of their fathers he wrought marvels in the land of Egypt
Psalms78:13 He divided the sea and let them pass through it
Psalms78:14 In the daytime he led them with a cloud
Psalms78:15 He cleft rocks in the wilderness
Psalms78:16 He made streams come out of the rock
Psalms78:17 Yet they sinned still more against him
Psalms78:18 They tested God in their heart by demanding the food they craved.
Psalms78:19 They spoke against God
Psalms78:20 He smote the rock so that water gushed out and streams overflowed. Can he also give bread
Psalms78:21 Therefore
Psalms78:22 because they had no faith in God
Psalms78:23 Yet he commanded the skies above
Psalms78:24 and he rained down upon them manna to eat
Psalms78:25 Man ate of the bread of the angels; he sent them food in abundance.
Psalms78:26 He caused the east wind to blow in the heavens
Psalms78:27 he rained flesh upon them like dust
Psalms78:28 he let them fall in the midst of their camp
Psalms78:29 And they ate and were well filled
Psalms78:30 But before they had sated their craving
Psalms78:31 the anger of God rose against them and he slew the strongest of them
Psalms78:32 In spite of all this they still sinned; despite his wonders they did not believe.
Psalms78:33 So he made their days vanish like a breath
Psalms78:34 When he slew them
Psalms78:35 They remembered that God was their rock
Psalms78:36 But they flattered him with their mouths; they lied to him with their tongues.
Psalms78:37 Their heart was not steadfast toward him; they were not true to his covenant.
Psalms78:38 Yet he
Psalms78:39 He remembered that they were but flesh
Psalms78:40 How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness and grieved him in the desert!
Psalms78:41 They tested him again and again
Psalms78:42 They did not keep in mind his power
Psalms78:43 when he wrought his signs in Egypt
Psalms78:44 He turned their rivers to blood
Psalms78:45 He sent among them swarms of flies
Psalms78:46 He gave their crops to the caterpillar
Psalms78:47 He destroyed their vines with hail
Psalms78:48 He gave over their cattle to the hail
Psalms78:49 He let loose on them his fierce anger
Psalms78:50 He made a path for his anger; he did not spare them from death
Psalms78:51 He smote all the first-born in Egypt
Psalms78:52 Then he led forth his people like sheep
Psalms78:53 He led them in safety
Psalms78:54 And he brought them to his holy land
Psalms78:55 He drove out nations before them; he apportioned them for a possession and settled the tribes of Israel in their tents.
Psalms78:56 Yet they tested and rebelled against the Most High God
Psalms78:57 but turned away and acted treacherously like their fathers; they twisted like a deceitful bow.
Psalms78:58 For they provoked him to anger with their high places; they moved him to jealousy with their graven images.
Psalms78:59 When God heard
Psalms78:60 He forsook his dwelling at Shiloh
Psalms78:61 and delivered his power to captivity
Psalms78:62 He gave his people over to the sword
Psalms78:63 Fire devoured their young men
Psalms78:64 Their priests fell by the sword
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord awoke as from sleep
Psalms78:66 And he put his adversaries to rout; he put them to everlasting shame.
Psalms78:67 He rejected the tent of Joseph
Psalms78:68 but he chose the tribe of Judah
Psalms78:69 He built his sanctuary like the high heavens
Psalms78:70 He chose David his servant
Psalms78:71 from tending the ewes that had young he brought him to be the shepherd of Jacob his people
Psalms78:72 With upright heart he tended them
Psalms79:1 O God

Psalms79:7 For they have devoured Jacob

Proverbs1:4 that prudence may be given to the simple
Proverbs1:5 the wise man also may hear and increase in learning
Proverbs1:6 to understand a proverb and a figure
Proverbs1:7 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge; fools despise wisdom and instruction.
Proverbs1:8 Hear
Proverbs1:9 for they are a fair garland for your head
Proverbs1:10 My son
Proverbs1:11 If they say
Proverbs1:12 like Sheol let us swallow them alive and whole
Proverbs1:13 we shall find all precious goods
Proverbs1:14 throw in your lot among us
Proverbs1:15 my son
Proverbs1:16 for their feet run to evil
Proverbs1:17 For in vain is a net spread in the sight of any bird;
Proverbs1:18 but these men lie in wait for their own blood
Proverbs1:19 Such are the ways of all who get gain by violence; it takes away the life of its possessors.
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom cries aloud in the street; in the markets she raises her voice;
Proverbs1:21 on the top of the walls she cries out; at the entrance of the city gates she speaks:
Proverbs1:22 “How long
Proverbs1:23 Give heed to my reproof; behold
Proverbs1:24 Because I have called and you refused to listen
Proverbs1:25 and you have ignored all my counsel and would have none of my reproof
Proverbs1:26 I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when panic strikes you
Proverbs1:27 when panic strikes you like a storm
Proverbs1:28 Then they will call upon me
Proverbs1:29 Because they hated knowledge and did not choose the fear of the Lord
Proverbs1:30 would have none of my counsel
Proverbs1:31 therefore they shall eat the fruit of their way and be sated with their own devices.
Proverbs1:32 For the simple are killed by their turning away
Proverbs1:33 but he who listens to me will dwell secure and will be at ease
Proverbs2:1 My son
Proverbs2:2 making your ear attentive to wisdom and inclining your heart to understanding;
Proverbs2:3 yes
Proverbs2:4 if you seek it like silver and search for it as for hidden treasures;
Proverbs2:5 then you will understand the fear of the Lord and find the knowledge of God.
Proverbs2:6 For the Lord gives wisdom; from his mouth come knowledge and understanding;
Proverbs2:7 he stores up sound wisdom for the upright; he is a shield to those who walk in integrity
Proverbs2:8 guarding the paths of justice and preserving the way of his saints.
Proverbs2:9 Then you will understand righteousness and justice and equity
Proverbs2:10 for wisdom will come into your heart
Proverbs2:11 discretion will watch over you; understanding will guard you;
Proverbs2:12 delivering you from the way of evil
Proverbs2:13 who forsake the paths of uprightness to walk in the ways of darkness
Proverbs2:14 who rejoice in doing evil and delight in the perverseness of evil;
Proverbs2:15 men whose paths are crooked
Proverbs2:16 You will be saved from the loose woman
Proverbs2:17 who forsakes the companion of her youth and forgets the covenant of her God;
Proverbs2:18 for her house sinks down to death
Proverbs2:19 none who go to her come back nor do they regain the paths of life.
Proverbs2:20 So you will walk in the way of good men and keep to the paths of the righteous.
Proverbs2:21 For the upright will inhabit the land
Proverbs2:22 but the wicked will be cut off from the land
Proverbs3:1 My son

Proverbs3:4 So you will find favor and good repute in the sight of God and man.
Proverbs3:5 Trust in the Lord with all your heart

Proverbs3:10 then your barns will be filled with plenty

Proverbs3:12 for the Lord reproves him whom he loves
Proverbs3:13 Happy is the man who finds wisdom
Proverbs3:14 for the gain from it is better than gain from silver and its profit better than gold.

Proverbs3:16 Long life is in her right hand; in her left hand are riches and honor.
Proverbs3:17 Her ways are ways of pleasantness
Proverbs3:18 She is a tree of life to those who lay hold of her; those who hold her fast are called happy.
Proverbs3:19 The Lord by wisdom founded the earth; by understanding he established the heavens;
Proverbs3:20 by his knowledge the deeps broke forth
Proverbs3:21 My son

Proverbs3:23 Then you will walk on your way securely and your foot will not stumble.
Proverbs3:24 If you sit down
Proverbs3:25 Do not be afraid of sudden panic
Proverbs3:26 for the Lord will be your confidence and will keep your foot from being caught.
Proverbs3:27 Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due
Proverbs3:28 Do not say to your neighbor
Proverbs3:29 Do not plan evil against your neighbor who dwells trustingly beside you.

Proverbs3:32 for the perverse man is an abomination to the Lord

Proverbs3:34 Toward the scorners he is scornful
Proverbs3:35 The wise will inherit honor

Proverbs4:12 When you walk
Proverbs4:13 Keep hold of instruction
Proverbs4:14 Do not enter the path of the wicked
Proverbs4:15 Avoid it; do not go on it; turn away from it and pass on.
Proverbs4:16 For they cannot sleep unless they have done wrong; they are robbed of sleep unless they have made some one stumble.
Proverbs4:17 For they eat the bread of wickedness and drink the wine of violence.
Proverbs4:18 But the path of the righteous is like the light of dawn
Proverbs4:19 The way of the wicked is like deep darkness; they do not know over what they stumble.
Proverbs4:20 My son
Proverbs4:21 Let them not escape from your sight; keep them within your heart.
Proverbs4:22 For they are life to him who finds them
Proverbs4:23 Keep your heart with all vigilance; for from it flow the springs of life.
Proverbs4:24 Put away from you crooked speech
Proverbs4:25 Let your eyes look directly forward
Proverbs4:26 Take heed to the path of your feet
Proverbs4:27 Do not swerve to the right or to the left; turn your foot away from evil.
Proverbs5:1 My son
Proverbs5:2 that you may keep discretion
Proverbs5:3 For the lips of a loose woman drip honey
Proverbs5:4 but in the end she is bitter as wormwood
Proverbs5:5 Her feet go down to death; her steps follow the path to Sheol;
Proverbs5:6 she does not take heed to the path of life; her ways wander
Proverbs5:7 And now
Proverbs5:8 Keep your way far from her
Proverbs5:9 lest you give your honor to others and your years to the merciless;
Proverbs5:10 lest strangers take their fill of your strength
Proverbs5:11 and at the end of your life you groan
Proverbs5:12 and you say
Proverbs5:13 I did not listen to the voice of my teachers or incline my ear to my instructors.

Proverbs5:21 For a man's ways are before the eyes of the Lord
Proverbs5:22 The iniquities of the wicked ensnare him
Proverbs5:23 He dies for lack of discipline

Proverbs6:8 she prepares her food in summer

Proverbs6:13 winks with his eyes
Proverbs6:14 with perverted heart devises evil
Proverbs6:15 therefore calamity will come upon him suddenly; in a moment he will be broken beyond healing.

Proverbs7:3 bind them on your fingers
Proverbs7:4 Say to wisdom
Proverbs7:5 to preserve you from the loose woman
Proverbs7:6 For at the window of my house I have looked out through my lattice
Proverbs7:7 and I have seen among the simple
Proverbs7:8 passing along the street near her corner
Proverbs7:9 in the twilight
Proverbs7:10 And lo
Proverbs7:11 She is loud and wayward
Proverbs7:12 now in the street
Proverbs7:13 She seizes him and kisses him
Proverbs7:14 “I had to offer sacrifices
Proverbs7:15 so now I have come out to meet you
Proverbs7:16 I have decked my couch with coverings
Proverbs7:17 I have perfumed my bed with myrrh
Proverbs7:18 Come
Proverbs7:19 For my husband is not at home; he has gone on a long journey;
Proverbs7:20 he took a bag of money with him; at full moon he will come home.”
Proverbs7:21 With much seductive speech she persuades him; with her smooth talk she compels him.
Proverbs7:22 All at once he follows her
Proverbs7:23 till an arrow pierces its entrails; as a bird rushes into a snare; he does not know that it will cost him his life.
Proverbs7:24 And now
Proverbs7:25 Let not your heart turn aside to her ways
Proverbs7:26 for many a victim has she laid low; yea
Proverbs7:27 Her house is the way to Sheol

Proverbs8:2 On the heights beside the way
Proverbs8:3 beside the gates in front of the town
Proverbs8:4 “To you
Proverbs8:5 O simple ones
Proverbs8:6 Hear
Proverbs8:7 for my mouth will utter truth; wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
Proverbs8:8 All the words of my mouth are righteous; there is nothing twisted or crooked in them.
Proverbs8:9 They are all straight to him who understands and right to those who find knowledge.

Proverbs8:11 for wisdom is better than jewels
Proverbs8:12 I
Proverbs8:13 The fear of the Lord is hatred of evil. Pride and arrogance and the way of evil and perverted speech I hate.
Proverbs8:14 I have counsel and sound wisdom
Proverbs8:15 By me kings reign
Proverbs8:16 by me princes rule
Proverbs8:17 I love those who love me
Proverbs8:18 Riches and honor are with me
Proverbs8:19 My fruit is better than gold
Proverbs8:20 I walk in the way of righteousness
Proverbs8:21 endowing with wealth those who love me
Proverbs8:22 The Lord created me at the beginning of his work
Proverbs8:23 Ages ago I was set up
Proverbs8:24 When there were no depths I was brought forth
Proverbs8:25 Before the mountains had been shaped
Proverbs8:26 before he had made the earth with its fields
Proverbs8:27 When he established the heavens
Proverbs8:28 when he made firm the skies above
Proverbs8:29 when he assigned to the sea its limit
Proverbs8:30 then I was beside him
Proverbs8:31 rejoicing in his inhabited world and delighting in the sons of men.
Proverbs8:32 And now
Proverbs8:33 Hear instruction and be wise
Proverbs8:34 Happy is the man who listens to me
Proverbs8:35 For he who finds me finds life and obtains favor from the Lord;
Proverbs8:36 but he who misses me injures himself; all who hate me love death.”

Ecclesiastes1:1 The words of the Preacher
Ecclesiastes1:2 Vanity of vanities
Ecclesiastes1:3 What does man gain by all the toil at which he toils under the sun?
Ecclesiastes1:4 A generation goes
Ecclesiastes1:5 The sun rises and the sun goes down
Ecclesiastes1:6 The wind blows to the south
Ecclesiastes1:7 All streams run to the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 All things are full of weariness; a man cannot utter it; the eye is not satisfied with seeing
Ecclesiastes1:9 What has been is what will be
Ecclesiastes1:10 Is there a thing of which it is said
Ecclesiastes1:11 There is no remembrance of former things
Ecclesiastes1:12 I the Preacher have been king over Israel in Jerusalem.
Ecclesiastes1:13 And I applied my mind to seek and to search out by wisdom all that is done under heaven; it is an unhappy business that God has given to the sons of men to be busy with.
Ecclesiastes1:14 I have seen everything that is done under the sun; and behold
Ecclesiastes1:15 What is crooked cannot be made straight
Ecclesiastes1:16 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes1:17 And I applied my mind to know wisdom and to know madness and folly. I perceived that this also is but a striving after wind.
Ecclesiastes1:18 For in much wisdom is much vexation
Ecclesiastes2:1 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes2:2 I said of laughter
Ecclesiastes2:3 I searched with my mind how to cheer my body with wine—my mind still guiding me with wisdom—and how to lay hold on folly
Ecclesiastes2:4 I made great works; I built houses and planted vineyards for myself;
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made myself gardens and parks
Ecclesiastes2:6 I made myself pools from which to water the forest of growing trees.
Ecclesiastes2:7 I bought male and female slaves
Ecclesiastes2:8 I also gathered for myself silver and gold and the treasure of kings and provinces; I got singers
Ecclesiastes2:9 So I became great and surpassed all who were before me in Jerusalem; also my wisdom remained with me.
Ecclesiastes2:10 And whatever my eyes desired I did not keep from them; I kept my heart from no pleasure
Ecclesiastes2:11 Then I considered all that my hands had done and the toil I had spent in doing it
Ecclesiastes2:12 So I turned to consider wisdom and madness and folly; for what can the man do who comes after the king? Only what he has already done.
Ecclesiastes2:13 Then I saw that wisdom excels folly as light excels darkness.
Ecclesiastes2:14 The wise man has his eyes in his head
Ecclesiastes2:15 Then I said to myself
Ecclesiastes2:16 For of the wise man as of the fool there is no enduring remembrance
Ecclesiastes2:17 So I hated life
Ecclesiastes2:18 I hated all my toil in which I had toiled under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:19 and who knows whether he will be a wise man or a fool? Yet he will be master of all for which I toiled and used my wisdom under the sun. This also is vanity.
Ecclesiastes2:20 So I turned about and gave my heart up to despair over all the toil of my labors under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:21 because sometimes a man who has toiled with wisdom and knowledge and skill must leave all to be enjoyed by a man who did not toil for it. This also is vanity and a great evil.
Ecclesiastes2:22 What has a man from all the toil and strain with which he toils beneath the sun?
Ecclesiastes2:23 For all his days are full of pain
Ecclesiastes2:24 There is nothing better for a man than that he should eat and drink and find enjoyment in his toil. This also
Ecclesiastes2:25 for apart from him who can eat or who can have enjoyment?
Ecclesiastes2:26 For to the man who pleases him God gives wisdom and knowledge and joy; but to the sinner he gives the work of gathering and heaping
Ecclesiastes3:1 For everything there is a season
Ecclesiastes3:2 a time to be born
Ecclesiastes3:3 a time to kill
Ecclesiastes3:4 a time to weep
Ecclesiastes3:5 a time to cast away stones
Ecclesiastes3:6 a time to seek
Ecclesiastes3:7 a time to rend
Ecclesiastes3:8 a time to love
Ecclesiastes3:9 What gain has the worker from his toil?
Ecclesiastes3:10 I have seen the business that God has given to the sons of men to be busy with.
Ecclesiastes3:11 He has made everything beautiful in its time; also he has put eternity into man's mind
Ecclesiastes3:12 I know that there is nothing better for them than to be happy and enjoy themselves as long as they live;
Ecclesiastes3:13 also that it is God's gift to man that every one should eat and drink and take pleasure in all his toil.
Ecclesiastes3:14 I know that whatever God does endures for ever; nothing can be added to it
Ecclesiastes3:15 That which is
Ecclesiastes3:16 Moreover I saw under the sun that in the place of justice
Ecclesiastes3:17 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes3:18 I said in my heart with regard to the sons of men that God is testing them to show them that they are but beasts.
Ecclesiastes3:19 For the fate of the sons of men and the fate of beasts is the same; as one dies
Ecclesiastes3:20 All go to one place; all are from the dust
Ecclesiastes3:21 Who knows whether the spirit of man goes upward and the spirit of the beast goes down to the earth?
Ecclesiastes3:22 So I saw that there is nothing better than that a man should enjoy his work
Ecclesiastes4:1 Again I saw all the oppressions that are practiced under the sun. And behold
Ecclesiastes4:2 And I thought the dead who are already dead more fortunate than the living who are still alive;
Ecclesiastes4:3 but better than both is he who has not yet been
Ecclesiastes4:4 Then I saw that all toil and all skill in work come from a man's envy of his neighbor. This also is vanity and a striving after wind.
Ecclesiastes4:5 The fool folds his hands
Ecclesiastes4:6 Better is a handful of quietness than two hands full of toil and a striving after wind.
Ecclesiastes4:7 Again
Ecclesiastes4:8 a person who has no one
Ecclesiastes4:9 Two are better than one
Ecclesiastes4:10 For if they fall
Ecclesiastes4:11 Again
Ecclesiastes4:12 And though a man might prevail against one who is alone
Ecclesiastes4:13 Better is a poor and wise youth than an old and foolish king
Ecclesiastes4:14 even though he had gone from prison to the throne or in his own kingdom had been born poor.
Ecclesiastes4:15 I saw all the living who move about under the sun
Ecclesiastes4:16 there was no end of all the people; he was over all of them. Yet those who come later will not rejoice in him. Surely this also is vanity and a striving after wind.
Ecclesiastes5:1 Guard your steps when you go to the house of God; to draw near to listen is better than to offer the sacrifice of fools; for they do not know that they are doing evil.
Ecclesiastes5:2 Be not rash with your mouth
Ecclesiastes5:3 For a dream comes with much business
Ecclesiastes5:4 When you vow a vow to God
Ecclesiastes5:5 It is better that you should not vow than that you should vow and not pay.
Ecclesiastes5:6 Let not your mouth lead you into sin
Ecclesiastes5:7 For when dreams increase
Ecclesiastes5:8 If you see in a province the poor oppressed and justice and right violently taken away
Ecclesiastes5:9 But in all
Ecclesiastes5:10 He who loves money will not be satisfied with money; nor he who loves wealth
Ecclesiastes5:11 When goods increase
Ecclesiastes5:12 Sweet is the sleep of a laborer
Ecclesiastes5:13 There is a grievous evil which I have seen under the sun: riches were kept by their owner to his hurt
Ecclesiastes5:14 and those riches were lost in a bad venture; and he is father of a son
Ecclesiastes5:15 As he came from his mother's womb he shall go again
Ecclesiastes5:16 This also is a grievous evil: just as he came
Ecclesiastes5:17 and spent all his days in darkness and grief
Ecclesiastes5:18 Behold
Ecclesiastes5:19 Every man also to whom God has given wealth and possessions and power to enjoy them
Ecclesiastes5:20 For he will not much remember the days of his life because God keeps him occupied with joy in his heart.
Ecclesiastes6:1 There is an evil which I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes6:2 a man to whom God gives wealth
Ecclesiastes6:3 If a man begets a hundred children
Ecclesiastes6:4 For it comes into vanity and goes into darkness
Ecclesiastes6:5 moreover it has not seen the sun or known anything; yet it finds rest rather than he.
Ecclesiastes6:6 Even though he should live a thousand years twice told
Ecclesiastes6:7 All the toil of man is for his mouth
Ecclesiastes6:8 For what advantage has the wise man over the fool? And what does the poor man have who knows how to conduct himself before the living?
Ecclesiastes6:9 Better is the sight of the eyes than the wandering of desire; this also is vanity and a striving after wind.
Ecclesiastes6:10 Whatever has come to be has already been named
Ecclesiastes6:11 The more words
Ecclesiastes6:12 For who knows what is good for man while he lives the few days of his vain life
Ecclesiastes7:1 A good name is better than precious ointment; and the day of death
Ecclesiastes7:2 It is better to go to the house of mourning than to go to the house of feasting; for this is the end of all men
Ecclesiastes7:3 Sorrow is better than laughter
Ecclesiastes7:4 The heart of the wise is in the house of mourning; but the heart of fools is in the house of mirth.
Ecclesiastes7:5 It is better for a man to hear the rebuke of the wise than to hear the song of fools.
Ecclesiastes7:6 For as the crackling of thorns under a pot
Ecclesiastes7:7 Surely oppression makes the wise man foolish
Ecclesiastes7:8 Better is the end of a thing than its beginning; and the patient in spirit is better than the proud in spirit.
Ecclesiastes7:9 Be not quick to anger
Ecclesiastes7:10 Say not
Ecclesiastes7:11 Wisdom is good with an inheritance
Ecclesiastes7:12 For the protection of wisdom is like the protection of money; and the advantage of knowledge is that wisdom preserves the life of him who has it.
Ecclesiastes7:13 Consider the work of God; who can make straight what he has made crooked?
Ecclesiastes7:14 In the day of prosperity be joyful
Ecclesiastes7:15 In my vain life I have seen everything; there is a righteous man who perishes in his righteousness
Ecclesiastes7:16 Be not righteous overmuch
Ecclesiastes7:17 Be not wicked overmuch
Ecclesiastes7:18 It is good that you should take hold of this
Ecclesiastes7:19 Wisdom gives strength to the wise man more than ten rulers that are in a city.
Ecclesiastes7:20 Surely there is not a righteous man on earth who does good and never sins.
Ecclesiastes7:21 Do not give heed to all the things that men say
Ecclesiastes7:22 your heart knows that many times you have yourself cursed others.
Ecclesiastes7:23 All this I have tested by wisdom; I said
Ecclesiastes7:24 That which is
Ecclesiastes7:25 I turned my mind to know and to search out and to seek wisdom and the sum of things
Ecclesiastes7:26 And I found more bitter than death the woman whose heart is snares and nets
Ecclesiastes7:27 Behold
Ecclesiastes7:28 which my mind has sought repeatedly
Ecclesiastes7:29 Behold
Ecclesiastes8:1 Who is like the wise man? And who knows the interpretation of a thing? A man's wisdom makes his face shine
Ecclesiastes8:2 Keep the king's command
Ecclesiastes8:3 go from his presence
Ecclesiastes8:4 For the word of the king is supreme
Ecclesiastes8:5 He who obeys a command will meet no harm
Ecclesiastes8:6 For every matter has its time and way
Ecclesiastes8:7 For he does not know what is to be
Ecclesiastes8:8 No man has power to retain the spirit
Ecclesiastes8:9 All this I observed while applying my mind to all that is done under the sun
Ecclesiastes8:10 Then I saw the wicked buried; they used to go in and out of the holy place
Ecclesiastes8:11 Because sentence against an evil deed is not executed speedily
Ecclesiastes8:12 Though a sinner does evil a hundred times and prolongs his life
Ecclesiastes8:13 but it will not be well with the wicked
Ecclesiastes8:14 There is a vanity which takes place on earth
Ecclesiastes8:15 And I commend enjoyment
Ecclesiastes8:16 When I applied my mind to know wisdom
Ecclesiastes8:17 then I saw all the work of God
Ecclesiastes9:1 But all this I laid to heart
Ecclesiastes9:2 since one fate comes to all
Ecclesiastes9:3 This is an evil in all that is done under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:4 But he who is joined with all the living has hope
Ecclesiastes9:5 For the living know that they will die
Ecclesiastes9:6 Their love and their hate and their envy have already perished
Ecclesiastes9:7 Go
Ecclesiastes9:8 Let your garments be always white; let not oil be lacking on your head.
Ecclesiastes9:9 Enjoy life with the wife whom you love
Ecclesiastes9:10 Whatever your hand finds to do
Ecclesiastes9:11 Again I saw that under the sun the race is not to the swift
Ecclesiastes9:12 For man does not know his time. Like fish which are taken in an evil net
Ecclesiastes9:13 I have also seen this example of wisdom under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:14 There was a little city with few men in it; and a great king came against it and besieged it
Ecclesiastes9:15 But there was found in it a poor wise man
Ecclesiastes9:16 But I say that wisdom is better than might
Ecclesiastes9:17 The words of the wise heard in quiet are better than the shouting of a ruler among fools.
Ecclesiastes9:18 Wisdom is better than weapons of war
Ecclesiastes10:1 Dead flies make the perfumer's ointment give off an evil odor; so a little folly outweighs wisdom and honor.
Ecclesiastes10:2 A wise man's heart inclines him toward the right
Ecclesiastes10:3 Even when the fool walks on the road
Ecclesiastes10:4 If the anger of the ruler rises against you
Ecclesiastes10:5 There is an evil which I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes10:6 folly is set in many high places
Ecclesiastes10:7 I have seen slaves on horses
Ecclesiastes10:8 He who digs a pit will fall into it; and a serpent will bite him who breaks through a wall.
Ecclesiastes10:9 He who quarries stones is hurt by them; and he who splits logs is endangered by them.
Ecclesiastes10:10 If the iron is blunt
Ecclesiastes10:11 If the serpent bites before it is charmed
Ecclesiastes10:12 The words of a wise man's mouth win him favor
Ecclesiastes10:13 The beginning of the words of his mouth is foolishness
Ecclesiastes10:14 A fool multiplies words
Ecclesiastes10:15 The toil of a fool wearies him
Ecclesiastes10:16 Woe to you
Ecclesiastes10:17 Happy are you
Ecclesiastes10:18 Through sloth the roof sinks in
Ecclesiastes10:19 Bread is made for laughter
Ecclesiastes10:20 Even in your thought

Ecclesiastes11:2 Give a portion to seven
Ecclesiastes11:3 If the clouds are full of rain

Ecclesiastes11:6 In the morning sow your seed
Ecclesiastes11:7 Light is sweet
Ecclesiastes11:8 For if a man lives many years

Isaiah1:2 Hear

Isaiah1:4 Ah

Isaiah1:6 From the sole of the foot even to the head
Isaiah1:7 Your country lies desolate
Isaiah1:8 And the daughter of Zion is left like a booth in a vineyard
Isaiah1:9 If the Lord of hosts had not left us a few survivors
Isaiah1:10 Hear the word of the Lord
Isaiah1:11 “What to me is the multitude of your sacrifices? says the Lord; I have had enough of burnt offerings of rams and the fat of fed beasts; I do not delight in the blood of bulls
Isaiah1:12 “When you come to appear before me
Isaiah1:13 Bring no more vain offerings; incense is an abomination to me. New moon and sabbath and the calling of assemblies— I cannot endure iniquity and solemn assembly.
Isaiah1:14 Your new moons and your appointed feasts my soul hates; they have become a burden to me
Isaiah1:15 When you spread forth your hands
Isaiah1:16 Wash yourselves; make yourselves clean; remove the evil of your doings from before my eyes; cease to do evil
Isaiah1:17 learn to do good; seek justice
Isaiah1:18 “Come now
Isaiah1:19 If you are willing and obedient
Isaiah1:20 but if you refuse and rebel
Isaiah1:21 How the faithful city has become a harlot
Isaiah1:22 Your silver has become dross
Isaiah1:23 Your princes are rebels and companions of thieves. Every one loves a bribe and runs after gifts. They do not defend the fatherless
Isaiah1:24 Therefore the Lord says
Isaiah1:25 I will turn my hand against you and will smelt away your dross as with lye and remove all your alloy.
Isaiah1:26 And I will restore your judges as at the first
Isaiah1:27 Zion shall be redeemed by justice
Isaiah1:28 But rebels and sinners shall be destroyed together
Isaiah1:29 For you shall be ashamed of the oaks in which you delighted; and you shall blush for the gardens which you have chosen.
Isaiah1:30 For you shall be like an oak whose leaf withers
Isaiah1:31 And the strong shall become tow

Isaiah10:2 to turn aside the needy from justice and to rob the poor of my people of their right
Isaiah10:3 What will you do on the day of punishment
Isaiah10:4 Nothing remains but to crouch among the prisoners or fall among the slain. For all this his anger is not turned away and his hand is stretched out still.

Isaiah10:6 Against a godless nation I send him
Isaiah10:7 But he does not so intend
Isaiah10:8 for he says: “Are not my commanders all kings?
Isaiah10:9 Is not Calno like Carchemish? Is not Hamath like Arpad? Is not Samaria like Damascus?
