Isaiah10:11 shall I not do to Jerusalem and her idols as I have done to Samaria and her images?”
Isaiah10:12 When the Lord has finished all his work on Mount Zion and on Jerusalem he will punish the arrogant boasting of the king of Assyria and his haughty pride.
Isaiah10:13 For he says: “By the strength of my hand I have done it

Isaiah10:16 Therefore the Lord

Isaiah10:18 The glory of his forest and of his fruitful land the Lord will destroy

Isaiah11:5 Righteousness shall be the girdle of his waist
Isaiah11:6 The wolf shall dwell with the lamb
Isaiah11:7 The cow and the bear shall feed; their young shall lie down together; and the lion shall eat straw like the ox.
Isaiah11:8 The sucking child shall play over the hole of the asp
Isaiah11:9 They shall not hurt or destroy in all my holy mountain; for the earth shall be full of the knowledge of the Lord as the waters cover the sea.

Isaiah11:11 In that day the Lord will extend his hand yet a second time to recover the remnant which is left of his people

Isaiah13:8 and they will be dismayed. Pangs and agony will seize them; they will be in anguish like a woman in travail. They will look aghast at one another; their faces will be aflame.
Isaiah13:9 Behold
Isaiah13:10 For the stars of the heavens and their constellations will not give their light; the sun will be dark at its rising and the moon will not shed its light.
Isaiah13:11 I will punish the world for its evil
Isaiah13:12 I will make men more rare than fine gold
Isaiah13:13 Therefore I will make the heavens tremble
Isaiah13:14 And like a hunted gazelle
Isaiah13:15 Whoever is found will be thrust through
Isaiah13:16 Their infants will be dashed in pieces before their eyes; their houses will be plundered and their wives ravished.

Isaiah14:6 that smote the peoples in wrath with unceasing blows
Isaiah14:7 The whole earth is at rest and quiet; they break forth into singing.

Isaiah14:9 Sheol beneath is stirred up to meet you when you come

Isaiah15:3 in the streets they gird on sackcloth; on the housetops and in the squares every one wails and melts in tears.

Isaiah15:6 the waters of Nimrim are a desolation; the grass is withered

Jeremiah29:7 But seek the welfare of the city where I have sent you into exile
Jeremiah29:8 For thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah29:9 for it is a lie which they are prophesying to you in my name; I did not send them
Jeremiah29:10 “For thus says the Lord: When seventy years are completed for Babylon
Jeremiah29:11 For I know the plans I have for you
Jeremiah29:12 Then you will call upon me and come and pray to me
Jeremiah29:13 You will seek me and find me; when you seek me with all your heart
Jeremiah29:14 I will be found by you
Jeremiah29:15 “Because you have said
Jeremiah29:16 Thus says the Lord concerning the king who sits on the throne of David
Jeremiah29:17 ‘Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah29:18 I will pursue them with sword
Jeremiah29:19 because they did not heed my words
Jeremiah29:20 Hear the word of the Lord
Jeremiah29:21 ‘Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah29:22 Because of them this curse shall be used by all the exiles from Judah in Babylon: “The Lord make you like Zedekiah and Ahab

Jeremiah44:2 “Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah44:3 because of the wickedness which they committed
Jeremiah44:4 Yet I persistently sent to you all my servants the prophets
Jeremiah44:5 But they did not listen or incline their ear
Jeremiah44:6 Therefore my wrath and my anger were poured forth and kindled in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem; and they became a waste and a desolation
Jeremiah44:7 And now thus says the Lord God of hosts
Jeremiah44:8 Why do you provoke me to anger with the works of your hands

Jeremiah44:12 I will take the remnant of Judah who have set their faces to come to the land of Egypt to live
Jeremiah44:13 I will punish those who dwell in the land of Egypt
Jeremiah44:14 so that none of the remnant of Judah who have come to live in the land of Egypt shall escape or survive or return to the land of Judah
Jeremiah44:15 Then all the men who knew that their wives had offered incense to other gods
Jeremiah44:16 “As for the word which you have spoken to us in the name of the Lord
Jeremiah44:17 But we will do everything that we have vowed
Jeremiah44:18 But since we left off burning incense to the queen of heaven and pouring out libations to her
Jeremiah44:19 And the women said
Jeremiah44:20 Then Jeremiah said to all the people
Jeremiah44:21 “As for the incense that you burned in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem
Jeremiah44:22 The Lord could no longer bear your evil doings and the abominations which you committed; therefore your land has become a desolation and a waste and a curse
Jeremiah44:23 It is because you burned incense
Jeremiah44:24 Jeremiah said to all the people and all the women
Jeremiah44:25 Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah44:26 Therefore hear the word of the Lord
Jeremiah44:27 Behold
Jeremiah44:28 And those who escape the sword shall return from the land of Egypt to the land of Judah
Jeremiah44:29 This shall be the sign to you
Jeremiah44:30 Thus says the Lord

Jeremiah48:1 Concerning Moab. Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah48:2 the renown of Moab is no more. In Heshbon they planned evil against her: ‘Come
Jeremiah48:3 “Hark! a cry from Horonaim
Jeremiah48:4 Moab is destroyed; a cry is heard as far as Zoar.
Jeremiah48:5 For at the ascent of Luhith they go up weeping; for at the descent of Horonaim they have heard the cry of destruction.
Jeremiah48:6 Flee! Save yourselves! Be like a wild ass in the desert!
Jeremiah48:7 For
Jeremiah48:8 The destroyer shall come upon every city
Jeremiah48:9 “Give wings to Moab
Jeremiah48:10 “Cursed is he who does the work of the Lord with slackness; and cursed is he who keeps back his sword from bloodshed.
Jeremiah48:11 “Moab has been at ease from his youth and has settled on his lees; he has not been emptied from vessel to vessel
Jeremiah48:12 “Therefore
Jeremiah48:13 Then Moab shall be ashamed of Chemosh
Jeremiah48:14 “How do you say
Jeremiah48:15 The destroyer of Moab and his cities has come up
Jeremiah48:16 The calamity of Moab is near at hand and his affliction hastens apace.
Jeremiah48:17 Bemoan him
Jeremiah48:18 “Come down from your glory
Jeremiah48:19 Stand by the way and watch
Jeremiah48:20 Moab is put to shame
Jeremiah48:21 “Judgment has come upon the tableland
Jeremiah48:22 and Dibon
Jeremiah48:23 and Kiriathaim
Jeremiah48:24 and Keri-oth
Jeremiah48:25 The horn of Moab is cut off
Jeremiah48:26 “Make him drunk
Jeremiah48:27 Was not Israel a derision to you? Was he found among thieves
Jeremiah48:28 “Leave the cities
Jeremiah48:29 We have heard of the pride of Moab— he is very proud— of his loftiness
Jeremiah48:30 I know his insolence
Jeremiah48:31 Therefore I wail for Moab; I cry out for all Moab; for the men of Kir-heres I mourn.
Jeremiah48:32 More than for Jazer I weep for you
Jeremiah48:33 Gladness and joy have been taken away from the fruitful land of Moab; I have made the wine cease from the wine presses; no one treads them with shouts of joy; the shouting is not the shout of joy.
Jeremiah48:34 “Heshbon and Ele-aleh cry out; as far as Jahaz they utter their voice
Jeremiah48:35 And I will bring to an end in Moab
Jeremiah48:36 Therefore my heart moans for Moab like a flute
Jeremiah48:37 “For every head is shaved and every beard cut off; upon all the hands are gashes
Jeremiah48:38 On all the housetops of Moab and in the squares there is nothing but lamentation; for I have broken Moab like a vessel for which no one cares
Jeremiah48:39 How it is broken! How they wail! How Moab has turned his back in shame! So Moab has become a derision and a horror to all that are round about him.”
Jeremiah48:40 For thus says the Lord: “Behold
Jeremiah48:41 the cities shall be taken and the strongholds seized. The heart of the warriors of Moab shall be in that day like the heart of a woman in her pangs;
Jeremiah48:42 Moab shall be destroyed and be no longer a people
Jeremiah48:43 Terror
Jeremiah48:44 He who flees from the terror shall fall into the pit
Jeremiah48:45 “In the shadow of Heshbon fugitives stop without strength; for a fire has gone forth from Heshbon
Jeremiah48:46 Woe to you
Jeremiah48:47 Yet I will restore the fortunes of Moab in the latter days

Jeremiah51:1 Thus says the Lord: “Behold
Jeremiah51:2 and I will send to Babylon winnowers
Jeremiah51:3 Let not the archer bend his bow
Jeremiah51:4 They shall fall down slain in the land of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah51:5 For Israel and Judah have not been forsaken by their God
Jeremiah51:6 “Flee from the midst of Babylon
Jeremiah51:7 Babylon was a golden cup in the Lord 's hand
Jeremiah51:8 Suddenly Babylon has fallen and been broken; wail for her! Take balm for her pain; perhaps she may be healed.
Jeremiah51:9 We would have healed Babylon
Jeremiah51:10 The Lord has brought forth our vindication; come
Jeremiah51:11 “Sharpen the arrows! Take up the shields! The Lord has stirred up the spirit of the kings of the Medes
Jeremiah51:12 Set up a standard against the walls of Babylon; make the watch strong; set up watchmen; prepare the ambushes; for the Lord has both planned and done what he spoke concerning the inhabitants of Babylon.
Jeremiah51:13 O you who dwell by many waters
Jeremiah51:14 The Lord of hosts has sworn by himself: Surely I will fill you with men
Jeremiah51:15 “It is he who made the earth by his power
Jeremiah51:16 When he utters his voice there is a tumult of waters in the heavens
Jeremiah51:17 Every man is stupid and without knowledge; every goldsmith is put to shame by his idols; for his images are false
Jeremiah51:18 They are worthless
Jeremiah51:19 Not like these is he who is the portion of Jacob
Jeremiah51:20 “You are my hammer and weapon of war: with you I break nations in pieces; with you I destroy kingdoms;
Jeremiah51:21 with you I break in pieces the horse and his rider; with you I break in pieces the chariot and the charioteer;
Jeremiah51:22 with you I break in pieces man and woman; with you I break in pieces the old man and the youth; with you I break in pieces the young man and the maiden;
Jeremiah51:23 with you I break in pieces the shepherd and his flock; with you I break in pieces the farmer and his team; with you I break in pieces governors and commanders.
Jeremiah51:24 “I will requite Babylon and all the inhabitants of Chaldea before your very eyes for all the evil that they have done in Zion
Jeremiah51:25 “Behold
Jeremiah51:26 No stone shall be taken from you for a corner and no stone for a foundation
Jeremiah51:27 “Set up a standard on the earth
Jeremiah51:28 Prepare the nations for war against her
Jeremiah51:29 The land trembles and writhes in pain
Jeremiah51:30 The warriors of Babylon have ceased fighting
Jeremiah51:31 One runner runs to meet another
Jeremiah51:32 the fords have been seized
Jeremiah51:33 For thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah51:34 “Nebuchadrezzar the king of Babylon has devoured me
Jeremiah51:35 The violence done to me and to my kinsmen be upon Babylon
Jeremiah51:36 Therefore thus says the Lord: “Behold
Jeremiah51:37 and Babylon shall become a heap of ruins
Jeremiah51:38 “They shall roar together like lions; they shall growl like lions' whelps.
Jeremiah51:39 While they are inflamed I will prepare them a feast and make them drunk
Jeremiah51:40 I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter
Jeremiah51:41 “How Babylon is taken
Jeremiah51:42 The sea has come up on Babylon; she is covered with its tumultuous waves.
Jeremiah51:43 Her cities have become a horror
Jeremiah51:44 And I will punish Bel in Babylon
Jeremiah51:45 “Go out of the midst of her
Jeremiah51:46 Let not your heart faint
Jeremiah51:47 “Therefore
Jeremiah51:48 Then the heavens and the earth

Jeremiah51:52 “Therefore

Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king; and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Hamutal the daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.
Jeremiah52:2 And he did what was evil in the sight of the Lord
Jeremiah52:3 Surely because of the anger of the Lord things came to such a pass in Jerusalem and Judah that he cast them out from his presence. And Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.
Jeremiah52:4 And in the ninth year of his reign
Jeremiah52:5 So the city was besieged till the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.
Jeremiah52:6 On the ninth day of the fourth month the famine was so severe in the city
Jeremiah52:7 Then a breach was made in the city; and all the men of war fled and went out from the city by night by the way of a gate between the two walls
Jeremiah52:8 But the army of the Chaldeans pursued the king
Jeremiah52:9 Then they captured the king
Jeremiah52:10 The king of Babylon slew the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes
Jeremiah52:11 He put out the eyes of Zedekiah
Jeremiah52:12 In the fifth month
Jeremiah52:13 And he burned the house of the Lord
Jeremiah52:14 And all the army of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah52:15 And Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard carried away captive some of the poorest of the people and the rest of the people who were left in the city and the deserters who had deserted to the king of Babylon
Jeremiah52:16 But Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard left some of the poorest of the land to be vinedressers and plowmen.
Jeremiah52:17 And the pillars of bronze that were in the house of the Lord
Jeremiah52:18 And they took away the pots
Jeremiah52:19 also the small bowls
Jeremiah52:20 As for the two pillars
Jeremiah52:21 As for the pillars
Jeremiah52:22 Upon it was a capital of bronze; the height of the one capital was five cubits; a network and pomegranates
Jeremiah52:23 There were ninety-six pomegranates on the sides; all the pomegranates were a hundred upon the network round about.
Jeremiah52:24 And the captain of the guard took Seraiah the chief priest
Jeremiah52:25 and from the city he took an officer who had been in command of the men of war
Jeremiah52:26 And Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took them
Jeremiah52:27 And the king of Babylon smote them
Jeremiah52:28 This is the number of the people whom Nebuchadrezzar carried away captive: in the seventh year
Jeremiah52:29 in the eighteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar he carried away captive from Jerusalem eight hundred and thirty-two persons;
Jeremiah52:30 in the twenty-third year of Nebuchadrezzar
Jeremiah52:31 And in the thirty-seventh year of the captivity of Jehoiachin king of Judah
Jeremiah52:32 and he spoke kindly to him
Jeremiah52:33 So Jehoiachin put off his prison garments. And every day of his life he dined regularly at the king's table;
Jeremiah52:34 as for his allowance
Lamentations1:1 How lonely sits the city that was full of people! How like a widow has she become
Lamentations1:2 She weeps bitterly in the night
Lamentations1:3 Judah has gone into exile because of affliction and hard servitude; she dwells now among the nations
Lamentations1:4 The roads to Zion mourn
Lamentations1:5 Her foes have become the head
Lamentations1:6 From the daughter of Zion has departed all her majesty. Her princes have become like harts that find no pasture; they fled without strength before the pursuer.
Lamentations1:7 Jerusalem remembers in the days of her affliction and bitterness all the precious things that were hers from days of old. When her people fell into the hand of the foe
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem sinned grievously
Lamentations1:9 Her uncleanness was in her skirts; she took no thought of her doom; therefore her fall is terrible
Lamentations1:10 The enemy has stretched out his hands over all her precious things; yea
Lamentations1:11 All her people groan as they search for bread; they trade their treasures for food to revive their strength. “Look
Lamentations1:12 “Is it nothing to you
Lamentations1:13 “From on high he sent fire; into my bones he made it descend; he spread a net for my feet; he turned me back; he has left me stunned
Lamentations1:14 “My transgressions were bound into a yoke; by his hand they were fastened together; they were set upon my neck; he caused my strength to fail; the Lord gave me into the hands of those whom I cannot withstand.
Lamentations1:15 “The Lord flouted all my mighty men in the midst of me; he summoned an assembly against me to crush my young men; the Lord has trodden as in a wine press the virgin daughter of Judah.
Lamentations1:16 “For these things I weep; my eyes flow with tears; for a comforter is far from me
Lamentations1:17 Zion stretches out her hands
Lamentations1:18 “The Lord is in the right
Lamentations1:19 “I called to my lovers but they deceived me; my priests and elders perished in the city
Lamentations1:20 “Behold
Lamentations1:21 “Hear how I groan; there is none to comfort me. All my enemies have heard of my trouble; they are glad that thou hast done it. Bring thou the day thou hast announced
Lamentations1:22 “Let all their evil-doing come before thee; and deal with them as thou hast dealt with me because of all my transgressions; for my groans are many and my heart is faint.”
Lamentations2:1 How the Lord in his anger has set the daughter of Zion under a cloud! He has cast down from heaven to earth the splendor of Israel; he has not remembered his footstool in the day of his anger.
Lamentations2:2 The Lord has destroyed without mercy all the habitations of Jacob; in his wrath he has broken down the strongholds of the daughter of Judah; he has brought down to the ground in dishonor the kingdom and its rulers.
Lamentations2:3 He has cut down in fierce anger all the might of Israel; he has withdrawn from them his right hand in the face of the enemy; he has burned like a flaming fire in Jacob
Lamentations2:4 He has bent his bow like an enemy
Lamentations2:5 The Lord has become like an enemy
Lamentations2:6 He has broken down his booth like that of a garden
Lamentations2:7 The Lord has scorned his altar
Lamentations2:8 The Lord determined to lay in ruins the wall of the daughter of Zion; he marked it off by the line; he restrained not his hand from destroying; he caused rampart and wall to lament
Lamentations2:9 Her gates have sunk into the ground; he has ruined and broken her bars; her king and princes are among the nations; the law is no more
Lamentations2:10 The elders of the daughter of Zion sit on the ground in silence; they have cast dust on their heads and put on sackcloth; the maidens of Jerusalem have bowed their heads to the ground.
Lamentations2:11 My eyes are spent with weeping; my soul is in tumult; my heart is poured out in grief because of the destruction of the daughter of my people
Lamentations2:12 They cry to their mothers
Lamentations2:13 What can I say for you
Lamentations2:14 Your prophets have seen for you false and deceptive visions; they have not exposed your iniquity to restore your fortunes
Lamentations2:15 All who pass along the way clap their hands at you; they hiss and wag their heads at the daughter of Jerusalem; “Is this the city which was called the perfection of beauty
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies rail against you; they hiss
Lamentations2:17 The Lord has done what he purposed
Lamentations2:18 Cry aloud to the Lord! O daughter of Zion! Let tears stream down like a torrent day and night! Give yourself no rest
Lamentations2:19 Arise
Lamentations2:20 Look
Lamentations2:21 In the dust of the streets lie the young and the old; my maidens and my young men have fallen by the sword; in the day of thy anger thou hast slain them
Lamentations2:22 Thou didst invite as to the day of an appointed feast my terrors on every side; and on the day of the anger of the Lord none escaped or survived; those whom I dandled and reared my enemy destroyed.
Lamentations3:1 I am the man who has seen affliction under the rod of his wrath;
Lamentations3:2 he has driven and brought me into darkness without any light;
Lamentations3:3 surely against me he turns his hand again and again the whole day long.
Lamentations3:4 He has made my flesh and my skin waste away
Lamentations3:5 he has beseiged and enveloped me with bitterness and tribulation;
Lamentations3:6 he has made me dwell in darkness like the dead of long ago.
Lamentations3:7 He has walled me about so that I cannot escape; he has put heavy chains on me;
Lamentations3:8 though I call and cry for help
Lamentations3:9 he has blocked my ways with hewn stones
Lamentations3:10 He is to me like a bear lying in wait
Lamentations3:11 he led me off my way and tore me to pieces; he has made me desolate;
Lamentations3:12 he bent his bow and set me as a mark for his arrow.
Lamentations3:13 He drove into my heart the arrows of his quiver;
Lamentations3:14 I have become the laughingstock of all peoples
Lamentations3:15 He has filled me with bitterness
Lamentations3:16 He has made my teeth grind on gravel
Lamentations3:17 my soul is bereft of peace
Lamentations3:18 so I say
Lamentations3:19 Remember my affliction and my bitterness
Lamentations3:20 My soul continually thinks of it and is bowed down within me.
Lamentations3:21 But this I call to mind
Lamentations3:22 The steadfast love of the Lord never ceases
Lamentations3:23 they are new every morning; great is thy faithfulness.
Lamentations3:24 “The Lord is my portion
Lamentations3:25 The Lord is good to those who wait for him
Lamentations3:26 It is good that one should wait quietly for the salvation of the Lord.
Lamentations3:27 It is good for a man that he bear the yoke in his youth.
Lamentations3:28 Let him sit alone in silence when he has laid it on him;
Lamentations3:29 let him put his mouth in the dust— there may yet be hope;
Lamentations3:30 let him give his cheek to the smiter
Lamentations3:31 For the Lord will not cast off for ever
Lamentations3:32 but
Lamentations3:33 for he does not willingly afflict or grieve the sons of men.
Lamentations3:34 To crush under foot all the prisoners of the earth
Lamentations3:35 to turn aside the right of a man in the presence of the Most High
Lamentations3:36 to subvert a man in his cause
Lamentations3:37 Who has commanded and it came to pass
Lamentations3:38 Is it not from the mouth of the Most High that good and evil come?
Lamentations3:39 Why should a living man complain
Lamentations3:40 Let us test and examine our ways
Lamentations3:41 Let us lift up our hearts and hands to God in heaven:
Lamentations3:42 “We have transgressed and rebelled
Lamentations3:43 “Thou hast wrapped thyself with anger and pursued us
Lamentations3:44 thou hast wrapped thyself with a cloud so that no prayer can pass through.
Lamentations3:45 Thou hast made us offscouring and refuse among the peoples.
Lamentations3:46 “All our enemies rail against us;
Lamentations3:47 panic and pitfall have come upon us
Lamentations3:48 my eyes flow with rivers of tears because of the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations3:49 “My eyes will flow without ceasing
Lamentations3:50 until the Lord from heaven looks down and sees;
Lamentations3:51 my eyes cause me grief at the fate of all the maidens of my city.
Lamentations3:52 “I have been hunted like a bird by those who were my enemies without cause;
Lamentations3:53 they flung me alive into the pit and cast stones on me;
Lamentations3:54 water closed over my head; I said
Lamentations3:55 “I called on thy name
Lamentations3:56 thou didst hear my plea
Lamentations3:57 Thou didst come near when I called on thee; thou didst say
Lamentations3:58 “Thou hast taken up my cause
Lamentations3:59 Thou hast seen the wrong done to me
Lamentations3:60 Thou hast seen all their vengeance
Lamentations3:61 “Thou hast heard their taunts
Lamentations3:62 The lips and thoughts of my assailants are against me all the day long.
Lamentations3:63 Behold their sitting and their rising; I am the burden of their songs.
Lamentations3:64 “Thou wilt requite them
Lamentations3:65 Thou wilt give them dullness of heart; thy curse will be on them.
Lamentations3:66 Thou wilt pursue them in anger and destroy them from under thy heavens
Lamentations4:1 How the gold has grown dim
Lamentations4:2 The precious sons of Zion
Lamentations4:3 Even the jackals give the breast and suckle their young
Lamentations4:4 The tongue of the nursling cleaves to the roof of its mouth for thirst; the children beg for food
Lamentations4:5 Those who feasted on dainties perish in the streets; those who were brought up in purple lie on ash heaps.
Lamentations4:6 For the chastisement of the daughter of my people has been greater than the punishment of Sodom
Lamentations4:7 Her princes were purer than snow
Lamentations4:8 Now their visage is blacker than soot
Lamentations4:9 Happier were the victims of the sword than the victims of hunger
Lamentations4:10 The hands of compassionate women have boiled their own children; they became their food in the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations4:11 The Lord gave full vent to his wrath
Lamentations4:12 The kings of the earth did not believe
Lamentations4:13 This was for the sins of her prophets and the iniquities of her priests
Lamentations4:14 They wandered
Lamentations4:15 “Away! Unclean!” men cried at them; “Away! Away! Touch not!” So they became fugitives and wanderers; men said among the nations
Lamentations4:16 The Lord himself has scattered them
Lamentations4:17 Our eyes failed
Lamentations4:18 Men dogged our steps so that we could not walk in our streets; our end drew near; our days were numbered; for our end had come.
Lamentations4:19 Our pursuers were swifter than the vultures in the heavens; they chased us on the mountains
Lamentations4:20 The breath of our nostrils
Lamentations4:21 Rejoice and be glad
Lamentations4:22 The punishment of your iniquity
Lamentations5:1 Remember
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance has been turned over to strangers
Lamentations5:3 We have become orphans
Lamentations5:4 We must pay for the water we drink
Lamentations5:5 With a yoke on our necks we are hard driven; we are weary
Lamentations5:6 We have given the hand to Egypt
Lamentations5:7 Our fathers sinned
Lamentations5:8 Slaves rule over us; there is none to deliver us from their hand.
Lamentations5:9 We get our bread at the peril of our lives
Lamentations5:10 Our skin is hot as an oven with the burning heat of famine.
Lamentations5:11 Women are ravished in Zion
Lamentations5:12 Princes are hung up by their hands; no respect is shown to the elders.
Lamentations5:13 Young men are compelled to grind at the mill; and boys stagger under loads of wood.
Lamentations5:14 The old men have quit the city gate
Lamentations5:15 The joy of our hearts has ceased; our dancing has been turned to mourning.
Lamentations5:16 The crown has fallen from our head; woe to us
Lamentations5:17 For this our heart has become sick
Lamentations5:18 for Mount Zion which lies desolate; jackals prowl over it.
Lamentations5:19 But thou
Lamentations5:20 Why dost thou forget us for ever
Lamentations5:21 Restore us to thyself
Lamentations5:22 Or hast thou utterly rejected us? Art thou exceedingly angry with us?
Ezekiel1:1 In the thirtieth year
Ezekiel1:2 On the fifth day of the month (it was the fifth year of the exile of King Jehoiachin)
Ezekiel1:3 the word of the Lord came to Ezekiel the priest
Ezekiel1:4 As I looked
Ezekiel1:5 And from the midst of it came the likeness of four living creatures. And this was their appearance: they had the form of men
Ezekiel1:6 but each had four faces
Ezekiel1:7 Their legs were straight
Ezekiel1:8 Under their wings on their four sides they had human hands. And the four had their faces and their wings thus:
Ezekiel1:9 their wings touched one another; they went every one straight forward
Ezekiel1:10 As for the likeness of their faces
Ezekiel1:11 Such were their faces. And their wings were spread out above; each creature had two wings
Ezekiel1:12 And each went straight forward; wherever the spirit would go
Ezekiel1:13 In the midst of the living creatures there was something that looked like burning coals of fire
Ezekiel1:14 And the living creatures darted to and fro
Ezekiel1:15 Now as I looked at the living creatures
Ezekiel1:16 As for the appearance of the wheels and their construction: their appearance was like the gleaming of a chrysolite; and the four had the same likeness
Ezekiel1:17 When they went
Ezekiel1:18 The four wheels had rims and they had spokes; and their rims were full of eyes round about.
Ezekiel1:19 And when the living creatures went
Ezekiel1:20 Wherever the spirit would go
Ezekiel1:21 When those went
Ezekiel1:22 Over the heads of the living creatures there was the likeness of a firmament
Ezekiel1:23 And under the firmament their wings were stretched out straight
Ezekiel1:24 And when they went
Ezekiel1:25 And there came a voice from above the firmament over their heads; when they stood still
Ezekiel1:26 And above the firmament over their heads there was the likeness of a throne
Ezekiel1:27 And upward from what had the appearance of his loins I saw as it were gleaming bronze
Ezekiel1:28 Like the appearance of the bow that is in the cloud on the day of rain

Ezekiel16:23 “And after all your wickedness (woe
Ezekiel16:24 you built yourself a vaulted chamber
Ezekiel16:25 at the head of every street you built your lofty place and prostituted your beauty
Ezekiel16:26 You also played the harlot with the Egyptians
Ezekiel16:27 Behold
Ezekiel16:28 You played the harlot also with the Assyrians
Ezekiel16:29 You multiplied your harlotry also with the trading land of Chaldea; and even with this you were not satisfied.
Ezekiel16:30 “How lovesick is your heart
Ezekiel16:31 building your vaulted chamber at the head of every street
Ezekiel16:32 Adulterous wife
Ezekiel16:33 Men give gifts to all harlots; but you gave your gifts to all your lovers
Ezekiel16:34 So you were different from other women in your harlotries; none solicited you to play the harlot: and you gave hire
Ezekiel16:35 “Wherefore
Ezekiel16:36 Thus says the Lord God
Ezekiel16:37 therefore
Ezekiel16:38 And I will judge you as women who break wedlock and shed blood are judged
Ezekiel16:39 And I will give you into the hand of your lovers
Ezekiel16:40 They shall bring up a host against you
Ezekiel16:41 And they shall burn your houses and execute judgments upon you in the sight of many women; I will make you stop playing the harlot
Ezekiel16:42 So will I satisfy my fury on you
Ezekiel16:43 Because you have not remembered the days of your youth
Ezekiel16:44 Behold
Ezekiel16:45 You are the daughter of your mother
Ezekiel16:46 And your elder sister is Samaria
Ezekiel16:47 Yet you were not content to walk in their ways
Ezekiel16:48 As I live
Ezekiel16:49 Behold
Ezekiel16:50 They were haughty
Ezekiel16:51 Samaria has not committed half your sins; you have committed more abominations than they
Ezekiel16:52 Bear your disgrace
Ezekiel16:53 “I will restore their fortunes
Ezekiel16:54 that you may bear your disgrace and be ashamed of all that you have done
Ezekiel16:55 As for your sisters
Ezekiel16:56 Was not your sister Sodom a byword in your mouth in the day of your pride
Ezekiel16:57 before your wickedness was uncovered? Now you have become like her an object of reproach for the daughters of Edom and all her neighbors
Ezekiel16:58 You bear the penalty of your lewdness and your abominations
Ezekiel16:59 “Yea
Ezekiel16:60 yet I will remember my covenant with you in the days of your youth
Ezekiel16:61 Then you will remember your ways
Ezekiel16:62 I will establish my covenant with you

Ezekiel17:16 As I live
Ezekiel17:17 Pharaoh with his mighty army and great company will not help him in war
Ezekiel17:18 Because he despised the oath and broke the covenant
Ezekiel17:19 Therefore thus says the Lord God: As I live
Ezekiel17:20 I will spread my net over him
Ezekiel17:21 And all the pick of his troops shall fall by the sword
Ezekiel17:22 Thus says the Lord God: “I myself will take a sprig from the lofty top of the cedar
Ezekiel17:23 on the mountain height of Israel will I plant it
Ezekiel17:24 And all the trees of the field shall know that I the Lord bring low the high tree
Ezekiel18:1 The word of the Lord came to me again:
Ezekiel18:2 “What do you mean by repeating this proverb concerning the land of Israel
Ezekiel18:3 As I live
Ezekiel18:4 Behold
Ezekiel18:5 “If a man is righteous and does what is lawful and right—
Ezekiel18:6 if he does not eat upon the mountains or lift up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel
Ezekiel18:7 does not oppress any one
Ezekiel18:8 does not lend at interest or take any increase
Ezekiel18:9 walks in my statutes
Ezekiel18:10 “If he begets a son who is a robber
Ezekiel18:11 who does none of these duties
Ezekiel18:12 oppresses the poor and needy
Ezekiel18:13 lends at interest
Ezekiel18:14 “But if this man begets a son who sees all the sins which his father has done
Ezekiel18:15 who does not eat upon the mountains or lift up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel
Ezekiel18:16 does not wrong any one
Ezekiel18:17 withholds his hand from iniquity
Ezekiel18:18 As for his father
Ezekiel18:19 “Yet you say
Ezekiel18:20 The soul that sins shall die. The son shall not suffer for the iniquity of the father
Ezekiel18:21 “But if a wicked man turns away from all his sins which he has committed and keeps all my statutes and does what is lawful and right
Ezekiel18:22 None of the transgressions which he has committed shall be remembered against him; for the righteousness which he has done he shall live.
Ezekiel18:23 Have I any pleasure in the death of the wicked
Ezekiel18:24 But when a righteous man turns away from his righteousness and commits iniquity and does the same abominable things that the wicked man does
Ezekiel18:25 “Yet you say
Ezekiel18:26 When a righteous man turns away from his righteousness and commits iniquity
Ezekiel18:27 Again
Ezekiel18:28 Because he considered and turned away from all the transgressions which he had committed
Ezekiel18:29 Yet the house of Israel says
Ezekiel18:30 “Therefore I will judge you
Ezekiel18:31 Cast away from you all the transgressions which you have committed against me
Ezekiel18:32 For I have no pleasure in the death of any one
Ezekiel19:1 And you
Ezekiel19:2 and say: What a lioness was your mother among lions! She couched in the midst of young lions
Ezekiel19:3 And she brought up one of her whelps; he became a young lion
Ezekiel19:4 The nations sounded an alarm against him; he was taken in their pit; and they brought him with hooks to the land of Egypt.
Ezekiel19:5 When she saw that she was baffled
Ezekiel19:6 He prowled among the lions; he became a young lion
Ezekiel19:7 And he ravaged their strongholds
Ezekiel19:8 Then the nations set against him snares on every side; they spread their net over him; he was taken in their pit.
Ezekiel19:9 With hooks they put him in a cage
Ezekiel19:10 Your mother was like a vine in a vineyard transplanted by the water
Ezekiel19:11 Its strongest stem became a ruler's scepter; it towered aloft among the thick boughs; it was seen in its height with the mass of its branches.
Ezekiel19:12 But the vine was plucked up in fury
Ezekiel19:13 Now it is transplanted in the wilderness
Ezekiel19:14 And fire has gone out from its stem
Ezekiel20:1 In the seventh year
Ezekiel20:2 And the word of the Lord came to me:
Ezekiel20:3 “Son of man
Ezekiel20:4 Will you judge them
Ezekiel20:5 and say to them
Ezekiel20:6 On that day I swore to them that I would bring them out of the land of Egypt into a land that I had searched out for them
Ezekiel20:7 And I said to them
Ezekiel20:8 But they rebelled against me and would not listen to me; they did not every man cast away the detestable things their eyes feasted on
Ezekiel20:9 But I acted for the sake of my name
Ezekiel20:10 So I led them out of the land of Egypt and brought them into the wilderness.
Ezekiel20:11 I gave them my statutes and showed them my ordinances
Ezekiel20:12 Moreover I gave them my sabbaths
Ezekiel20:13 But the house of Israel rebelled against me in the wilderness; they did not walk in my statutes but rejected my ordinances
Ezekiel20:14 But I acted for the sake of my name
Ezekiel20:15 Moreover I swore to them in the wilderness that I would not bring them into the land which I had given them
Ezekiel20:16 because they rejected my ordinances and did not walk in my statutes
Ezekiel20:17 Nevertheless my eye spared them
Ezekiel20:18 “And I said to their children in the wilderness
Ezekiel20:19 I the Lord am your God; walk in my statutes
Ezekiel20:20 and hallow my sabbaths that they may be a sign between me and you
Ezekiel20:21 But the children rebelled against me; they did not walk in my statutes
Ezekiel20:22 But I withheld my hand
Ezekiel20:23 Moreover I swore to them in the wilderness that I would scatter them among the nations and disperse them through the countries
Ezekiel20:24 because they had not executed my ordinances
Ezekiel20:25 Moreover I gave them statutes that were not good and ordinances by which they could not have life;
Ezekiel20:26 and I defiled them through their very gifts in making them offer by fire all their first-born
Ezekiel20:27 “Therefore
Ezekiel20:28 For when I had brought them into the land which I swore to give them
Ezekiel20:29 (I said to them
Ezekiel20:30 Wherefore say to the house of Israel
Ezekiel20:31 When you offer your gifts and sacrifice your sons by fire
Ezekiel20:32 “What is in your mind shall never happen—the thought
Ezekiel20:33 “As I live
Ezekiel20:34 I will bring you out from the peoples and gather you out of the countries where you are scattered
Ezekiel20:35 and I will bring you into the wilderness of the peoples
Ezekiel20:36 As I entered into judgment with your fathers in the wilderness of the land of Egypt
Ezekiel20:37 I will make you pass under the rod
Ezekiel20:38 I will purge out the rebels from among you
Ezekiel20:39 “As for you
Ezekiel20:40 “For on my holy mountain
Ezekiel20:41 As a pleasing odor I will accept you
Ezekiel20:42 And you shall know that I am the Lord
Ezekiel20:43 And there you shall remember your ways and all the doings with which you have polluted yourselves; and you shall loathe yourselves for all the evils that you have committed.
Ezekiel20:44 And you shall know that I am the Lord

Ezekiel20:48 All flesh shall see that I the Lord have kindled it; it shall not be quenched.”

Ezekiel21:6 Sigh therefore
Ezekiel21:7 And when they say to you
Ezekiel21:8 And the word of the Lord came to me:
Ezekiel21:9 “Son of man
Ezekiel21:10 sharpened for slaughter
Ezekiel21:11 So the sword is given to be polished
Ezekiel21:12 Cry and wail
Ezekiel21:13 For it will not be a testing—what could it do if you despise the rod?” says the Lord God.
Ezekiel21:14 “Prophesy therefore
Ezekiel21:15 that their hearts may melt
Ezekiel21:16 Cut sharply to right and left where your edge is directed.
Ezekiel21:17 I also will clap my hands
Ezekiel21:18 The word of the Lord came to me again:
Ezekiel21:19 “Son of man
Ezekiel21:20 mark a way for the sword to come to Rabbah of the Ammonites and to Judah and to Jerusalem the fortified.
Ezekiel21:21 For the king of Babylon stands at the parting of the way
Ezekiel21:22 Into his right hand comes the lot for Jerusalem
Ezekiel21:23 But to them it will seem like a false divination; they have sworn solemn oaths; but he brings their guilt to remembrance
Ezekiel21:24 “Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you have made your guilt to be remembered
Ezekiel21:25 And you
Ezekiel21:26 thus says the Lord God: Remove the turban
Ezekiel21:27 A ruin
Ezekiel21:28 “And you
Ezekiel21:29 while they see for you false visions
Ezekiel21:30 Return it to its sheath. In the place where you were created
Ezekiel21:31 And I will pour out my indignation upon you; I will blow upon you with the fire of my wrath; and I will deliver you into the hands of brutal men
Ezekiel21:32 You shall be fuel for the fire; your blood shall be in the midst of the land; you shall be no more remembered; for I the Lord have spoken.”
Ezekiel22:1 Moreover the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel22:2 “And you
Ezekiel22:3 You shall say
Ezekiel22:4 You have become guilty by the blood which you have shed
Ezekiel22:5 Those who are near and those who are far from you will mock you
Ezekiel22:6 “Behold
Ezekiel22:7 Father and mother are treated with contempt in you; the sojourner suffers extortion in your midst; the fatherless and the widow are wronged in you.
Ezekiel22:8 You have despised my holy things
Ezekiel22:9 There are men in you who slander to shed blood
Ezekiel22:10 In you men uncover their fathers' nakedness; in you they humble women who are unclean in their impurity.
Ezekiel22:11 One commits abomination with his neighbor's wife; another lewdly defiles his daughter-in-law; another in you defiles his sister
Ezekiel22:12 In you men take bribes to shed blood; you take interest and increase and make gain of your neighbors by extortion; and you have forgotten me
Ezekiel22:13 “Behold
Ezekiel22:14 Can your courage endure
Ezekiel22:15 I will scatter you among the nations and disperse you through the countries
Ezekiel22:16 And I shall be profaned through you in the sight of the nations; and you shall know that I am the Lord.”
Ezekiel22:17 And the word of the Lord came to me:
Ezekiel22:18 “Son of man
Ezekiel22:19 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you have all become dross
Ezekiel22:20 As men gather silver and bronze and iron and lead and tin into a furnace
Ezekiel22:21 I will gather you and blow upon you with the fire of my wrath
Ezekiel22:22 As silver is melted in a furnace
Ezekiel22:23 And the word of the Lord came to me:
Ezekiel22:24 “Son of man
Ezekiel22:25 Her princes in the midst of her are like a roaring lion tearing the prey; they have devoured human lives; they have taken treasure and precious things; they have made many widows in the midst of her.
Ezekiel22:26 Her priests have done violence to my law and have profaned my holy things; they have made no distinction between the holy and the common
Ezekiel22:27 Her princes in the midst of her are like wolves tearing the prey
Ezekiel22:28 And her prophets have daubed for them with whitewash
Ezekiel22:29 The people of the land have practiced extortion and committed robbery; they have oppressed the poor and needy
Ezekiel22:30 And I sought for a man among them who should build up the wall and stand in the breach before me for the land
Ezekiel22:31 Therefore I have poured out my indignation upon them; I have consumed them with the fire of my wrath; their way have I requited upon their heads
Ezekiel23:1 The word of the Lord came to me:
Ezekiel23:2 “Son of man
Ezekiel23:3 they played the harlot in Egypt; they played the harlot in their youth; there their breasts were pressed and their virgin bosoms handled.
Ezekiel23:4 Oholah was the name of the elder and Oholibah the name of her sister. They became mine
Ezekiel23:5 “Oholah played the harlot while she was mine; and she doted on her lovers the Assyrians
Ezekiel23:6 warriors clothed in purple
Ezekiel23:7 She bestowed her harlotries upon them
Ezekiel23:8 She did not give up her harlotry which she had practiced since her days in Egypt; for in her youth men had lain with her and handled her virgin bosom and poured out their lust upon her.
Ezekiel23:9 Therefore I delivered her into the hands of her lovers
Ezekiel23:10 These uncovered her nakedness; they seized her sons and her daughters; and her they slew with the sword; and she became a byword among women
Ezekiel23:11 “Her sister Oholibah saw this
Ezekiel23:12 She doted upon the Assyrians
Ezekiel23:13 And I saw that she was defiled; they both took the same way.
Ezekiel23:14 But she carried her harlotry further; she saw men portrayed upon the wall
Ezekiel23:15 girded with belts on their loins
Ezekiel23:16 When she saw them she doted upon them
Ezekiel23:17 And the Babylonians came to her into the bed of love
Ezekiel23:18 When she carried on her harlotry so openly and flaunted her nakedness
Ezekiel23:19 Yet she increased her harlotry
Ezekiel23:20 and doted upon her paramours there
Ezekiel23:21 Thus you longed for the lewdness of your youth
Ezekiel23:22 Therefore
Ezekiel23:23 the Babylonians and all the Chaldeans
Ezekiel23:24 And they shall come against you from the north with chariots and wagons and a host of peoples; they shall set themselves against you on every side with buckler
Ezekiel23:25 And I will direct my indignation against you
Ezekiel23:26 They shall also strip you of your clothes and take away your fine jewels.
Ezekiel23:27 Thus I will put an end to your lewdness and your harlotry brought from the land of Egypt; so that you shall not lift up your eyes to the Egyptians or remember them any more.
Ezekiel23:28 For thus says the Lord God: Behold
Ezekiel23:29 and they shall deal with you in hatred
Ezekiel23:30 have brought this upon you
Ezekiel23:31 You have gone the way of your sister; therefore I will give her cup into your hand.
Ezekiel23:32 Thus says the Lord God: “You shall drink your sister's cup which is deep and large; you shall be laughed at and held in derision
Ezekiel23:33 you will be filled with drunkenness and sorrow. A cup of horror and desolation
Ezekiel23:34 you shall drink it and drain it out
Ezekiel23:35 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you have forgotten me and cast me behind your back
Ezekiel23:36 The Lord said to me: “Son of man
Ezekiel23:37 For they have committed adultery
Ezekiel23:38 Moreover this they have done to me: they have defiled my sanctuary on the same day and profaned my sabbaths.
Ezekiel23:39 For when they had slaughtered their children in sacrifice to their idols
Ezekiel23:40 They even sent for men to come from far
Ezekiel23:41 you sat upon a stately couch
Ezekiel23:42 The sound of a carefree multitude was with her; and with men of the common sort drunkards were brought from the wilderness; and they put bracelets upon the hands of the women
Ezekiel23:43 “Then I said
Ezekiel23:44 For they have gone in to her
Ezekiel23:45 But righteous men shall pass judgment on them with the sentence of adulteresses
Ezekiel23:46 For thus says the Lord God: “Bring up a host against them
Ezekiel23:47 And the host shall stone them and dispatch them with their swords; they shall slay their sons and their daughters
Ezekiel23:48 Thus will I put an end to lewdness in the land
Ezekiel23:49 And your lewdness shall be requited upon you

Ezekiel24:7 For the blood she has shed is still in the midst of her; she put it on the bare rock
Ezekiel24:8 To rouse my wrath

Ezekiel24:11 Then set it empty upon the coals

Ezekiel26:1 In the eleventh year
Ezekiel26:2 “Son of man
Ezekiel26:3 therefore thus says the Lord God: Behold
Ezekiel26:4 They shall destroy the walls of Tyre
Ezekiel26:5 She shall be in the midst of the sea a place for the spreading of nets; for I have spoken
Ezekiel26:6 and her daughters on the mainland shall be slain by the sword. Then they will know that I am the Lord.
Ezekiel26:7 “For thus says the Lord God: Behold
Ezekiel26:8 He will slay with the sword your daughters on the mainland; he will set up a siege wall against you
Ezekiel26:9 He will direct the shock of his battering rams against your walls
Ezekiel26:10 His horses will be so many that their dust will cover you; your walls will shake at the noise of the horsemen and wagons and chariots
Ezekiel26:11 With the hoofs of his horses he will trample all your streets; he will slay your people with the sword; and your mighty pillars will fall to the ground.
Ezekiel26:12 They will make a spoil of your riches and a prey of your merchandise; they will break down your walls and destroy your pleasant houses; your stones and timber and soil they will cast into the midst of the waters.
Ezekiel26:13 And I will stop the music of your songs
Ezekiel26:14 I will make you a bare rock; you shall be a place for the spreading of nets; you shall never be rebuilt; for I the Lord have spoken
Ezekiel26:15 “Thus says the Lord God to Tyre: Will not the coastlands shake at the sound of your fall
Ezekiel26:16 Then all the princes of the sea will step down from their thrones
Ezekiel26:17 And they will raise a lamentation over you
Ezekiel26:18 Now the isles tremble on the day of your fall; yea
Ezekiel26:19 “For thus says the Lord God: When I make you a city laid waste
Ezekiel26:20 then I will thrust you down with those who descend into the Pit
Ezekiel26:21 I will bring you to a dreadful end
Ezekiel27:1 The word of the Lord came to me:
Ezekiel27:2 “Now you
Ezekiel27:3 and say to Tyre
Ezekiel27:4 Your borders are in the heart of the seas; your builders made perfect your beauty.
Ezekiel27:5 They made all your planks of fir trees from Senir; they took a cedar from Lebanon to make a mast for you.
Ezekiel27:6 Of oaks of Bashan they made your oars; they made your deck of pines from the coasts of Cyprus
Ezekiel27:7 Of fine embroidered linen from Egypt was your sail
Ezekiel27:8 The inhabitants of Sidon and Arvad were your rowers; skilled men of Zemer were in you
Ezekiel27:9 The elders of Gebal and her skilled men were in you
Ezekiel27:10 “Persia and Lud and Put were in your army as your men of war; they hung the shield and helmet in you; they gave you splendor.
Ezekiel27:11 The men of Arvad and Helech were upon your walls round about
Ezekiel27:12 “Tarshish trafficked with you because of your great wealth of every kind; silver
Ezekiel27:13 Javan
Ezekiel27:14 Beth-togarmah exchanged for your wares horses
Ezekiel27:15 The men of Rhodes traded with you; many coastlands were your own special markets
Ezekiel27:16 Edom trafficked with you because of your abundant goods; they exchanged for your wares emeralds
Ezekiel27:17 Judah and the land of Israel traded with you; they exchanged for your merchandise wheat
Ezekiel27:18 Damascus trafficked with you for your abundant goods
Ezekiel27:19 and wine from Uzal they exchanged for your wares; wrought iron
Ezekiel27:20 Dedan traded with you in saddlecloths for riding.
Ezekiel27:21 Arabia and all the princes of Kedar were your favored dealers in lambs
Ezekiel27:22 The traders of Sheba and Raamah traded with you; they exchanged for your wares the best of all kinds of spices
Ezekiel27:23 Haran
Ezekiel27:24 These traded with you in choice garments
Ezekiel27:25 The ships of Tarshish traveled for you with your merchandise. “So you were filled and heavily laden in the heart of the seas.
Ezekiel27:26 Your rowers have brought you out into the high seas. The east wind has wrecked you in the heart of the seas.
Ezekiel27:27 Your riches
Ezekiel27:28 At the sound of the cry of your pilots the countryside shakes
Ezekiel27:29 and down from their ships come all that handle the oar. The mariners and all the pilots of the sea stand on the shore
Ezekiel27:30 and wail aloud over you
Ezekiel27:31 they make themselves bald for you
Ezekiel27:32 In their wailing they raise a lamentation for you
Ezekiel27:33 When your wares came from the seas
Ezekiel27:34 Now you are wrecked by the seas
Ezekiel27:35 All the inhabitants of the coastlands are appalled at you; and their kings are horribly afraid
Ezekiel27:36 The merchants among the peoples hiss at you; you have come to a dreadful end and shall be no more for ever.’ ”

Ezekiel28:2 “Son of man
Ezekiel28:3 you are indeed wiser than Daniel; no secret is hidden from you;
Ezekiel28:4 by your wisdom and your understanding you have gotten wealth for yourself
Ezekiel28:5 by your great wisdom in trade you have increased your wealth
Ezekiel28:6 therefore thus says the Lord God: “Because you consider yourself as wise as a god
Ezekiel28:7 therefore
Ezekiel28:8 They shall thrust you down into the Pit
Ezekiel28:9 Will you still say
Ezekiel28:10 You shall die the death of the uncircumcised by the hand of foreigners; for I have spoken
Ezekiel28:11 Moreover the word of the Lord came to me:
Ezekiel28:12 “Son of man
Ezekiel28:13 You were in Eden
Ezekiel28:14 With an anointed guardian cherub I placed you; you were on the holy mountain of God; in the midst of the stones of fire you walked.
Ezekiel28:15 You were blameless in your ways from the day you were created
Ezekiel28:16 In the abundance of your trade you were filled with violence
Ezekiel28:17 Your heart was proud because of your beauty; you corrupted your wisdom for the sake of your splendor. I cast you to the ground; I exposed you before kings
Ezekiel28:18 By the multitude of your iniquities
Ezekiel28:19 All who know you among the peoples are appalled at you; you have come to a dreadful end and shall be no more for ever.”

Ezekiel28:22 and say
Ezekiel28:23 for I will send pestilence into her

Ezekiel28:25 “Thus says the Lord God: When I gather the house of Israel from the peoples among whom they are scattered
Ezekiel28:26 And they shall dwell securely in it

Ezekiel30:2 “Son of man
Ezekiel30:3 For the day is near
Ezekiel30:4 A sword shall come upon Egypt
Ezekiel30:5 Ethiopia
Ezekiel30:6 “Thus says the Lord: Those who support Egypt shall fall
Ezekiel30:7 And she shall be desolated in the midst of desolated countries and her cities shall be in the midst of cities that are laid waste.
Ezekiel30:8 Then they will know that I am the Lord

Ezekiel30:10 “Thus says the Lord God: I will put an end to the wealth of Egypt

Ezekiel30:12 And I will dry up the Nile

Ezekiel45:1 “When you allot the land as a possession

Ezekiel45:3 And in the holy district you shall measure off a section twenty-five thousand cubits long and ten thousand broad
Ezekiel45:4 It shall be the holy portion of the land; it shall be for the priests
Ezekiel45:5 Another section
Ezekiel45:6 “Alongside the portion set apart as the holy district you shall assign for the possession of the city an area five thousand cubits broad
Ezekiel45:7 “And to the prince shall belong the land on both sides of the holy district and the property of the city
Ezekiel45:8 It is to be his property in Israel. And my princes shall no more oppress my people; but they shall let the house of Israel have the land according to their tribes.
Ezekiel45:9 “Thus says the Lord God: Enough
Ezekiel45:10 “You shall have just balances
Ezekiel45:11 The ephah and the bath shall be of the same measure
Ezekiel45:12 The shekel shall be twenty gerahs; five shekels shall be five shekels
Ezekiel45:13 “This is the offering which you shall make: one sixth of an ephah from each homer of wheat
Ezekiel45:14 and as the fixed portion of oil
Ezekiel45:15 and one sheep from every flock of two hundred
Ezekiel45:16 All the people of the land shall give this offering to the prince in Israel.
Ezekiel45:17 It shall be the prince's duty to furnish the burnt offerings
Ezekiel45:18 “Thus says the Lord God: In the first month
Ezekiel45:19 The priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering and put it on the doorposts of the temple
Ezekiel45:20 You shall do the same on the seventh day of the month for any one who has sinned through error or ignorance; so you shall make atonement for the temple.
Ezekiel45:21 “In the first month
Ezekiel45:22 On that day the prince shall provide for himself and all the people of the land a young bull for a sin offering.
Ezekiel45:23 And on the seven days of the festival he shall provide as a burnt offering to the Lord seven young bulls and seven rams without blemish
Ezekiel45:24 And he shall provide as a cereal offering an ephah for each bull
Ezekiel45:25 In the seventh month
Ezekiel46:1 “Thus says the Lord God: The gate of the inner court that faces east shall be shut on the six working days; but on the sabbath day it shall be opened and on the day of the new moon it shall be opened.
Ezekiel46:2 The prince shall enter by the vestibule of the gate from without
Ezekiel46:3 The people of the land shall worship at the entrance of that gate before the Lord on the sabbaths and on the new moons.

Daniel1:1 In the third year of the reign of Jehoiakim king of Judah
Daniel1:2 And the Lord gave Jehoiakim king of Judah into his hand
Daniel1:3 Then the king commanded Ashpenaz
Daniel1:4 youths without blemish
Daniel1:5 The king assigned them a daily portion of the rich food which the king ate
Daniel1:6 Among these were Daniel
Daniel1:7 And the chief of the eunuchs gave them names: Daniel he called Belteshazzar
Daniel1:8 But Daniel resolved that he would not defile himself with the king's rich food
Daniel1:9 And God gave Daniel favor and compassion in the sight of the chief of the eunuchs;
Daniel1:10 and the chief of the eunuchs said to Daniel
Daniel1:11 Then Daniel said to the steward whom the chief of the eunuchs had appointed over Daniel
Daniel1:12 “Test your servants for ten days; let us be given vegetables to eat and water to drink.
Daniel1:13 Then let our appearance and the appearance of the youths who eat the king's rich food be observed by you
Daniel1:14 So he hearkened to them in this matter
Daniel1:15 At the end of ten days it was seen that they were better in appearance and fatter in flesh than all the youths who ate the king's rich food.
Daniel1:16 So the steward took away their rich food and the wine they were to drink
Daniel1:17 As for these four youths
Daniel1:18 At the end of the time
Daniel1:19 And the king spoke with them
Daniel1:20 And in every matter of wisdom and understanding concerning which the king inquired of them
Daniel1:21 And Daniel continued until the first year of King Cyrus.

Daniel11:16 But he who comes against him shall do according to his own will

Daniel11:19 Then he shall turn his face back toward the fortresses of his own land; but he shall stumble and fall
Daniel11:20 “Then shall arise in his place one who shall send an exactor of tribute through the glory of the kingdom; but within a few days he shall be broken
Daniel11:21 In his place shall arise a contemptible person to whom royal majesty has not been given; he shall come in without warning and obtain the kingdom by flatteries.
Daniel11:22 Armies shall be utterly swept away before him and broken
Daniel11:23 And from the time that an alliance is made with him he shall act deceitfully; and he shall become strong with a small people.
Daniel11:24 Without warning he shall come into the richest parts of the province; and he shall do what neither his fathers nor his fathers' fathers have done
Daniel11:25 And he shall stir up his power and his courage against the king of the south with a great army; and the king of the south shall wage war with an exceedingly great and mighty army; but he shall not stand
Daniel11:26 Even those who eat his rich food shall be his undoing; his army shall be swept away
Daniel11:27 And as for the two kings
Daniel11:28 And he shall return to his land with great substance

Daniel11:30 For ships of Kittim shall come against him
Daniel11:31 Forces from him shall appear and profane the temple and fortress
Daniel11:32 He shall seduce with flattery those who violate the covenant; but the people who know their God shall stand firm and take action.
Daniel11:33 And those among the people who are wise shall make many understand
Daniel11:34 When they fall
Daniel11:35 and some of those who are wise shall fall
Daniel11:36 “And the king shall do according to his will; he shall exalt himself and magnify himself above every god
Daniel11:37 He shall give no heed to the gods of his fathers
Daniel11:38 He shall honor the god of fortresses instead of these; a god whom his fathers did not know he shall honor with gold and silver
Daniel11:39 He shall deal with the strongest fortresses by the help of a foreign god; those who acknowledge him he shall magnify with honor. He shall make them rulers over many and shall divide the land for a price.
Daniel11:40 “At the time of the end the king of the south shall attack him; but the king of the north shall rush upon him like a whirlwind
Daniel11:41 He shall come into the glorious land. And tens of thousands shall fall
Daniel11:42 He shall stretch out his hand against the countries
Daniel11:43 He shall become ruler of the treasures of gold and of silver
Daniel11:44 But tidings from the east and the north shall alarm him
Daniel11:45 And he shall pitch his palatial tents between the sea and the glorious holy mountain; yet he shall come to his end

Obadiah1:9 And your mighty men shall be dismayed
Obadiah1:10 For the violence done to your brother Jacob
Obadiah1:11 On the day that you stood aloof
Obadiah1:12 But you should not have gloated over the day of your brother in the day of his misfortune; you should not have rejoiced over the people of Judah in the day of their ruin; you should not have boasted in the day of distress.
Obadiah1:13 You should not have entered the gate of my people in the day of his calamity; you should not have gloated over his disaster in the day of his calamity; you should not have looted his goods in the day of his calamity.
Obadiah1:14 You should not have stood at the parting of the ways to cut off his fugitives; you should not have delivered up his survivors in the day of distress.
Obadiah1:15 For the day of the Lord is near upon all the nations. As you have done
Obadiah1:16 For as you have drunk upon my holy mountain
Obadiah1:17 But in Mount Zion there shall be those that escape
Obadiah1:18 The house of Jacob shall be a fire
Obadiah1:19 Those of the Negeb shall possess Mount Esau
Obadiah1:20 The exiles in Halah who are of the people of Israel shall possess Phoenicia as far as Zarephath; and the exiles of Jerusalem who are in Sepharad shall possess the cities of the Negeb.
Obadiah1:21 Saviors shall go up to Mount Zion to rule Mount Esau; and the kingdom shall be the Lord 's.
Jonah1:1 Now the word of the Lord came to Jonah the son of Amittai
Jonah1:2 “Arise
Jonah1:3 But Jonah rose to flee to Tarshish from the presence of the Lord. He went down to Joppa and found a ship going to Tarshish; so he paid the fare
Jonah1:4 But the Lord hurled a great wind upon the sea
Jonah1:5 Then the mariners were afraid
Jonah1:6 So the captain came and said to him
Jonah1:7 And they said to one another
Jonah1:8 Then they said to him
Jonah1:9 And he said to them
Jonah1:10 Then the men were exceedingly afraid
Jonah1:11 Then they said to him
Jonah1:12 He said to them
Jonah1:13 Nevertheless the men rowed hard to bring the ship back to land
Jonah1:14 Therefore they cried to the Lord
Jonah1:15 So they took up Jonah and threw him into the sea; and the sea ceased from its raging.
Jonah1:16 Then the men feared the Lord exceedingly
Jonah1:17 And the Lord appointed a great fish to swallow up Jonah; and Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights.
Jonah2:1 Then Jonah prayed to the Lord his God from the belly of the fish
Jonah2:2 saying

Jonah2:4 Then I said
Jonah2:5 The waters closed in over me
Jonah2:6 at the roots of the mountains. I went down to the land whose bars closed upon me for ever; yet thou didst bring up my life from the Pit
Jonah2:7 When my soul fainted within me
Jonah2:8 Those who pay regard to vain idols forsake their true loyalty.
Jonah2:9 But I with the voice of thanksgiving will sacrifice to thee; what I have vowed I will pay. Deliverance belongs to the Lord!”
Jonah2:10 And the Lord spoke to the fish

Micah1:1 The word of the Lord that came to Micah of Moresheth in the days of Jotham
Micah1:2 Hear
Micah1:3 For behold
Micah1:4 And the mountains will melt under him and the valleys will be cleft
Micah1:5 All this is for the transgression of Jacob and for the sins of the house of Israel. What is the transgression of Jacob? Is it not Samaria? And what is the sin of the house of Judah? Is it not Jerusalem?
Micah1:6 Therefore I will make Samaria a heap in the open country
Micah1:7 All her images shall be beaten to pieces
Micah1:8 For this I will lament and wail; I will go stripped and naked; I will make lamentation like the jackals
Micah1:9 For her wound is incurable; and it has come to Judah
Micah1:10 Tell it not in Gath
Micah1:11 Pass on your way
Micah1:12 For the inhabitants of Maroth wait anxiously for good
Micah1:13 Harness the steeds to the chariots
Micah1:14 Therefore you shall give parting gifts to Moresheth-gath; the houses of Achzib shall be a deceitful thing to the kings of Israel.

Micah1:16 Make yourselves bald and cut off your hair
Micah2:1 Woe to those who devise wickedness and work evil upon their beds! When the morning dawns

Micah2:5 Therefore you will have none to cast the line by lot in the assembly of the Lord.

Micah2:11 If a man should go about and utter wind and lies
Micah2:12 I will surely gather all of you
Micah2:13 He who opens the breach will go up before them; they will break through and pass the gate

Micah3:9 Hear this
Micah3:10 who build Zion with blood and Jerusalem with wrong.
Micah3:11 Its heads give judgment for a bribe
Micah3:12 Therefore because of you Zion shall be plowed as a field; Jerusalem shall become a heap of ruins

Nahum1:1 An oracle concerning Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum of Elkosh.
Nahum1:2 The Lord is a jealous God and avenging
Nahum1:3 The Lord is slow to anger and of great might
Nahum1:4 He rebukes the sea and makes it dry
Nahum1:5 The mountains quake before him
Nahum1:6 Who can stand before his indignation? Who can endure the heat of his anger? His wrath is poured out like fire
Nahum1:7 The Lord is good
Nahum1:8 But with an overflowing flood he will make a full end of his adversaries
Nahum1:9 What do you plot against the Lord? He will make a full end; he will not take vengeance twice on his foes.
Nahum1:10 Like entangled thorns they are consumed
Nahum1:11 Did one not come out from you
Nahum1:12 Thus says the Lord
Nahum1:13 And now I will break his yoke from off you and will burst your bonds asunder.”
Nahum1:14 The Lord has given commandment about you: “No more shall your name be perpetuated; from the house of your gods I will cut off the graven image and the molten image. I will make your grave
Nahum1:15 Behold
Nahum2:1 The shatterer has come up against you. Man the ramparts; watch the road; gird your loins; collect all your strength.
Nahum2:2 (For the Lord is restoring the majesty of Jacob as the majesty of Israel
Nahum2:3 The shield of his mighty men is red
Nahum2:4 The chariots rage in the streets
Nahum2:5 The officers are summoned
Nahum2:6 The river gates are opened
Nahum2:7 its mistress is stripped
Nahum2:8 Nineveh is like a pool whose waters run away. “Halt! Halt!” they cry; but none turns back.
Nahum2:9 Plunder the silver
Nahum2:10 Desolate! Desolation and ruin! Hearts faint and knees tremble
Nahum2:11 Where is the lions' den
Nahum2:12 The lion tore enough for his whelps and strangled prey for his lionesses; he filled his caves with prey and his dens with torn flesh.
Nahum2:13 Behold

Nahum3:3 Horsemen charging
Nahum3:4 And all for the countless harlotries of the harlot
Nahum3:5 Behold
Nahum3:6 I will throw filth at you and treat you with contempt
Nahum3:7 And all who look on you will shrink from you and say
Nahum3:8 Are you better than Thebes that sat by the Nile
Nahum3:9 Ethiopia was her strength
Nahum3:10 Yet she was carried away
Nahum3:11 You also will be drunken
Nahum3:12 All your fortresses are like fig trees with first-ripe figs— if shaken they fall into the mouth of the eater.
Nahum3:13 Behold
Nahum3:14 Draw water for the siege
Nahum3:15 There will the fire devour you
Nahum3:16 You increased your merchants more than the stars of the heavens. The locust spreads its wings and flies away.
Nahum3:17 Your princes are like grasshoppers
Nahum3:18 Your shepherds are asleep
Nahum3:19 There is no assuaging your hurt
Habakkuk1:1 The oracle of God which Habakkuk the prophet saw.
Habakkuk1:2 O Lord
Habakkuk1:3 Why dost thou make me see wrongs and look upon trouble? Destruction and violence are before me; strife and contention arise.
Habakkuk1:4 So the law is slacked and justice never goes forth. For the wicked surround the righteous
Habakkuk1:5 Look among the nations
Habakkuk1:6 For lo
Habakkuk1:7 Dread and terrible are they; their justice and dignity proceed from themselves.
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses are swifter than leopards
Habakkuk1:9 They all come for violence; terror of them goes before them. They gather captives like sand.
Habakkuk1:10 At kings they scoff
Habakkuk1:11 Then they sweep by like the wind and go on
Habakkuk1:12 Art thou not from everlasting
Habakkuk1:13 Thou who art of purer eyes than to behold evil and canst not look on wrong
Habakkuk1:14 For thou makest men like the fish of the sea
Habakkuk1:15 He brings all of them up with a hook
Habakkuk1:16 Therefore he sacrifices to his net and burns incense to his seine; for by them he lives in luxury
Habakkuk1:17 Is he then to keep on emptying his net
Habakkuk2:1 I will take my stand to watch
Habakkuk2:2 And the Lord answered me: “Write the vision; make it plain upon tablets
Habakkuk2:3 For still the vision awaits its time; it hastens to the end—it will not lie. If it seem slow
Habakkuk2:4 Behold
Habakkuk2:5 Moreover
Habakkuk2:6 Shall not all these take up their taunt against him
Habakkuk2:7 Will not your debtors suddenly arise
Habakkuk2:8 Because you have plundered many nations
Habakkuk2:9 Woe to him who gets evil gain for his house
Habakkuk2:10 You have devised shame to your house by cutting off many peoples; you have forfeited your life.
Habakkuk2:11 For the stone will cry out from the wall
Habakkuk2:12 Woe to him who builds a town with blood
Habakkuk2:13 Behold
Habakkuk2:14 For the earth will be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the Lord
Habakkuk2:15 Woe to him who makes his neighbors drink of the cup of his wrath
Habakkuk2:16 You will be sated with contempt instead of glory. Drink
Habakkuk2:17 The violence done to Lebanon will overwhelm you; the destruction of the beasts will terrify you
Habakkuk2:18 What profit is an idol when its maker has shaped it
Habakkuk2:19 Woe to him who says to a wooden thing
Habakkuk2:20 But the Lord is in his holy temple; let all the earth keep silence before him.

Habakkuk3:18 yet I will rejoice in the Lord

Matthew24:1 Jesus left the temple and was going away
Matthew24:2 But he answered them
Matthew24:3 As he sat on the Mount of Olives
Matthew24:4 And Jesus answered them
Matthew24:5 For many will come in my name
Matthew24:6 And you will hear of wars and rumors of wars; see that you are not alarmed; for this must take place
Matthew24:7 For nation will rise against nation
Matthew24:8 all this is but the beginning of the birth-pangs.
Matthew24:9 “Then they will deliver you up to tribulation
Matthew24:10 And then many will fall away
Matthew24:11 And many false prophets will arise and lead many astray.
Matthew24:12 And because wickedness is multiplied
Matthew24:13 But he who endures to the end will be saved.
Matthew24:14 And this gospel of the kingdom will be preached throughout the whole world
Matthew24:15 “So when you see the desolating sacrilege spoken of by the prophet Daniel
Matthew24:16 then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains;
Matthew24:17 let him who is on the housetop not go down to take what is in his house;
Matthew24:18 and let him who is in the field not turn back to take his mantle.
Matthew24:19 And alas for those who are with child and for those who give suck in those days!
Matthew24:20 Pray that your flight may not be in winter or on a sabbath.
Matthew24:21 For then there will be great tribulation
Matthew24:22 And if those days had not been shortened
Matthew24:23 Then if any one says to you
Matthew24:24 For false Christs and false prophets will arise and show great signs and wonders
Matthew24:25 Lo
Matthew24:26 So
Matthew24:27 For as the lightning comes from the east and shines as far as the west
Matthew24:28 Wherever the body is
Matthew24:29 “Immediately after the tribulation of those days the sun will be darkened
Matthew24:30 then will appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven
Matthew24:31 and he will send out his angels with a loud trumpet call
Matthew24:32 “From the fig tree learn its lesson: as soon as its branch becomes tender and puts forth its leaves
Matthew24:33 So also
Matthew24:34 Truly
Matthew24:35 Heaven and earth will pass away
Matthew24:36 “But of that day and hour no one knows
Matthew24:37 As were the days of Noah
Matthew24:38 For as in those days before the flood they were eating and drinking
Matthew24:39 and they did not know until the flood came and swept them all away
Matthew24:40 Then two men will be in the field; one is taken and one is left.
Matthew24:41 Two women will be grinding at the mill; one is taken and one is left.
Matthew24:42 Watch therefore
Matthew24:43 But know this
Matthew24:44 Therefore you also must be ready; for the Son of man is coming at an hour you do not expect.
Matthew24:45 “Who then is the faithful and wise servant
Matthew24:46 Blessed is that servant whom his master when he comes will find so doing.
Matthew24:47 Truly
Matthew24:48 But if that wicked servant says to himself
Matthew24:49 and begins to beat his fellow servants
Matthew24:50 the master of that servant will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour he does not know
Matthew24:51 and will punish him
Matthew25:1 “Then the kingdom of heaven shall be compared to ten maidens who took their lamps and went to meet the bridegroom.
Matthew25:2 Five of them were foolish
Matthew25:3 For when the foolish took their lamps
Matthew25:4 but the wise took flasks of oil with their lamps.
Matthew25:5 As the bridegroom was delayed
Matthew25:6 But at midnight there was a cry
Matthew25:7 Then all those maidens rose and trimmed their lamps.
Matthew25:8 And the foolish said to the wise
Matthew25:9 But the wise replied
Matthew25:10 And while they went to buy
Matthew25:11 Afterward the other maidens came also
Matthew25:12 But he replied
Matthew25:13 Watch therefore
Matthew25:14 “For it will be as when a man going on a journey called his servants and entrusted to them his property;
Matthew25:15 to one he gave five talents
Matthew25:16 He who had received the five talents went at once and traded with them; and he made five talents more.
Matthew25:17 So also
Matthew25:18 But he who had received the one talent went and dug in the ground and hid his master's money.
Matthew25:19 Now after a long time the master of those servants came and settled accounts with them.
Matthew25:20 And he who had received the five talents came forward
Matthew25:21 His master said to him
Matthew25:22 And he also who had the two talents came forward
Matthew25:23 His master said to him
Matthew25:24 He also who had received the one talent came forward
Matthew25:25 so I was afraid
Matthew25:26 But his master answered him
Matthew25:27 Then you ought to have invested my money with the bankers
Matthew25:28 So take the talent from him
Matthew25:29 For to every one who has will more be given
Matthew25:30 And cast the worthless servant into the outer darkness; there men will weep and gnash their teeth.’
Matthew25:31 “When the Son of man comes in his glory
Matthew25:32 Before him will be gathered all the nations
Matthew25:33 and he will place the sheep at his right hand
Matthew25:34 Then the King will say to those at his right hand
Matthew25:35 for I was hungry and you gave me food
Matthew25:36 I was naked and you clothed me
Matthew25:37 Then the righteous will answer him
Matthew25:38 And when did we see thee a stranger and welcome thee
Matthew25:39 And when did we see thee sick or in prison and visit thee?’
Matthew25:40 And the King will answer them
Matthew25:41 Then he will say to those at his left hand
Matthew25:42 for I was hungry and you gave me no food
Matthew25:43 I was a stranger and you did not welcome me
Matthew25:44 Then they also will answer
Matthew25:45 Then he will answer them
Matthew25:46 And they will go away into eternal punishment

Mark1:32 That evening

Mark4:2 And he taught them many things in parables
Mark4:3 “Listen! A sower went out to sow.
Mark4:4 And as he sowed
Mark4:5 Other seed fell on rocky ground
Mark4:6 and when the sun rose it was scorched
Mark4:7 Other seed fell among thorns and the thorns grew up and choked it
Mark4:8 And other seeds fell into good soil and brought forth grain
Mark4:9 And he said
Mark4:10 And when he was alone
Mark4:11 And he said to them
Mark4:12 so that they may indeed see but not perceive
Mark4:13 And he said to them

Mark4:17 and they have no root in themselves

Mark4:19 but the cares of the world

Mark4:22 For there is nothing hid

John3:19 And this is the judgment

John3:24 For John had not yet been put in prison.

John3:34 For he whom God has sent utters the words of God

John3:36 He who believes in the Son has eternal life; he who does not obey the Son shall not see life

John5:37 And the Father who sent me has himself borne witness to me. His voice you have never heard
John5:38 and you do not have his word abiding in you
John5:39 You search the scriptures
John5:40 yet you refuse to come to me that you may have life.

John6:61 But Jesus

John12:2 There they made him a supper; Martha served
John12:3 Mary took a pound of costly ointment of pure nard and anointed the feet of Jesus and wiped his feet with her hair; and the house was filled with the fragrance of the ointment.
John12:4 But Judas Iscariot
John12:5 “Why was this ointment not sold for three hundred denarii and given to the poor?”
John12:6 This he said
John12:7 Jesus said
John12:8 The poor you always have with you
John12:9 When the great crowd of the Jews learned that he was there
John12:10 So the chief priests planned to put Lazarus also to death
John12:11 because on account of him many of the Jews were going away and believing in Jesus.
John12:12 The next day a great crowd who had come to the feast heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.
John12:13 So they took branches of palm trees and went out to meet him
John12:14 And Jesus found a young ass and sat upon it; as it is written
John12:15 “Fear not
John12:16 His disciples did not understand this at first; but when Jesus was glorified
John12:17 The crowd that had been with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead bore witness.
John12:18 The reason why the crowd went to meet him was that they heard he had done this sign.
John12:19 The Pharisees then said to one another
John12:20 Now among those who went up to worship at the feast were some Greeks.
John12:21 So these came to Philip
John12:22 Philip went and told Andrew; Andrew went with Philip and they told Jesus.
John12:23 And Jesus answered them
John12:24 Truly
John12:25 He who loves his life loses it

John12:27 “Now is my soul troubled. And what shall I say? ‘Father
John12:28 Father
John12:29 The crowd standing by heard it and said that it had thundered. Others said
John12:30 Jesus answered
John12:31 Now is the judgment of this world
John12:32 and I
John12:33 He said this to show by what death he was to die.
John12:34 The crowd answered him
John12:35 Jesus said to them
John12:36 While you have the light
John12:37 Though he had done so many signs before them
John12:38 it was that the word spoken by the prophet Isaiah might be fulfilled: “Lord
John12:39 Therefore they could not believe. For Isaiah again said
John12:40 “He has blinded their eyes and hardened their heart
John12:41 Isaiah said this because he saw his glory and spoke of him.
John12:42 Nevertheless many even of the authorities believed in him
John12:43 for they loved the praise of men more than the praise of God.
