Acts7:19 He dealt craftily with our race and forced our fathers to expose their infants
Acts7:20 At this time Moses was born
Acts7:21 and when he was exposed
Acts7:22 And Moses was instructed in all the wisdom of the Egyptians
Acts7:23 “When he was forty years old
Acts7:24 And seeing one of them being wronged
Acts7:25 He supposed that his brethren understood that God was giving them deliverance by his hand
Acts7:26 And on the following day he appeared to them as they were quarreling and would have reconciled them
Acts7:27 But the man who was wronging his neighbor thrust him aside
Acts7:28 Do you want to kill me as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?’
Acts7:29 At this retort Moses fled
Acts7:30 “Now when forty years had passed
Acts7:31 When Moses saw it he wondered at the sight; and as he drew near to look
Acts7:32 ‘I am the God of your fathers
Acts7:33 And the Lord said to him
Acts7:34 I have surely seen the ill-treatment of my people that are in Egypt and heard their groaning
Acts7:35 “This Moses whom they refused
Acts7:36 He led them out
Acts7:37 This is the Moses who said to the Israelites
Acts7:38 This is he who was in the congregation in the wilderness with the angel who spoke to him at Mount Sinai
Acts7:39 Our fathers refused to obey him
Acts7:40 saying to Aaron
Acts7:41 And they made a calf in those days
Acts7:42 But God turned and gave them over to worship the host of heaven
Acts7:43 And you took up the tent of Moloch
Acts7:44 “Our fathers had the tent of witness in the wilderness
Acts7:45 Our fathers in turn brought it in with Joshua when they dispossessed the nations which God thrust out before our fathers. So it was until the days of David
Acts7:46 who found favor in the sight of God and asked leave to find a habitation for the God of Jacob.
Acts7:47 But it was Solomon who built a house for him.
Acts7:48 Yet the Most High does not dwell in houses made with hands; as the prophet says
Acts7:49 ‘Heaven is my throne
Acts7:50 Did not my hand make all these things?’
Acts7:51 “You stiff-necked people
Acts7:52 Which of the prophets did not your fathers persecute? And they killed those who announced beforehand the coming of the Righteous One
Acts7:53 you who received the law as delivered by angels and did not keep it.”
Acts7:54 Now when they heard these things they were enraged
Acts7:55 But he
Acts7:56 and he said
Acts7:57 But they cried out with a loud voice and stopped their ears and rushed together upon him.
Acts7:58 Then they cast him out of the city and stoned him; and the witnesses laid down their garments at the feet of a young man named Saul.
Acts7:59 And as they were stoning Stephen
Acts7:60 And he knelt down and cried with a loud voice
Acts8:1 And Saul was consenting to his death. And on that day a great persecution arose against the church in Jerusalem; and they were all scattered throughout the region of Judea and Samaria

Acts8:3 But Saul was ravaging the church

Acts8:7 For unclean spirits came out of many who were possessed
Acts8:8 So there was much joy in that city.
Acts8:9 But there was a man named Simon who had previously practiced magic in the city and amazed the nation of Samaria

Acts8:11 And they gave heed to him
Acts8:12 But when they believed Philip as he preached good news about the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ

Acts18:20 When they asked him to stay for a longer period

Acts18:25 He had been instructed in the way of the Lord; and being fervent in spirit
Acts18:26 He began to speak boldly in the synagogue; but when Priscilla and Aquila heard him

Acts18:28 for he powerfully confuted the Jews in public

Acts19:3 And he said
Acts19:4 And Paul said
Acts19:5 On hearing this
Acts19:6 And when Paul had laid his hands upon them

Acts19:9 but when some were stubborn and disbelieved
Acts19:10 This continued for two years
Acts19:11 And God did extraordinary miracles by the hands of Paul
Acts19:12 so that handkerchiefs or aprons were carried away from his body to the sick
Acts19:13 Then some of the itinerant Jewish exorcists undertook to pronounce the name of the Lord Jesus over those who had evil spirits
Acts19:14 Seven sons of a Jewish high priest named Sceva were doing this.
Acts19:15 But the evil spirit answered them
Acts19:16 And the man in whom the evil spirit was leaped on them
Acts19:17 And this became known to all residents of Ephesus
Acts19:18 Many also of those who were now believers came
Acts19:19 And a number of those who practiced magic arts brought their books together and burned them in the sight of all; and they counted the value of them and found it came to fifty thousand pieces of silver.
Acts19:20 So the word of the Lord grew and prevailed mightily.
Acts19:21 Now after these events Paul resolved in the Spirit to pass through Macedonia and Achaia and go to Jerusalem
Acts19:22 And having sent into Macedonia two of his helpers
Acts19:23 About that time there arose no little stir concerning the Way.
Acts19:24 For a man named Demetrius
Acts19:25 These he gathered together
Acts19:26 And you see and hear that not only at Ephesus but almost throughout all Asia this Paul has persuaded and turned away a considerable company of people
Acts19:27 And there is danger not only that this trade of ours may come into disrepute but also that the temple of the great goddess Artemis may count for nothing
Acts19:28 When they heard this they were enraged
Acts19:29 So the city was filled with the confusion; and they rushed together into the theater

Acts19:39 But if you seek anything further
Acts19:40 For we are in danger of being charged with rioting today

Acts20:1 After the uproar ceased

Acts20:18 And when they came to him
Acts20:19 serving the Lord with all humility and with tears and with trials which befell me through the plots of the Jews;

Acts20:25 And now

Acts20:27 for I did not shrink from declaring to you the whole counsel of God.
Acts20:28 Take heed to yourselves and to all the flock

Acts20:30 and from among your own selves will arise men speaking perverse things
Acts20:31 Therefore be alert
Acts20:32 And now I commend you to God and to the word of his grace
Acts20:33 I coveted no one's silver or gold or apparel.
Acts20:34 You yourselves know that these hands ministered to my necessities
Acts20:35 In all things I have shown you that by so toiling one must help the weak

Acts26:1 Agrippa said to Paul

Acts26:7 to which our twelve tribes hope to attain
Acts26:8 Why is it thought incredible by any of you that God raises the dead?

Acts27:1 And when it was decided that we should sail for Italy

Acts27:4 And putting to sea from there we sailed under the lee of Cyprus
Acts27:5 And when we had sailed across the sea which is off Cilicia and Pamphylia
Acts27:6 There the centurion found a ship of Alexandria sailing for Italy
Acts27:7 We sailed slowly for a number of days
Acts27:8 Coasting along it with difficulty
Acts27:9 As much time had been lost
Acts27:10 saying
Acts27:11 But the centurion paid more attention to the captain and to the owner of the ship than to what Paul said.
Acts27:12 And because the harbor was not suitable to winter in
Acts27:13 And when the south wind blew gently
Acts27:14 But soon a tempestuous wind
Acts27:15 and when the ship was caught and could not face the wind
Acts27:16 And running under the lee of a small island called Cauda
Acts27:17 after hoisting it up
Acts27:18 As we were violently storm-tossed
Acts27:19 and the third day they cast out with their own hands the tackle of the ship.
Acts27:20 And when neither sun nor stars appeared for many a day
Acts27:21 As they had been long without food
Acts27:22 I now bid you take heart; for there will be no loss of life among you
Acts27:23 For this very night there stood by me an angel of the God to whom I belong and whom I worship
Acts27:24 and he said
Acts27:25 So take heart
Acts27:26 But we shall have to run on some island.”
Acts27:27 When the fourteenth night had come
Acts27:28 So they sounded and found twenty fathoms; a little farther on they sounded again and found fifteen fathoms.
Acts27:29 And fearing that we might run on the rocks
Acts27:30 And as the sailors were seeking to escape from the ship
Acts27:31 Paul said to the centurion and the soldiers
Acts27:32 Then the soldiers cut away the ropes of the boat
Acts27:33 As day was about to dawn
Acts27:34 Therefore I urge you to take some food; it will give you strength
Acts27:35 And when he had said this
Acts27:36 Then they all were encouraged and ate some food themselves.
Acts27:37 (We were in all two hundred and seventy-six persons in the ship.)
Acts27:38 And when they had eaten enough
Acts27:39 Now when it was day
Acts27:40 So they cast off the anchors and left them in the sea
Acts27:41 But striking a shoal they ran the vessel aground; the bow stuck and remained immovable
Acts27:42 The soldiers' plan was to kill the prisoners
Acts27:43 but the centurion
Acts27:44 and the rest on planks or on pieces of the ship. And so it was that all escaped to land.

Acts28:6 They waited

Acts28:29

Acts28:31 preaching the kingdom of God and teaching about the Lord Jesus Christ quite openly and unhindered.
Romans1:1 Paul
Romans1:2 which he promised beforehand through his prophets in the holy scriptures
Romans1:3 the gospel concerning his Son
Romans1:4 and designated Son of God in power according to the Spirit of holiness by his resurrection from the dead
Romans1:5 through whom we have received grace and apostleship to bring about the obedience of faith for the sake of his name among all the nations
Romans1:6 including yourselves who are called to belong to Jesus Christ;
Romans1:7 To all God's beloved in Rome
Romans1:8 First
Romans1:9 For God is my witness
Romans1:10 asking that somehow by God's will I may now at last succeed in coming to you.
Romans1:11 For I long to see you
Romans1:12 that is
Romans1:13 I want you to know
Romans1:14 I am under obligation both to Greeks and to barbarians
Romans1:15 so I am eager to preach the gospel to you also who are in Rome.
Romans1:16 For I am not ashamed of the gospel: it is the power of God for salvation to every one who has faith
Romans1:17 For in it the righteousness of God is revealed through faith for faith; as it is written
Romans1:18 For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and wickedness of men who by their wickedness suppress the truth.
Romans1:19 For what can be known about God is plain to them
Romans1:20 Ever since the creation of the world his invisible nature
Romans1:21 for although they knew God they did not honor him as God or give thanks to him
Romans1:22 Claiming to be wise
Romans1:23 and exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images resembling mortal man or birds or animals or reptiles.
Romans1:24 Therefore God gave them up in the lusts of their hearts to impurity
Romans1:25 because they exchanged the truth about God for a lie and worshiped and served the creature rather than the Creator
Romans1:26 For this reason God gave them up to dishonorable passions. Their women exchanged natural relations for unnatural
Romans1:27 and the men likewise gave up natural relations with women and were consumed with passion for one another
Romans1:28 And since they did not see fit to acknowledge God
Romans1:29 They were filled with all manner of wickedness
Romans1:30 slanderers
Romans1:31 foolish
Romans1:32 Though they know God's decree that those who do such things deserve to die
Romans2:1 Therefore you have no excuse
Romans2:2 We know that the judgment of God rightly falls upon those who do such things.
Romans2:3 Do you suppose
Romans2:4 Or do you presume upon the riches of his kindness and forbearance and patience? Do you not know that God's kindness is meant to lead you to repentance?
Romans2:5 But by your hard and impenitent heart you are storing up wrath for yourself on the day of wrath when God's righteous judgment will be revealed.
Romans2:6 For he will render to every man according to his works:
Romans2:7 to those who by patience in well-doing seek for glory and honor and immortality
Romans2:8 but for those who are factious and do not obey the truth
Romans2:9 There will be tribulation and distress for every human being who does evil
Romans2:10 but glory and honor and peace for every one who does good
Romans2:11 For God shows no partiality.
Romans2:12 All who have sinned without the law will also perish without the law
Romans2:13 For it is not the hearers of the law who are righteous before God
Romans2:14 When Gentiles who have not the law do by nature what the law requires
Romans2:15 They show that what the law requires is written on their hearts
Romans2:16 on that day when
Romans2:17 But if you call yourself a Jew and rely upon the law and boast of your relation to God
Romans2:18 and know his will and approve what is excellent
Romans2:19 and if you are sure that you are a guide to the blind
Romans2:20 a corrector of the foolish
Romans2:21 you then who teach others
Romans2:22 You who say that one must not commit adultery
Romans2:23 You who boast in the law
Romans2:24 For
Romans2:25 Circumcision indeed is of value if you obey the law; but if you break the law
Romans2:26 So
Romans2:27 Then those who are physically uncircumcised but keep the law will condemn you who have the written code and circumcision but break the law.
Romans2:28 For he is not a real Jew who is one outwardly
Romans2:29 He is a Jew who is one inwardly
Romans3:1 Then what advantage has the Jew? Or what is the value of circumcision?
Romans3:2 Much in every way. To begin with
Romans3:3 What if some were unfaithful? Does their faithlessness nullify the faithfulness of God?
Romans3:4 By no means! Let God be true though every man be false
Romans3:5 But if our wickedness serves to show the justice of God
Romans3:6 By no means! For then how could God judge the world?
Romans3:7 But if through my falsehood God's truthfulness abounds to his glory
Romans3:8 And why not do evil that good may come?—as some people slanderously charge us with saying. Their condemnation is just.
Romans3:9 What then? Are we Jews any better off? No
Romans3:10 as it is written: “None is righteous
Romans3:11 no one understands
Romans3:12 All have turned aside
Romans3:13 “Their throat is an open grave
Romans3:14 “Their mouth is full of curses and bitterness.”
Romans3:15 “Their feet are swift to shed blood
Romans3:16 in their paths are ruin and misery
Romans3:17 and the way of peace they do not know.”
Romans3:18 “There is no fear of God before their eyes.”
Romans3:19 Now we know that whatever the law says it speaks to those who are under the law
Romans3:20 For no human being will be justified in his sight by works of the law
Romans3:21 But now the righteousness of God has been manifested apart from law
Romans3:22 the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ for all who believe. For there is no distinction;
Romans3:23 since all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God
Romans3:24 they are justified by his grace as a gift
Romans3:25 whom God put forward as an expiation by his blood
Romans3:26 it was to prove at the present time that he himself is righteous and that he justifies him who has faith in Jesus.
Romans3:27 Then what becomes of our boasting? It is excluded. On what principle? On the principle of works? No
Romans3:28 For we hold that a man is justified by faith apart from works of law.
Romans3:29 Or is God the God of Jews only? Is he not the God of Gentiles also? Yes
Romans3:30 since God is one; and he will justify the circumcised on the ground of their faith and the uncircumcised through their faith.
Romans3:31 Do we then overthrow the law by this faith? By no means! On the contrary
Romans4:1 What then shall we say about Abraham
Romans4:2 For if Abraham was justified by works
Romans4:3 For what does the scripture say? “Abraham believed God
Romans4:4 Now to one who works

Romans4:12 and likewise the father of the circumcised who are not merely circumcised but also follow the example of the faith which our father Abraham had before he was circumcised.

Romans6:1 What shall we say then? Are we to continue in sin that grace may abound?
Romans6:2 By no means! How can we who died to sin still live in it?
Romans6:3 Do you not know that all of us who have been baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?
Romans6:4 We were buried therefore with him by baptism into death
Romans6:5 For if we have been united with him in a death like his
Romans6:6 We know that our old self was crucified with him so that the sinful body might be destroyed
Romans6:7 For he who has died is freed from sin.
Romans6:8 But if we have died with Christ
Romans6:9 For we know that Christ being raised from the dead will never die again; death no longer has dominion over him.
Romans6:10 The death he died he died to sin
Romans6:11 So you also must consider yourselves dead to sin and alive to God in Christ Jesus.
Romans6:12 Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal bodies
Romans6:13 Do not yield your members to sin as instruments of wickedness
Romans6:14 For sin will have no dominion over you

1Corinthians2:13 And we impart this in words not taught by human wisdom but taught by the Spirit

1Corinthians2:15 The spiritual man judges all things
1Corinthians2:16 “For who has known the mind of the Lord so as to instruct him?” But we have the mind of Christ.

1Corinthians5:6 Your boasting is not good. Do you not know that a little leaven leavens the whole lump?
1Corinthians5:7 Cleanse out the old leaven that you may be a new lump
1Corinthians5:8 Let us
1Corinthians5:9 I wrote to you in my letter not to associate with immoral men;
1Corinthians5:10 not at all meaning the immoral of this world
1Corinthians5:11 But rather I wrote to you not to associate with any one who bears the name of brother if he is guilty of immorality or greed
1Corinthians5:12 For what have I to do with judging outsiders? Is it not those inside the church whom you are to judge?
1Corinthians5:13 God judges those outside. “Drive out the wicked person from among you.”
1Corinthians6:1 When one of you has a grievance against a brother
1Corinthians6:2 Do you not know that the saints will judge the world? And if the world is to be judged by you
1Corinthians6:3 Do you not know that we are to judge angels? How much more
1Corinthians6:4 If then you have such cases
1Corinthians6:5 I say this to your shame. Can it be that there is no man among you wise enough to decide between members of the brotherhood
1Corinthians6:6 but brother goes to law against brother
1Corinthians6:7 To have lawsuits at all with one another is defeat for you. Why not rather suffer wrong? Why not rather be defrauded?
1Corinthians6:8 But you yourselves wrong and defraud
1Corinthians6:9 Do you not know that the unrighteous will not inherit the kingdom of God? Do not be deceived; neither the immoral
1Corinthians6:10 nor thieves
1Corinthians6:11 And such were some of you. But you were washed
1Corinthians6:12 “All things are lawful for me
1Corinthians6:13 “Food is meant for the stomach and the stomach for food”—and God will destroy both one and the other. The body is not meant for immorality
1Corinthians6:14 And God raised the Lord and will also raise us up by his power.
1Corinthians6:15 Do you not know that your bodies are members of Christ? Shall I therefore take the members of Christ and make them members of a prostitute? Never!
1Corinthians6:16 Do you not know that he who joins himself to a prostitute becomes one body with her? For
1Corinthians6:17 But he who is united to the Lord becomes one spirit with him.
1Corinthians6:18 Shun immorality. Every other sin which a man commits is outside the body; but the immoral man sins against his own body.
1Corinthians6:19 Do you not know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit within you
1Corinthians6:20 you were bought with a price. So glorify God in your body.
1Corinthians7:1 Now concerning the matters about which you wrote. It is well for a man not to touch a woman.
1Corinthians7:2 But because of the temptation to immorality
1Corinthians7:3 The husband should give to his wife her conjugal rights
1Corinthians7:4 For the wife does not rule over her own body
1Corinthians7:5 Do not refuse one another except perhaps by agreement for a season
1Corinthians7:6 I say this by way of concession
1Corinthians7:7 I wish that all were as I myself am. But each has his own special gift from God
1Corinthians7:8 To the unmarried and the widows I say that it is well for them to remain single as I do.
1Corinthians7:9 But if they cannot exercise self-control
1Corinthians7:10 To the married I give charge
1Corinthians7:11 (but if she does
1Corinthians7:12 To the rest I say
1Corinthians7:13 If any woman has a husband who is an unbeliever
1Corinthians7:14 For the unbelieving husband is consecrated through his wife
1Corinthians7:15 But if the unbelieving partner desires to separate
1Corinthians7:16 Wife
1Corinthians7:17 Only
1Corinthians7:18 Was any one at the time of his call already circumcised? Let him not seek to remove the marks of circumcision. Was any one at the time of his call uncircumcised? Let him not seek circumcision.
1Corinthians7:19 For neither circumcision counts for anything nor uncircumcision
1Corinthians7:20 Every one should remain in the state in which he was called.
1Corinthians7:21 Were you a slave when called? Never mind. But if you can gain your freedom
1Corinthians7:22 For he who was called in the Lord as a slave is a freedman of the Lord. Likewise he who was free when called is a slave of Christ.
1Corinthians7:23 You were bought with a price; do not become slaves of men.
1Corinthians7:24 So
1Corinthians7:25 Now concerning the unmarried
1Corinthians7:26 I think that in view of the present distress it is well for a person to remain as he is.
1Corinthians7:27 Are you bound to a wife? Do not seek to be free. Are you free from a wife? Do not seek marriage.
1Corinthians7:28 But if you marry
1Corinthians7:29 I mean
1Corinthians7:30 and those who mourn as though they were not mourning
1Corinthians7:31 and those who deal with the world as though they had no dealings with it. For the form of this world is passing away.
1Corinthians7:32 I want you to be free from anxieties. The unmarried man is anxious about the affairs of the Lord
1Corinthians7:33 but the married man is anxious about worldly affairs
1Corinthians7:34 and his interests are divided. And the unmarried woman or girl is anxious about the affairs of the Lord
1Corinthians7:35 I say this for your own benefit
1Corinthians7:36 If any one thinks that he is not behaving properly toward his betrothed
1Corinthians7:37 But whoever is firmly established in his heart
1Corinthians7:38 So that he who marries his betrothed does well; and he who refrains from marriage will do better.
1Corinthians7:39 A wife is bound to her husband as long as he lives. If the husband dies
1Corinthians7:40 But in my judgment she is happier if she remains as she is. And I think that I have the Spirit of God.
1Corinthians8:1 Now concerning food offered to idols: we know that “all of us possess knowledge.” “Knowledge” puffs up
1Corinthians8:2 If any one imagines that he knows something
1Corinthians8:3 But if one loves God
1Corinthians8:4 Hence
1Corinthians8:5 For although there may be so-called gods in heaven or on earth—as indeed there are many “gods” and many “lords” —

1Corinthians8:8 Food will not commend us to God. We are no worse off if we do not eat

1Corinthians8:11 And so by your knowledge this weak man is destroyed

1Corinthians9:1 Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are not you my workmanship in the Lord?
1Corinthians9:2 If to others I am not an apostle
1Corinthians9:3 This is my defense to those who would examine me.
1Corinthians9:4 Do we not have the right to our food and drink?
1Corinthians9:5 Do we not have the right to be accompanied by a wife
1Corinthians9:6 Or is it only Barnabas and I who have no right to refrain from working for a living?
1Corinthians9:7 Who serves as a soldier at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard without eating any of its fruit? Who tends a flock without getting some of the milk?
1Corinthians9:8 Do I say this on human authority? Does not the law say the same?
1Corinthians9:9 For it is written in the law of Moses
1Corinthians9:10 Does he not speak entirely for our sake? It was written for our sake
1Corinthians9:11 If we have sown spiritual good among you
1Corinthians9:12 If others share this rightful claim upon you
1Corinthians9:13 Do you not know that those who are employed in the temple service get their food from the temple
1Corinthians9:14 In the same way
1Corinthians9:15 But I have made no use of any of these rights
1Corinthians9:16 For if I preach the gospel
1Corinthians9:17 For if I do this of my own will
1Corinthians9:18 What then is my reward? Just this: that in my preaching I may make the gospel free of charge
1Corinthians9:19 For though I am free from all men
1Corinthians9:20 To the Jews I became as a Jew
1Corinthians9:21 To those outside the law I became as one outside the law—not being without law toward God but under the law of Christ—that I might win those outside the law.
1Corinthians9:22 To the weak I became weak
1Corinthians9:23 I do it all for the sake of the gospel
1Corinthians9:24 Do you not know that in a race all the runners compete
1Corinthians9:25 Every athlete exercises self-control in all things. They do it to receive a perishable wreath
1Corinthians9:26 Well
1Corinthians9:27 but I pommel my body and subdue it

1Corinthians12:2 You know that when you were heathen
1Corinthians12:3 Therefore I want you to understand that no one speaking by the Spirit of God ever says “Jesus be cursed!” and no one can say “Jesus is Lord” except by the Holy Spirit.
1Corinthians12:4 Now there are varieties of gifts
1Corinthians12:5 and there are varieties of service
1Corinthians12:6 and there are varieties of working
1Corinthians12:7 To each is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the common good.

1Corinthians12:9 to another faith by the same Spirit
1Corinthians12:10 to another the working of miracles
1Corinthians12:11 All these are inspired by one and the same Spirit

1Corinthians12:14 For the body does not consist of one member but of many.

1Corinthians12:16 And if the ear should say

1Corinthians12:18 But as it is
1Corinthians12:19 If all were a single organ
1Corinthians12:20 As it is
1Corinthians12:21 The eye cannot say to the hand
1Corinthians12:22 On the contrary
1Corinthians12:23 and those parts of the body which we think less honorable we invest with the greater honor
1Corinthians12:24 which our more presentable parts do not require. But God has so composed the body
1Corinthians12:25 that there may be no discord in the body
1Corinthians12:26 If one member suffers
1Corinthians12:27 Now you are the body of Christ and individually members of it.

1Corinthians12:30 Do all possess gifts of healing? Do all speak with tongues? Do all interpret?

2Corinthians13:2 I warned those who sinned before and all the others
2Corinthians13:3 since you desire proof that Christ is speaking in me. He is not weak in dealing with you
2Corinthians13:4 For he was crucified in weakness

2Corinthians13:7 But we pray God that you may not do wrong—not that we may appear to have met the test
2Corinthians13:8 For we cannot do anything against the truth
2Corinthians13:9 For we are glad when we are weak and you are strong. What we pray for is your improvement.

Galatians1:5 to whom be the glory for ever and ever. Amen.

Galatians1:7 not that there is another gospel
Galatians1:8 But even if we
Galatians1:9 As we have said before

Galatians1:12 For I did not receive it from man

Galatians1:15 But when he who had set me apart before I was born

Galatians1:19 But I saw none of the other apostles except James the Lord's brother.

Galatians1:21 Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
Galatians1:22 And I was still not known by sight to the churches of Christ in Judea;
Galatians1:23 they only heard it said
Galatians1:24 And they glorified God because of me.

Galatians3:8 And the scripture
Galatians3:9 So then

Galatians3:15 To give a human example

Galatians3:17 This is what I mean: the law

Galatians3:19 Why then the law? It was added because of transgressions
Galatians3:20 Now an intermediary implies more than one; but God is one.

Galatians3:24 So that the law was our custodian until Christ came
Galatians3:25 But now that faith has come

Galatians3:28 There is neither Jew nor Greek

Ephesians1:2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

Ephesians1:5 He destined us in love to be his sons through Jesus Christ
Ephesians1:6 to the praise of his glorious grace which he freely bestowed on us in the Beloved.
Ephesians1:7 In him we have redemption through his blood
Ephesians1:8 which he lavished upon us.
Ephesians1:9 For he has made known to us in all wisdom and insight the mystery of his will
Ephesians1:10 as a plan for the fulness of time
Ephesians1:11 In him
Ephesians1:12 we who first hoped in Christ have been destined and appointed to live for the praise of his glory.
Ephesians1:13 In him you also
Ephesians1:14 which is the guarantee of our inheritance until we acquire possession of it
Ephesians1:15 For this reason
Ephesians1:16 I do not cease to give thanks for you
Ephesians1:17 that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ
Ephesians1:18 having the eyes of your hearts enlightened
Ephesians1:19 and what is the immeasurable greatness of his power in us who believe
Ephesians1:20 which he accomplished in Christ when he raised him from the dead and made him sit at his right hand in the heavenly places
Ephesians1:21 far above all rule and authority and power and dominion
Ephesians1:22 and he has put all things under his feet and has made him the head over all things for the church
Ephesians1:23 which is his body
Ephesians2:1 And you he made alive
Ephesians2:2 in which you once walked
Ephesians2:3 Among these we all once lived in the passions of our flesh
Ephesians2:4 But God
Ephesians2:5 even when we were dead through our trespasses
Ephesians2:6 and raised us up with him
Ephesians2:7 that in the coming ages he might show the immeasurable riches of his grace in kindness toward us in Christ Jesus.
Ephesians2:8 For by grace you have been saved through faith; and this is not your own doing
Ephesians2:9 not because of works
Ephesians2:10 For we are his workmanship
Ephesians2:11 Therefore remember that at one time you Gentiles in the flesh
Ephesians2:12 remember that you were at that time separated from Christ
Ephesians2:13 But now in Christ Jesus you who once were far off have been brought near in the blood of Christ.
Ephesians2:14 For he is our peace
Ephesians2:15 by abolishing in his flesh the law of commandments and ordinances
Ephesians2:16 and might reconcile us both to God in one body through the cross
Ephesians2:17 And he came and preached peace to you who were far off and peace to those who were near;
Ephesians2:18 for through him we both have access in one Spirit to the Father.
Ephesians2:19 So then you are no longer strangers and sojourners
Ephesians2:20 built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets
Ephesians2:21 in whom the whole structure is joined together and grows into a holy temple in the Lord;
Ephesians2:22 in whom you also are built into it for a dwelling place of God in the Spirit.
