Ephesians3:2 assuming that you have heard of the stewardship of God's grace that was given to me for you
Ephesians3:3 how the mystery was made known to me by revelation
Ephesians3:4 When you read this you can perceive my insight into the mystery of Christ
Ephesians3:5 which was not made known to the sons of men in other generations as it has now been revealed to his holy apostles and prophets by the Spirit;
Ephesians3:6 that is
Ephesians3:7 Of this gospel I was made a minister according to the gift of God's grace which was given me by the working of his power.
Ephesians3:8 To me
Ephesians3:9 and to make all men see what is the plan of the mystery hidden for ages in God who created all things;
Ephesians3:10 that through the church the manifold wisdom of God might now be made known to the principalities and powers in the heavenly places.
Ephesians3:11 This was according to the eternal purpose which he has realized in Christ Jesus our Lord
Ephesians3:12 in whom we have boldness and confidence of access through our faith in him.
Ephesians3:13 So I ask you not to lose heart over what I am suffering for you
Ephesians3:14 For this reason I bow my knees before the Father
Ephesians3:15 from whom every family in heaven and on earth is named
Ephesians3:16 that according to the riches of his glory he may grant you to be strengthened with might through his Spirit in the inner man
Ephesians3:17 and that Christ may dwell in your hearts through faith; that you
Ephesians3:18 may have power to comprehend with all the saints what is the breadth and length and height and depth
Ephesians3:19 and to know the love of Christ which surpasses knowledge
Ephesians3:20 Now to him who by the power at work within us is able to do far more abundantly than all that we ask or think
Ephesians3:21 to him be glory in the church and in Christ Jesus to all generations
Ephesians4:1 I therefore
Ephesians4:2 with all lowliness and meekness
Ephesians4:3 eager to maintain the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.
Ephesians4:4 There is one body and one Spirit
Ephesians4:5 one Lord
Ephesians4:6 one God and Father of us all
Ephesians4:7 But grace was given to each of us according to the measure of Christ's gift.
Ephesians4:8 Therefore it is said
Ephesians4:9 (In saying
Ephesians4:10 He who descended is he who also ascended far above all the heavens
Ephesians4:11 And his gifts were that some should be apostles
Ephesians4:12 to equip the saints for the work of ministry
Ephesians4:13 until we all attain to the unity of the faith and of the knowledge of the Son of God
Ephesians4:14 so that we may no longer be children
Ephesians4:15 Rather
Ephesians4:16 from whom the whole body
Ephesians4:17 Now this I affirm and testify in the Lord
Ephesians4:18 they are darkened in their understanding
Ephesians4:19 they have become callous and have given themselves up to licentiousness
Ephesians4:20 You did not so learn Christ! —
Ephesians4:21 assuming that you have heard about him and were taught in him
Ephesians4:22 Put off your old nature which belongs to your former manner of life and is corrupt through deceitful lusts
Ephesians4:23 and be renewed in the spirit of your minds
Ephesians4:24 and put on the new nature
Ephesians4:25 Therefore
Ephesians4:26 Be angry but do not sin; do not let the sun go down on your anger
Ephesians4:27 and give no opportunity to the devil.
Ephesians4:28 Let the thief no longer steal
Ephesians4:29 Let no evil talk come out of your mouths
Ephesians4:30 And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God
Ephesians4:31 Let all bitterness and wrath and anger and clamor and slander be put away from you
Ephesians4:32 and be kind to one another
Ephesians5:1 Therefore be imitators of God
Ephesians5:2 And walk in love
Ephesians5:3 But fornication and all impurity or covetousness must not even be named among you
Ephesians5:4 Let there be no filthiness
Ephesians5:5 Be sure of this
Ephesians5:6 Let no one deceive you with empty words
Ephesians5:7 Therefore do not associate with them
Ephesians5:8 for once you were darkness
Ephesians5:9 (for the fruit of light is found in all that is good and right and true)
Ephesians5:10 and try to learn what is pleasing to the Lord.
Ephesians5:11 Take no part in the unfruitful works of darkness
Ephesians5:12 For it is a shame even to speak of the things that they do in secret;
Ephesians5:13 but when anything is exposed by the light it becomes visible
Ephesians5:14 Therefore it is said
Ephesians5:15 Look carefully then how you walk
Ephesians5:16 making the most of the time
Ephesians5:17 Therefore do not be foolish
Ephesians5:18 And do not get drunk with wine
Ephesians5:19 addressing one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs
Ephesians5:20 always and for everything giving thanks in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ to God the Father.
Ephesians5:21 Be subject to one another out of reverence for Christ.
Ephesians5:22 Wives
Ephesians5:23 For the husband is the head of the wife as Christ is the head of the church
Ephesians5:24 As the church is subject to Christ
Ephesians5:25 Husbands
Ephesians5:26 that he might sanctify her
Ephesians5:27 that he might present the church to himself in splendor
Ephesians5:28 Even so husbands should love their wives as their own bodies. He who loves his wife loves himself.
Ephesians5:29 For no man ever hates his own flesh
Ephesians5:30 because we are members of his body.
Ephesians5:31 “For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife
Ephesians5:32 This mystery is a profound one
Ephesians5:33 however
Ephesians6:1 Children
Ephesians6:2 “Honor your father and mother” (this is the first commandment with a promise)
Ephesians6:3 “that it may be well with you and that you may live long on the earth.”
Ephesians6:4 Fathers
Ephesians6:5 Slaves
Ephesians6:6 not in the way of eyeservice
Ephesians6:7 rendering service with a good will as to the Lord and not to men
Ephesians6:8 knowing that whatever good any one does
Ephesians6:9 Masters
Ephesians6:10 Finally
Ephesians6:11 Put on the whole armor of God
Ephesians6:12 For we are not contending against flesh and blood
Ephesians6:13 Therefore take the whole armor of God
Ephesians6:14 Stand therefore
Ephesians6:15 and having shod your feet with the equipment of the gospel of peace;
Ephesians6:16 besides all these
Ephesians6:17 And take the helmet of salvation
Ephesians6:18 Pray at all times in the Spirit
Ephesians6:19 and also for me
Ephesians6:20 for which I am an ambassador in chains; that I may declare it boldly
Ephesians6:21 Now that you also may know how I am and what I am doing
Ephesians6:22 I have sent him to you for this very purpose
Ephesians6:23 Peace be to the brethren
Ephesians6:24 Grace be with all who love our Lord Jesus Christ with love undying.
Philippians1:1 Paul and Timothy
Philippians1:2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Philippians1:3 I thank my God in all my remembrance of you
Philippians1:4 always in every prayer of mine for you all making my prayer with joy
Philippians1:5 thankful for your partnership in the gospel from the first day until now.
Philippians1:6 And I am sure that he who began a good work in you will bring it to completion at the day of Jesus Christ.
Philippians1:7 It is right for me to feel thus about you all
Philippians1:8 For God is my witness
Philippians1:9 And it is my prayer that your love may abound more and more
Philippians1:10 so that you may approve what is excellent
Philippians1:11 filled with the fruits of righteousness which come through Jesus Christ
Philippians1:12 I want you to know
Philippians1:13 so that it has become known throughout the whole praetorian guard and to all the rest that my imprisonment is for Christ;
Philippians1:14 and most of the brethren have been made confident in the Lord because of my imprisonment
Philippians1:15 Some indeed preach Christ from envy and rivalry
Philippians1:16 The latter do it out of love
Philippians1:17 the former proclaim Christ out of partisanship
Philippians1:18 What then? Only that in every way
Philippians1:19 Yes
Philippians1:20 as it is my eager expectation and hope that I shall not be at all ashamed
Philippians1:21 For to me to live is Christ
Philippians1:22 If it is to be life in the flesh
Philippians1:23 I am hard pressed between the two. My desire is to depart and be with Christ
Philippians1:24 But to remain in the flesh is more necessary on your account.
Philippians1:25 Convinced of this
Philippians1:26 so that in me you may have ample cause to glory in Christ Jesus
Philippians1:27 Only let your manner of life be worthy of the gospel of Christ
Philippians1:28 and not frightened in anything by your opponents. This is a clear omen to them of their destruction
Philippians1:29 For it has been granted to you that for the sake of Christ you should not only believe in him but also suffer for his sake
Philippians1:30 engaged in the same conflict which you saw and now hear to be mine.
Philippians2:1 So if there is any encouragement in Christ
Philippians2:2 complete my joy by being of the same mind
Philippians2:3 Do nothing from selfishness or conceit
Philippians2:4 Let each of you look not only to his own interests
Philippians2:5 Have this mind among yourselves
Philippians2:6 who
Philippians2:7 but emptied himself
Philippians2:8 And being found in human form he humbled himself and became obedient unto death
Philippians2:9 Therefore God has highly exalted him and bestowed on him the name which is above every name
Philippians2:10 that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow
Philippians2:11 and every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord
Philippians2:12 Therefore
Philippians2:13 for God is at work in you
Philippians2:14 Do all things without grumbling or questioning
Philippians2:15 that you may be blameless and innocent
Philippians2:16 holding fast the word of life
Philippians2:17 Even if I am to be poured as a libation upon the sacrificial offering of your faith
Philippians2:18 Likewise you also should be glad and rejoice with me.
Philippians2:19 I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon
Philippians2:20 I have no one like him
Philippians2:21 They all look after their own interests
Philippians2:22 But Timothy's worth you know
Philippians2:23 I hope therefore to send him just as soon as I see how it will go with me;
Philippians2:24 and I trust in the Lord that shortly I myself shall come also.
Philippians2:25 I have thought it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus my brother and fellow worker and fellow soldier
Philippians2:26 for he has been longing for you all
Philippians2:27 Indeed he was ill
Philippians2:28 I am the more eager to send him
Philippians2:29 So receive him in the Lord with all joy; and honor such men
Philippians2:30 for he nearly died for the work of Christ
Philippians3:1 Finally
Philippians3:2 Look out for the dogs
Philippians3:3 For we are the true circumcision
Philippians3:4 Though I myself have reason for confidence in the flesh also. If any other man thinks he has reason for confidence in the flesh
Philippians3:5 circumcised on the eighth day
Philippians3:6 as to zeal a persecutor of the church
Philippians3:7 But whatever gain I had
Philippians3:8 Indeed I count everything as loss because of the surpassing worth of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord. For his sake I have suffered the loss of all things
Philippians3:9 and be found in him
Philippians3:10 that I may know him and the power of his resurrection
Philippians3:11 that if possible I may attain the resurrection from the dead.
Philippians3:12 Not that I have already obtained this or am already perfect; but I press on to make it my own
Philippians3:13 Brethren
Philippians3:14 I press on toward the goal for the prize of the upward call of God in Christ Jesus.
Philippians3:15 Let those of us who are mature be thus minded; and if in anything you are otherwise minded
Philippians3:16 Only let us hold true to what we have attained.
Philippians3:17 Brethren
Philippians3:18 For many
Philippians3:19 Their end is destruction
Philippians3:20 But our commonwealth is in heaven
Philippians3:21 who will change our lowly body to be like his glorious body
Philippians4:1 Therefore
Philippians4:2 I entreat Eu-odia and I entreat Syntyche to agree in the Lord.
Philippians4:3 And I ask you also
Philippians4:4 Rejoice in the Lord always; again I will say
Philippians4:5 Let all men know your forbearance. The Lord is at hand.
Philippians4:6 Have no anxiety about anything
Philippians4:7 And the peace of God
Philippians4:8 Finally
Philippians4:9 What you have learned and received and heard and seen in me
Philippians4:10 I rejoice in the Lord greatly that now at length you have revived your concern for me; you were indeed concerned for me
Philippians4:11 Not that I complain of want; for I have learned
Philippians4:12 I know how to be abased
Philippians4:13 I can do all things in him who strengthens me.
Philippians4:14 Yet it was kind of you to share my trouble.
Philippians4:15 And you Philippians yourselves know that in the beginning of the gospel
Philippians4:16 for even in Thessalonica you sent me help once and again.
Philippians4:17 Not that I seek the gift; but I seek the fruit which increases to your credit.
Philippians4:18 I have received full payment
Philippians4:19 And my God will supply every need of yours according to his riches in glory in Christ Jesus.
Philippians4:20 To our God and Father be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
Philippians4:21 Greet every saint in Christ Jesus. The brethren who are with me greet you.
Philippians4:22 All the saints greet you
Philippians4:23 The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
Colossians1:1 Paul
Colossians1:2 To the saints and faithful brethren in Christ at Colossae: Grace to you and peace from God our Father.
Colossians1:3 We always thank God
Colossians1:4 because we have heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and of the love which you have for all the saints
Colossians1:5 because of the hope laid up for you in heaven. Of this you have heard before in the word of the truth
Colossians1:6 which has come to you
Colossians1:7 as you learned it from Epaphras our beloved fellow servant. He is a faithful minister of Christ on our behalf
Colossians1:8 and has made known to us your love in the Spirit.
Colossians1:9 And so
Colossians1:10 to lead a life worthy of the Lord
Colossians1:11 May you be strengthened with all power
Colossians1:12 giving thanks to the Father
Colossians1:13 He has delivered us from the dominion of darkness and transferred us to the kingdom of his beloved Son
Colossians1:14 in whom we have redemption
Colossians1:15 He is the image of the invisible God
Colossians1:16 for in him all things were created
Colossians1:17 He is before all things
Colossians1:18 He is the head of the body
Colossians1:19 For in him all the fulness of God was pleased to dwell
Colossians1:20 and through him to reconcile to himself all things
Colossians1:21 And you
Colossians1:22 he has now reconciled in his body of flesh by his death
Colossians1:23 provided that you continue in the faith
Colossians1:24 Now I rejoice in my sufferings for your sake
Colossians1:25 of which I became a minister according to the divine office which was given to me for you
Colossians1:26 the mystery hidden for ages and generations but now made manifest to his saints.
Colossians1:27 To them God chose to make known how great among the Gentiles are the riches of the glory of this mystery
Colossians1:28 Him we proclaim
Colossians1:29 For this I toil
Colossians2:1 For I want you to know how greatly I strive for you
Colossians2:2 that their hearts may be encouraged as they are knit together in love
Colossians2:3 in whom are hid all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
Colossians2:4 I say this in order that no one may delude you with beguiling speech.
Colossians2:5 For though I am absent in body
Colossians2:6 As therefore you received Christ Jesus the Lord
Colossians2:7 rooted and built up in him and established in the faith
Colossians2:8 See to it that no one makes a prey of you by philosophy and empty deceit
Colossians2:9 For in him the whole fulness of deity dwells bodily
Colossians2:10 and you have come to fulness of life in him
Colossians2:11 In him also you were circumcised with a circumcision made without hands
Colossians2:12 and you were buried with him in baptism
Colossians2:13 And you
Colossians2:14 having canceled the bond which stood against us with its legal demands; this he set aside
Colossians2:15 He disarmed the principalities and powers and made a public example of them
Colossians2:16 Therefore let no one pass judgment on you in questions of food and drink or with regard to a festival or a new moon or a sabbath.
Colossians2:17 These are only a shadow of what is to come; but the substance belongs to Christ.
Colossians2:18 Let no one disqualify you
Colossians2:19 and not holding fast to the Head
Colossians2:20 If with Christ you died to the elemental spirits of the universe
Colossians2:21 “Do not handle
Colossians2:22 (referring to things which all perish as they are used)
Colossians2:23 These have indeed an appearance of wisdom in promoting rigor of devotion and self-abasement and severity to the body
Colossians3:1 If then you have been raised with Christ
Colossians3:2 Set your minds on things that are above
Colossians3:3 For you have died
Colossians3:4 When Christ who is our life appears
Colossians3:5 Put to death therefore what is earthly in you: fornication
Colossians3:6 On account of these the wrath of God is coming.
Colossians3:7 In these you once walked
Colossians3:8 But now put them all away: anger
Colossians3:9 Do not lie to one another
Colossians3:10 and have put on the new nature
Colossians3:11 Here there cannot be Greek and Jew
Colossians3:12 Put on then
Colossians3:13 forbearing one another and
Colossians3:14 And above all these put on love
Colossians3:15 And let the peace of Christ rule in your hearts
Colossians3:16 Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly
Colossians3:17 And whatever you do
Colossians3:18 Wives
Colossians3:19 Husbands
Colossians3:20 Children
Colossians3:21 Fathers
Colossians3:22 Slaves
Colossians3:23 Whatever your task
Colossians3:24 knowing that from the Lord you will receive the inheritance as your reward; you are serving the Lord Christ.
Colossians3:25 For the wrongdoer will be paid back for the wrong he has done
Colossians4:1 Masters
Colossians4:2 Continue steadfastly in prayer
Colossians4:3 and pray for us also
Colossians4:4 that I may make it clear
Colossians4:5 Conduct yourselves wisely toward outsiders
Colossians4:6 Let your speech always be gracious
Colossians4:7 Tychicus will tell you all about my affairs; he is a beloved brother and faithful minister and fellow servant in the Lord.
Colossians4:8 I have sent him to you for this very purpose
Colossians4:9 and with him Onesimus
Colossians4:10 Aristarchus my fellow prisoner greets you
Colossians4:11 and Jesus who is called Justus. These are the only men of the circumcision among my fellow workers for the kingdom of God
Colossians4:12 Epaphras
Colossians4:13 For I bear him witness that he has worked hard for you and for those in La-odicea and in Hi-erapolis.
Colossians4:14 Luke the beloved physician and Demas greet you.
Colossians4:15 Give my greetings to the brethren at La-odicea
Colossians4:16 And when this letter has been read among you
Colossians4:17 And say to Archippus
Colossians4:18 I
1Thessalonians1:1 Paul
1Thessalonians1:2 We give thanks to God always for you all
1Thessalonians1:3 remembering before our God and Father your work of faith and labor of love and steadfastness of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ.
1Thessalonians1:4 For we know
1Thessalonians1:5 for our gospel came to you not only in word
1Thessalonians1:6 And you became imitators of us and of the Lord
1Thessalonians1:7 so that you became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and in Achaia.
1Thessalonians1:8 For not only has the word of the Lord sounded forth from you in Macedonia and Achaia
1Thessalonians1:9 For they themselves report concerning us what a welcome we had among you
1Thessalonians1:10 and to wait for his Son from heaven
1Thessalonians2:1 For you yourselves know
1Thessalonians2:2 but though we had already suffered and been shamefully treated at Philippi
1Thessalonians2:3 For our appeal does not spring from error or uncleanness
1Thessalonians2:4 but just as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the gospel
1Thessalonians2:5 For we never used either words of flattery
1Thessalonians2:6 nor did we seek glory from men
1Thessalonians2:7 But we were gentle among you
1Thessalonians2:8 So
1Thessalonians2:9 For you remember our labor and toil
1Thessalonians2:10 You are witnesses
1Thessalonians2:11 for you know how
1Thessalonians2:12 to lead a life worthy of God
1Thessalonians2:13 And we also thank God constantly for this
1Thessalonians2:14 For you
1Thessalonians2:15 who killed both the Lord Jesus and the prophets
1Thessalonians2:16 by hindering us from speaking to the Gentiles that they may be saved—so as always to fill up the measure of their sins. But God's wrath has come upon them at last!
1Thessalonians2:17 But since we were bereft of you
1Thessalonians2:18 because we wanted to come to you—I
1Thessalonians2:19 For what is our hope or joy or crown of boasting before our Lord Jesus at his coming? Is it not you?
1Thessalonians2:20 For you are our glory and joy.
1Thessalonians3:1 Therefore when we could bear it no longer
1Thessalonians3:2 and we sent Timothy
1Thessalonians3:3 that no one be moved by these afflictions. You yourselves know that this is to be our lot.
1Thessalonians3:4 For when we were with you
1Thessalonians3:5 For this reason
1Thessalonians3:6 But now that Timothy has come to us from you
1Thessalonians3:7 for this reason
1Thessalonians3:8 for now we live
1Thessalonians3:9 For what thanksgiving can we render to God for you
1Thessalonians3:10 praying earnestly night and day that we may see you face to face and supply what is lacking in your faith?
1Thessalonians3:11 Now may our God and Father himself
1Thessalonians3:12 and may the Lord make you increase and abound in love to one another and to all men
1Thessalonians3:13 so that he may establish your hearts unblamable in holiness before our God and Father
1Thessalonians4:1 Finally
1Thessalonians4:2 For you know what instructions we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
1Thessalonians4:3 For this is the will of God
1Thessalonians4:4 that each one of you know how to take a wife for himself in holiness and honor
1Thessalonians4:5 not in the passion of lust like heathen who do not know God;
1Thessalonians4:6 that no man transgress
1Thessalonians4:7 For God has not called us for uncleanness
1Thessalonians4:8 Therefore whoever disregards this
1Thessalonians4:9 But concerning love of the brethren you have no need to have any one write to you
1Thessalonians4:10 and indeed you do love all the brethren throughout Macedonia. But we exhort you
1Thessalonians4:11 to aspire to live quietly
1Thessalonians4:12 so that you may command the respect of outsiders
1Thessalonians4:13 But we would not have you ignorant
1Thessalonians4:14 For since we believe that Jesus died and rose again
1Thessalonians4:15 For this we declare to you by the word of the Lord
1Thessalonians4:16 For the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a cry of command
1Thessalonians4:17 then we who are alive
1Thessalonians4:18 Therefore comfort one another with these words.
1Thessalonians5:1 But as to the times and the seasons
1Thessalonians5:2 For you yourselves know well that the day of the Lord will come like a thief in the night.
1Thessalonians5:3 When people say
1Thessalonians5:4 But you are not in darkness
1Thessalonians5:5 For you are all sons of light and sons of the day; we are not of the night or of darkness.
1Thessalonians5:6 So then let us not sleep
1Thessalonians5:7 For those who sleep at night
1Thessalonians5:8 But
1Thessalonians5:9 For God has not destined us for wrath
1Thessalonians5:10 who died for us so that whether we wake or sleep we might live with him.
1Thessalonians5:11 Therefore encourage one another and build one another up
1Thessalonians5:12 But we beseech you
1Thessalonians5:13 and to esteem them very highly in love because of their work. Be at peace among yourselves.
1Thessalonians5:14 And we exhort you
1Thessalonians5:15 See that none of you repays evil for evil
1Thessalonians5:16 Rejoice always
1Thessalonians5:17 pray constantly
1Thessalonians5:18 give thanks in all circumstances; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.
1Thessalonians5:19 Do not quench the Spirit
1Thessalonians5:20 do not despise prophesying
1Thessalonians5:21 but test everything; hold fast what is good
1Thessalonians5:22 abstain from every form of evil.
1Thessalonians5:23 May the God of peace himself sanctify you wholly; and may your spirit and soul and body be kept sound and blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
1Thessalonians5:24 He who calls you is faithful
1Thessalonians5:25 Brethren
1Thessalonians5:26 Greet all the brethren with a holy kiss.
1Thessalonians5:27 I adjure you by the Lord that this letter be read to all the brethren.
1Thessalonians5:28 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
2Thessalonians1:1 Paul
2Thessalonians1:2 Grace to you and peace from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
2Thessalonians1:3 We are bound to give thanks to God always for you
2Thessalonians1:4 Therefore we ourselves boast of you in the churches of God for your steadfastness and faith in all your persecutions and in the afflictions which you are enduring.
2Thessalonians1:5 This is evidence of the righteous judgment of God
2Thessalonians1:6 since indeed God deems it just to repay with affliction those who afflict you
2Thessalonians1:7 and to grant rest with us to you who are afflicted
2Thessalonians1:8 inflicting vengeance upon those who do not know God and upon those who do not obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus.
2Thessalonians1:9 They shall suffer the punishment of eternal destruction and exclusion from the presence of the Lord and from the glory of his might
2Thessalonians1:10 when he comes on that day to be glorified in his saints
2Thessalonians1:11 To this end we always pray for you
2Thessalonians1:12 so that the name of our Lord Jesus may be glorified in you
2Thessalonians2:1 Now concerning the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our assembling to meet him
2Thessalonians2:2 not to be quickly shaken in mind or excited
2Thessalonians2:3 Let no one deceive you in any way; for that day will not come
2Thessalonians2:4 who opposes and exalts himself against every so-called god or object of worship
2Thessalonians2:5 Do you not remember that when I was still with you I told you this?
2Thessalonians2:6 And you know what is restraining him now so that he may be revealed in his time.
2Thessalonians2:7 For the mystery of lawlessness is already at work; only he who now restrains it will do so until he is out of the way.
2Thessalonians2:8 And then the lawless one will be revealed
2Thessalonians2:9 The coming of the lawless one by the activity of Satan will be with all power and with pretended signs and wonders
2Thessalonians2:10 and with all wicked deception for those who are to perish
2Thessalonians2:11 Therefore God sends upon them a strong delusion
2Thessalonians2:12 so that all may be condemned who did not believe the truth but had pleasure in unrighteousness.
2Thessalonians2:13 But we are bound to give thanks to God always for you
2Thessalonians2:14 To this he called you through our gospel
2Thessalonians2:15 So then
2Thessalonians2:16 Now may our Lord Jesus Christ himself
2Thessalonians2:17 comfort your hearts and establish them in every good work and word.
2Thessalonians3:1 Finally
2Thessalonians3:2 and that we may be delivered from wicked and evil men; for not all have faith.
2Thessalonians3:3 But the Lord is faithful; he will strengthen you and guard you from evil.
2Thessalonians3:4 And we have confidence in the Lord about you
2Thessalonians3:5 May the Lord direct your hearts to the love of God and to the steadfastness of Christ.
2Thessalonians3:6 Now we command you
2Thessalonians3:7 For you yourselves know how you ought to imitate us; we were not idle when we were with you
2Thessalonians3:8 we did not eat any one's bread without paying
2Thessalonians3:9 It was not because we have not that right
2Thessalonians3:10 For even when we were with you
2Thessalonians3:11 For we hear that some of you are living in idleness
2Thessalonians3:12 Now such persons we command and exhort in the Lord Jesus Christ to do their work in quietness and to earn their own living.
2Thessalonians3:13 Brethren
2Thessalonians3:14 If any one refuses to obey what we say in this letter
2Thessalonians3:15 Do not look on him as an enemy
2Thessalonians3:16 Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times in all ways. The Lord be with you all.
2Thessalonians3:17 I
2Thessalonians3:18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.
1Timothy1:1 Paul
1Timothy1:2 To Timothy
1Timothy1:3 As I urged you when I was going to Macedonia
1Timothy1:4 nor to occupy themselves with myths and endless genealogies which promote speculations rather than the divine training that is in faith;
1Timothy1:5 whereas the aim of our charge is love that issues from a pure heart and a good conscience and sincere faith.
1Timothy1:6 Certain persons by swerving from these have wandered away into vain discussion
1Timothy1:7 desiring to be teachers of the law
1Timothy1:8 Now we know that the law is good
1Timothy1:9 understanding this
1Timothy1:10 immoral persons
1Timothy1:11 in accordance with the glorious gospel of the blessed God with which I have been entrusted.
1Timothy1:12 I thank him who has given me strength for this
1Timothy1:13 though I formerly blasphemed and persecuted and insulted him; but I received mercy because I had acted ignorantly in unbelief
1Timothy1:14 and the grace of our Lord overflowed for me with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
1Timothy1:15 The saying is sure and worthy of full acceptance
1Timothy1:16 but I received mercy for this reason
1Timothy1:17 To the King of ages
1Timothy1:18 This charge I commit to you
1Timothy1:19 holding faith and a good conscience. By rejecting conscience
1Timothy1:20 among them Hymenaeus and Alexander
1Timothy2:1 First of all
1Timothy2:2 for kings and all who are in high positions
1Timothy2:3 This is good
1Timothy2:4 who desires all men to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.
1Timothy2:5 For there is one God
1Timothy2:6 who gave himself as a ransom for all
1Timothy2:7 For this I was appointed a preacher and apostle (I am telling the truth
1Timothy2:8 I desire then that in every place the men should pray
1Timothy2:9 also that women should adorn themselves modestly and sensibly in seemly apparel
1Timothy2:10 but by good deeds
1Timothy2:11 Let a woman learn in silence with all submissiveness.
1Timothy2:12 I permit no woman to teach or to have authority over men; she is to keep silent.
1Timothy2:13 For Adam was formed first
1Timothy2:14 and Adam was not deceived
1Timothy2:15 Yet woman will be saved through bearing children
1Timothy3:1 The saying is sure: If any one aspires to the office of bishop
1Timothy3:2 Now a bishop must be above reproach
1Timothy3:3 no drunkard
1Timothy3:4 He must manage his own household well
1Timothy3:5 for if a man does not know how to manage his own household
1Timothy3:6 He must not be a recent convert
1Timothy3:7 moreover he must be well thought of by outsiders
1Timothy3:8 Deacons likewise must be serious
1Timothy3:9 they must hold the mystery of the faith with a clear conscience.
1Timothy3:10 And let them also be tested first; then if they prove themselves blameless let them serve as deacons.
1Timothy3:11 The women likewise must be serious
1Timothy3:12 Let deacons be the husband of one wife
1Timothy3:13 for those who serve well as deacons gain a good standing for themselves and also great confidence in the faith which is in Christ Jesus.
1Timothy3:14 I hope to come to you soon
1Timothy3:15 if I am delayed
1Timothy3:16 Great indeed
1Timothy4:1 Now the Spirit expressly says that in later times some will depart from the faith by giving heed to deceitful spirits and doctrines of demons
1Timothy4:2 through the pretensions of liars whose consciences are seared
1Timothy4:3 who forbid marriage and enjoin abstinence from foods which God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
1Timothy4:4 For everything created by God is good
1Timothy4:5 for then it is consecrated by the word of God and prayer.
1Timothy4:6 If you put these instructions before the brethren
1Timothy4:7 Have nothing to do with godless and silly myths. Train yourself in godliness;
1Timothy4:8 for while bodily training is of some value
1Timothy4:9 The saying is sure and worthy of full acceptance.
1Timothy4:10 For to this end we toil and strive
1Timothy4:11 Command and teach these things.
1Timothy4:12 Let no one despise your youth
1Timothy4:13 Till I come
1Timothy4:14 Do not neglect the gift you have
1Timothy4:15 Practice these duties
1Timothy4:16 Take heed to yourself and to your teaching; hold to that
1Timothy5:1 Do not rebuke an older man but exhort him as you would a father; treat younger men like brothers
1Timothy5:2 older women like mothers
1Timothy5:3 Honor widows who are real widows.
1Timothy5:4 If a widow has children or grandchildren
1Timothy5:5 She who is a real widow
1Timothy5:6 whereas she who is self-indulgent is dead even while she lives.
1Timothy5:7 Command this
1Timothy5:8 If any one does not provide for his relatives
1Timothy5:9 Let a widow be enrolled if she is not less than sixty years of age
1Timothy5:10 and she must be well attested for her good deeds
1Timothy5:11 But refuse to enrol younger widows; for when they grow wanton against Christ they desire to marry
1Timothy5:12 and so they incur condemnation for having violated their first pledge.
1Timothy5:13 Besides that
1Timothy5:14 So I would have younger widows marry
1Timothy5:15 For some have already strayed after Satan.
1Timothy5:16 If any believing woman has relatives who are widows
1Timothy5:17 Let the elders who rule well be considered worthy of double honor
1Timothy5:18 for the scripture says
1Timothy5:19 Never admit any charge against an elder except on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
1Timothy5:20 As for those who persist in sin
1Timothy5:21 In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus and of the elect angels I charge you to keep these rules without favor
1Timothy5:22 Do not be hasty in the laying on of hands
1Timothy5:23 No longer drink only water
1Timothy5:24 The sins of some men are conspicuous
1Timothy5:25 So also good deeds are conspicuous; and even when they are not
1Timothy6:1 Let all who are under the yoke of slavery regard their masters as worthy of all honor
1Timothy6:2 Those who have believing masters must not be disrespectful on the ground that they are brethren; rather they must serve all the better since those who benefit by their service are believers and beloved. Teach and urge these duties.
1Timothy6:3 If any one teaches otherwise and does not agree with the sound words of our Lord Jesus Christ and the teaching which accords with godliness
1Timothy6:4 he is puffed up with conceit
1Timothy6:5 and wrangling among men who are depraved in mind and bereft of the truth
1Timothy6:6 There is great gain in godliness with contentment;
1Timothy6:7 for we brought nothing into the world
1Timothy6:8 but if we have food and clothing
1Timothy6:9 But those who desire to be rich fall into temptation
1Timothy6:10 For the love of money is the root of all evils; it is through this craving that some have wandered away from the faith and pierced their hearts with many pangs.
1Timothy6:11 But as for you
1Timothy6:12 Fight the good fight of the faith; take hold of the eternal life to which you were called when you made the good confession in the presence of many witnesses.
1Timothy6:13 In the presence of God who gives life to all things
1Timothy6:14 I charge you to keep the commandment unstained and free from reproach until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ;
1Timothy6:15 and this will be made manifest at the proper time by the blessed and only Sovereign
1Timothy6:16 who alone has immortality and dwells in unapproachable light
1Timothy6:17 As for the rich in this world
1Timothy6:18 They are to do good
1Timothy6:19 thus laying up for themselves a good foundation for the future
1Timothy6:20 O Timothy
1Timothy6:21 for by professing it some have missed the mark as regards the faith. Grace be with you.
2Timothy1:1 Paul
2Timothy1:2 To Timothy
2Timothy1:3 I thank God whom I serve with a clear conscience
2Timothy1:4 As I remember your tears
2Timothy1:5 I am reminded of your sincere faith
2Timothy1:6 Hence I remind you to rekindle the gift of God that is within you through the laying on of my hands;
2Timothy1:7 for God did not give us a spirit of timidity but a spirit of power and love and self-control.
2Timothy1:8 Do not be ashamed then of testifying to our Lord
2Timothy1:9 who saved us and called us with a holy calling
2Timothy1:10 and now has manifested through the appearing of our Savior Christ Jesus
2Timothy1:11 For this gospel I was appointed a preacher and apostle and teacher
2Timothy1:12 and therefore I suffer as I do. But I am not ashamed
2Timothy1:13 Follow the pattern of the sound words which you have heard from me
2Timothy1:14 guard the truth that has been entrusted to you by the Holy Spirit who dwells within us.
2Timothy1:15 You are aware that all who are in Asia turned away from me
2Timothy1:16 May the Lord grant mercy to the household of Onesiphorus
2Timothy1:17 but when he arrived in Rome he searched for me eagerly and found me—
2Timothy1:18 may the Lord grant him to find mercy from the Lord on that Day—and you well know all the service he rendered at Ephesus.
2Timothy2:1 You then
2Timothy2:2 and what you have heard from me before many witnesses entrust to faithful men who will be able to teach others also.
2Timothy2:3 Share in suffering as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
2Timothy2:4 No soldier on service gets entangled in civilian pursuits
2Timothy2:5 An athlete is not crowned unless he competes according to the rules.
2Timothy2:6 It is the hard-working farmer who ought to have the first share of the crops.
2Timothy2:7 Think over what I say
2Timothy2:8 Remember Jesus Christ
2Timothy2:9 the gospel for which I am suffering and wearing fetters like a criminal. But the word of God is not fettered.
2Timothy2:10 Therefore I endure everything for the sake of the elect
2Timothy2:11 The saying is sure: If we have died with him
2Timothy2:12 if we endure
2Timothy2:13 if we are faithless
2Timothy2:14 Remind them of this
2Timothy2:15 Do your best to present yourself to God as one approved
2Timothy2:16 Avoid such godless chatter
2Timothy2:17 and their talk will eat its way like gangrene. Among them are Hymenaeus and Philetus
2Timothy2:18 who have swerved from the truth by holding that the resurrection is past already. They are upsetting the faith of some.
2Timothy2:19 But God's firm foundation stands
2Timothy2:20 In a great house there are not only vessels of gold and silver but also of wood and earthenware
2Timothy2:21 If any one purifies himself from what is ignoble
2Timothy2:22 So shun youthful passions and aim at righteousness
2Timothy2:23 Have nothing to do with stupid
2Timothy2:24 And the Lord's servant must not be quarrelsome but kindly to every one
2Timothy2:25 correcting his opponents with gentleness. God may perhaps grant that they will repent and come to know the truth
2Timothy2:26 and they may escape from the snare of the devil
2Timothy3:1 But understand this
2Timothy3:2 For men will be lovers of self
2Timothy3:3 inhuman
2Timothy3:4 treacherous
2Timothy3:5 holding the form of religion but denying the power of it. Avoid such people.
2Timothy3:6 For among them are those who make their way into households and capture weak women
2Timothy3:7 who will listen to anybody and can never arrive at a knowledge of the truth.
2Timothy3:8 As Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses
2Timothy3:9 but they will not get very far
2Timothy3:10 Now you have observed my teaching
2Timothy3:11 my persecutions
2Timothy3:12 Indeed all who desire to live a godly life in Christ Jesus will be persecuted
2Timothy3:13 while evil men and impostors will go on from bad to worse
2Timothy3:14 But as for you
2Timothy3:15 and how from childhood you have been acquainted with the sacred writings which are able to instruct you for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
2Timothy3:16 All scripture is inspired by God and profitable for teaching
2Timothy3:17 that the man of God may be complete
2Timothy4:1 I charge you in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus who is to judge the living and the dead
2Timothy4:2 preach the word
2Timothy4:3 For the time is coming when people will not endure sound teaching
2Timothy4:4 and will turn away from listening to the truth and wander into myths.
2Timothy4:5 As for you
2Timothy4:6 For I am already on the point of being sacrificed; the time of my departure has come.
2Timothy4:7 I have fought the good fight
2Timothy4:8 Henceforth there is laid up for me the crown of righteousness
2Timothy4:9 Do your best to come to me soon.
2Timothy4:10 For Demas
2Timothy4:11 Luke alone is with me. Get Mark and bring him with you; for he is very useful in serving me.
2Timothy4:12 Tychicus I have sent to Ephesus.
2Timothy4:13 When you come
2Timothy4:14 Alexander the coppersmith did me great harm; the Lord will requite him for his deeds.
2Timothy4:15 Beware of him yourself
2Timothy4:16 At my first defense no one took my part; all deserted me. May it not be charged against them!
2Timothy4:17 But the Lord stood by me and gave me strength to proclaim the message fully
2Timothy4:18 The Lord will rescue me from every evil and save me for his heavenly kingdom. To him be the glory for ever and ever. Amen.
2Timothy4:19 Greet Prisca and Aquila
2Timothy4:20 Erastus remained at Corinth; Trophimus I left ill at Miletus.
2Timothy4:21 Do your best to come before winter. Eubulus sends greetings to you
2Timothy4:22 The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you.
Titus1:1 Paul
Titus1:2 in hope of eternal life which God
Titus1:3 and at the proper time manifested in his word through the preaching with which I have been entrusted by command of God our Savior;
Titus1:4 To Titus
Titus1:5 This is why I left you in Crete
Titus1:6 if any man is blameless
Titus1:7 For a bishop
Titus1:8 but hospitable
Titus1:9 he must hold firm to the sure word as taught
Titus1:10 For there are many insubordinate men
Titus1:11 they must be silenced
Titus1:12 One of themselves
Titus1:13 This testimony is true. Therefore rebuke them sharply
Titus1:14 instead of giving heed to Jewish myths or to commands of men who reject the truth.
Titus1:15 To the pure all things are pure
Titus1:16 They profess to know God
Titus2:1 But as for you
Titus2:2 Bid the older men be temperate
Titus2:3 Bid the older women likewise to be reverent in behavior
Titus2:4 and so train the young women to love their husbands and children
Titus2:5 to be sensible
Titus2:6 Likewise urge the younger men to control themselves.
Titus2:7 Show yourself in all respects a model of good deeds
Titus2:8 and sound speech that cannot be censured
Titus2:9 Bid slaves to be submissive to their masters and to give satisfaction in every respect; they are not to be refractory
Titus2:10 nor to pilfer
Titus2:11 For the grace of God has appeared for the salvation of all men
Titus2:12 training us to renounce irreligion and worldly passions
Titus2:13 awaiting our blessed hope
Titus2:14 who gave himself for us to redeem us from all iniquity and to purify for himself a people of his own who are zealous for good deeds.
Titus2:15 Declare these things; exhort and reprove with all authority. Let no one disregard you.
Titus3:1 Remind them to be submissive to rulers and authorities
Titus3:2 to speak evil of no one
Titus3:3 For we ourselves were once foolish
Titus3:4 but when the goodness and loving kindness of God our Savior appeared
Titus3:5 he saved us
Titus3:6 which he poured out upon us richly through Jesus Christ our Savior
Titus3:7 so that we might be justified by his grace and become heirs in hope of eternal life.
Titus3:8 The saying is sure. I desire you to insist on these things
Titus3:9 But avoid stupid controversies
Titus3:10 As for a man who is factious
Titus3:11 knowing that such a person is perverted and sinful; he is self-condemned.
Titus3:12 When I send Artemas or Tychicus to you
Titus3:13 Do your best to speed Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their way; see that they lack nothing.
Titus3:14 And let our people learn to apply themselves to good deeds
Titus3:15 All who are with me send greetings to you. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with you all.
Philemon1:1 Paul
Philemon1:2 and Apphia our sister and Archippus our fellow soldier
Philemon1:3 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Philemon1:4 I thank my God always when I remember you in my prayers
Philemon1:5 because I hear of your love and of the faith which you have toward the Lord Jesus and all the saints
Philemon1:6 and I pray that the sharing of your faith may promote the knowledge of all the good that is ours in Christ.
Philemon1:7 For I have derived much joy and comfort from your love
Philemon1:8 Accordingly
Philemon1:9 yet for love's sake I prefer to appeal to you—I
Philemon1:10 I appeal to you for my child
Philemon1:11 (Formerly he was useless to you
Philemon1:12 I am sending him back to you
Philemon1:13 I would have been glad to keep him with me
Philemon1:14 but I preferred to do nothing without your consent in order that your goodness might not be by compulsion but of your own free will.
Philemon1:15 Perhaps this is why he was parted from you for a while
Philemon1:16 no longer as a slave but more than a slave
Philemon1:17 So if you consider me your partner
Philemon1:18 If he has wronged you at all
Philemon1:19 I
Philemon1:20 Yes
Philemon1:21 Confident of your obedience
Philemon1:22 At the same time
Philemon1:23 Epaphras
Philemon1:24 and so do Mark
Philemon1:25 The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
Hebrews1:1 In many and various ways God spoke of old to our fathers by the prophets;
Hebrews1:2 but in these last days he has spoken to us by a Son
Hebrews1:3 He reflects the glory of God and bears the very stamp of his nature
Hebrews1:4 having become as much superior to angels as the name he has obtained is more excellent than theirs.
Hebrews1:5 For to what angel did God ever say
Hebrews1:6 And again
Hebrews1:7 Of the angels he says
Hebrews1:8 But of the Son he says
Hebrews1:9 Thou hast loved righteousness and hated lawlessness; therefore God
Hebrews1:10 And
Hebrews1:11 they will perish
Hebrews1:12 like a mantle thou wilt roll them up
Hebrews1:13 But to what angel has he ever said
Hebrews1:14 Are they not all ministering spirits sent forth to serve
Hebrews2:1 Therefore we must pay the closer attention to what we have heard
Hebrews2:2 For if the message declared by angels was valid and every transgression or disobedience received a just retribution
Hebrews2:3 how shall we escape if we neglect such a great salvation? It was declared at first by the Lord
Hebrews2:4 while God also bore witness by signs and wonders and various miracles and by gifts of the Holy Spirit distributed according to his own will.
Hebrews2:5 For it was not to angels that God subjected the world to come
Hebrews2:6 It has been testified somewhere
Hebrews2:7 Thou didst make him for a little while lower than the angels
Hebrews2:8 putting everything in subjection under his feet.” Now in putting everything in subjection to him
Hebrews2:9 But we see Jesus
Hebrews2:10 For it was fitting that he
Hebrews2:11 For he who sanctifies and those who are sanctified have all one origin. That is why he is not ashamed to call them brethren
Hebrews2:12 saying
Hebrews2:13 And again
Hebrews2:14 Since therefore the children share in flesh and blood
Hebrews2:15 and deliver all those who through fear of death were subject to lifelong bondage.
Hebrews2:16 For surely it is not with angels that he is concerned but with the descendants of Abraham.
Hebrews2:17 Therefore he had to be made like his brethren in every respect
Hebrews2:18 For because he himself has suffered and been tempted
Hebrews3:1 Therefore
Hebrews3:2 He was faithful to him who appointed him
Hebrews3:3 Yet Jesus has been counted worthy of as much more glory than Moses as the builder of a house has more honor than the house.
Hebrews3:4 (For every house is built by some one
Hebrews3:5 Now Moses was faithful in all God's house as a servant
Hebrews3:6 but Christ was faithful over God's house as a son. And we are his house if we hold fast our confidence and pride in our hope.
Hebrews3:7 Therefore
Hebrews3:8 do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion
Hebrews3:9 where your fathers put me to the test and saw my works for forty years.
Hebrews3:10 Therefore I was provoked with that generation
Hebrews3:11 As I swore in my wrath
Hebrews3:12 Take care
Hebrews3:13 But exhort one another every day
Hebrews3:14 For we share in Christ
Hebrews3:15 while it is said
Hebrews3:16 Who were they that heard and yet were rebellious? Was it not all those who left Egypt under the leadership of Moses?
Hebrews3:17 And with whom was he provoked forty years? Was it not with those who sinned
Hebrews3:18 And to whom did he swear that they should never enter his rest
Hebrews3:19 So we see that they were unable to enter because of unbelief.
Hebrews4:1 Therefore
Hebrews4:2 For good news came to us just as to them; but the message which they heard did not benefit them
Hebrews4:3 For we who have believed enter that rest
Hebrews4:4 For he has somewhere spoken of the seventh day in this way
Hebrews4:5 And again in this place he said
Hebrews4:6 Since therefore it remains for some to enter it
Hebrews4:7 again he sets a certain day
Hebrews4:8 For if Joshua had given them rest
Hebrews4:9 So then
Hebrews4:10 for whoever enters God's rest also ceases from his labors as God did from his.
Hebrews4:11 Let us therefore strive to enter that rest
Hebrews4:12 For the word of God is living and active
Hebrews4:13 And before him no creature is hidden
Hebrews4:14 Since then we have a great high priest who has passed through the heavens
Hebrews4:15 For we have not a high priest who is unable to sympathize with our weaknesses
Hebrews4:16 Let us then with confidence draw near to the throne of grace
Hebrews5:1 For every high priest chosen from among men is appointed to act on behalf of men in relation to God
Hebrews5:2 He can deal gently with the ignorant and wayward
Hebrews5:3 Because of this he is bound to offer sacrifice for his own sins as well as for those of the people.
Hebrews5:4 And one does not take the honor upon himself
Hebrews5:5 So also Christ did not exalt himself to be made a high priest
Hebrews5:6 as he says also in another place
Hebrews5:7 In the days of his flesh
Hebrews5:8 Although he was a Son
Hebrews5:9 and being made perfect he became the source of eternal salvation to all who obey him
Hebrews5:10 being designated by God a high priest after the order of Melchizedek.
Hebrews5:11 About this we have much to say which is hard to explain
Hebrews5:12 For though by this time you ought to be teachers
Hebrews5:13 for every one who lives on milk is unskilled in the word of righteousness
Hebrews5:14 But solid food is for the mature
Hebrews6:1 Therefore let us leave the elementary doctrine of Christ and go on to maturity
Hebrews6:2 with instruction about ablutions
Hebrews6:3 And this we will do if God permits.
Hebrews6:4 For it is impossible to restore again to repentance those who have once been enlightened
Hebrews6:5 and have tasted the goodness of the word of God and the powers of the age to come
Hebrews6:6 if they then commit apostasy
Hebrews6:7 For land which has drunk the rain that often falls upon it
Hebrews6:8 But if it bears thorns and thistles
Hebrews6:9 Though we speak thus
Hebrews6:10 For God is not so unjust as to overlook your work and the love which you showed for his sake in serving the saints
Hebrews6:11 And we desire each one of you to show the same earnestness in realizing the full assurance of hope until the end
Hebrews6:12 so that you may not be sluggish
Hebrews6:13 For when God made a promise to Abraham
Hebrews6:14 saying
Hebrews6:15 And thus Abraham
Hebrews6:16 Men indeed swear by a greater than themselves
Hebrews6:17 So when God desired to show more convincingly to the heirs of the promise the unchangeable character of his purpose
Hebrews6:18 so that through two unchangeable things
Hebrews6:19 We have this as a sure and steadfast anchor of the soul
Hebrews6:20 where Jesus has gone as a forerunner on our behalf
Hebrews7:1 For this Melchizedek
Hebrews7:2 and to him Abraham apportioned a tenth part of everything. He is first
Hebrews7:3 He is without father or mother or genealogy
Hebrews7:4 See how great he is! Abraham the patriarch gave him a tithe of the spoils.
Hebrews7:5 And those descendants of Levi who receive the priestly office have a commandment in the law to take tithes from the people
Hebrews7:6 But this man who has not their genealogy received tithes from Abraham and blessed him who had the promises.
Hebrews7:7 It is beyond dispute that the inferior is blessed by the superior.
Hebrews7:8 Here tithes are received by mortal men; there
Hebrews7:9 One might even say that Levi himself
Hebrews7:10 for he was still in the loins of his ancestor when Melchizedek met him.
Hebrews7:11 Now if perfection had been attainable through the Levitical priesthood (for under it the people received the law)
Hebrews7:12 For when there is a change in the priesthood
Hebrews7:13 For the one of whom these things are spoken belonged to another tribe
Hebrews7:14 For it is evident that our Lord was descended from Judah
Hebrews7:15 This becomes even more evident when another priest arises in the likeness of Melchizedek
Hebrews7:16 who has become a priest
Hebrews7:17 For it is witnessed of him
Hebrews7:18 On the one hand
Hebrews7:19 (for the law made nothing perfect); on the other hand
Hebrews7:20 And it was not without an oath.
Hebrews7:21 Those who formerly became priests took their office without an oath
Hebrews7:22 This makes Jesus the surety of a better covenant.
Hebrews7:23 The former priests were many in number
Hebrews7:24 but he holds his priesthood permanently
Hebrews7:25 Consequently he is able for all time to save those who draw near to God through him
Hebrews7:26 For it was fitting that we should have such a high priest
Hebrews7:27 He has no need
Hebrews7:28 Indeed
Hebrews8:1 Now the point in what we are saying is this: we have such a high priest
Hebrews8:2 a minister in the sanctuary and the true tent which is set up not by man but by the Lord.
Hebrews8:3 For every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices; hence it is necessary for this priest also to have something to offer.
Hebrews8:4 Now if he were on earth
Hebrews8:5 They serve a copy and shadow of the heavenly sanctuary; for when Moses was about to erect the tent
Hebrews8:6 But as it is
Hebrews8:7 For if that first covenant had been faultless
Hebrews8:8 For he finds fault with them when he says: “The days will come
Hebrews8:9 not like the covenant that I made with their fathers on the day when I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt; for they did not continue in my covenant
Hebrews8:10 This is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days
Hebrews8:11 And they shall not teach every one his fellow or every one his brother
Hebrews8:12 For I will be merciful toward their iniquities
Hebrews8:13 In speaking of a new covenant he treats the first as obsolete. And what is becoming obsolete and growing old is ready to vanish away.
Hebrews9:1 Now even the first covenant had regulations for worship and an earthly sanctuary.
Hebrews9:2 For a tent was prepared
Hebrews9:3 Behind the second curtain stood a tent called the Holy of Holies
Hebrews9:4 having the golden altar of incense and the ark of the covenant covered on all sides with gold
Hebrews9:5 above it were the cherubim of glory overshadowing the mercy seat. Of these things we cannot now speak in detail.
Hebrews9:6 These preparations having thus been made
Hebrews9:7 but into the second only the high priest goes
Hebrews9:8 By this the Holy Spirit indicates that the way into the sanctuary is not yet opened as long as the outer tent is still standing
Hebrews9:9 (which is symbolic for the present age). According to this arrangement
Hebrews9:10 but deal only with food and drink and various ablutions
Hebrews9:11 But when Christ appeared as a high priest of the good things that have come
Hebrews9:12 he entered once for all into the Holy Place
Hebrews9:13 For if the sprinkling of defiled persons with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a heifer sanctifies for the purification of the flesh
Hebrews9:14 how much more shall the blood of Christ
Hebrews9:15 Therefore he is the mediator of a new covenant
Hebrews9:16 For where a will is involved
Hebrews9:17 For a will takes effect only at death
Hebrews9:18 Hence even the first covenant was not ratified without blood.
Hebrews9:19 For when every commandment of the law had been declared by Moses to all the people
Hebrews9:20 saying
Hebrews9:21 And in the same way he sprinkled with the blood both the tent and all the vessels used in worship.
Hebrews9:22 Indeed
Hebrews9:23 Thus it was necessary for the copies of the heavenly things to be purified with these rites
Hebrews9:24 For Christ has entered
Hebrews9:25 Nor was it to offer himself repeatedly
Hebrews9:26 for then he would have had to suffer repeatedly since the foundation of the world. But as it is
Hebrews9:27 And just as it is appointed for men to die once
Hebrews9:28 so Christ
Hebrews10:1 For since the law has but a shadow of the good things to come instead of the true form of these realities
Hebrews10:2 Otherwise
Hebrews10:3 But in these sacrifices there is a reminder of sin year after year.
Hebrews10:4 For it is impossible that the blood of bulls and goats should take away sins.
Hebrews10:5 Consequently
Hebrews10:6 in burnt offerings and sin offerings thou hast taken no pleasure.
Hebrews10:7 Then I said
Hebrews10:8 When he said above
Hebrews10:9 then he added
Hebrews10:10 And by that will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
Hebrews10:11 And every priest stands daily at his service
Hebrews10:12 But when Christ had offered for all time a single sacrifice for sins
Hebrews10:13 then to wait until his enemies should be made a stool for his feet.
Hebrews10:14 For by a single offering he has perfected for all time those who are sanctified.
Hebrews10:15 And the Holy Spirit also bears witness to us; for after saying
Hebrews10:16 “This is the covenant that I will make with them after those days
Hebrews10:17 then he adds
Hebrews10:18 Where there is forgiveness of these
Hebrews10:19 Therefore
Hebrews10:20 by the new and living way which he opened for us through the curtain
Hebrews10:21 and since we have a great priest over the house of God
Hebrews10:22 let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith
Hebrews10:23 Let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering
Hebrews10:24 and let us consider how to stir up one another to love and good works
Hebrews10:25 not neglecting to meet together
Hebrews10:26 For if we sin deliberately after receiving the knowledge of the truth
Hebrews10:27 but a fearful prospect of judgment
Hebrews10:28 A man who has violated the law of Moses dies without mercy at the testimony of two or three witnesses.
Hebrews10:29 How much worse punishment do you think will be deserved by the man who has spurned the Son of God
Hebrews10:30 For we know him who said
Hebrews10:31 It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
Hebrews10:32 But recall the former days when
Hebrews10:33 sometimes being publicly exposed to abuse and affliction
Hebrews10:34 For you had compassion on the prisoners
Hebrews10:35 Therefore do not throw away your confidence
Hebrews10:36 For you have need of endurance
Hebrews10:37 “For yet a little while
Hebrews10:38 but my righteous one shall live by faith
Hebrews10:39 But we are not of those who shrink back and are destroyed
Hebrews11:1 Now faith is the assurance of things hoped for
Hebrews11:2 For by it the men of old received divine approval.
Hebrews11:3 By faith we understand that the world was created by the word of God
Hebrews11:4 By faith Abel offered to God a more acceptable sacrifice than Cain
Hebrews11:5 By faith Enoch was taken up so that he should not see death; and he was not found
Hebrews11:6 And without faith it is impossible to please him. For whoever would draw near to God must believe that he exists and that he rewards those who seek him.
Hebrews11:7 By faith Noah
Hebrews11:8 By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place which he was to receive as an inheritance; and he went out
Hebrews11:9 By faith he sojourned in the land of promise
Hebrews11:10 For he looked forward to the city which has foundations
Hebrews11:11 By faith Sarah herself received power to conceive
Hebrews11:12 Therefore from one man
Hebrews11:13 These all died in faith
Hebrews11:14 For people who speak thus make it clear that they are seeking a homeland.
Hebrews11:15 If they had been thinking of that land from which they had gone out
Hebrews11:16 But as it is
Hebrews11:17 By faith Abraham
Hebrews11:18 of whom it was said
Hebrews11:19 He considered that God was able to raise men even from the dead; hence
Hebrews11:20 By faith Isaac invoked future blessings on Jacob and Esau.
Hebrews11:21 By faith Jacob
Hebrews11:22 By faith Joseph
Hebrews11:23 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:24 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:25 choosing rather to share ill-treatment with the people of God than to enjoy the fleeting pleasures of sin.
Hebrews11:26 He considered abuse suffered for the Christ greater wealth than the treasures of Egypt
Hebrews11:27 By faith he left Egypt
Hebrews11:28 By faith he kept the Passover and sprinkled the blood
Hebrews11:29 By faith the people crossed the Red Sea as if on dry land; but the Egyptians
Hebrews11:30 By faith the walls of Jericho fell down after they had been encircled for seven days.
Hebrews11:31 By faith Rahab the harlot did not perish with those who were disobedient
Hebrews11:32 And what more shall I say? For time would fail me to tell of Gideon
Hebrews11:33 who through faith conquered kingdoms
Hebrews11:34 quenched raging fire
Hebrews11:35 Women received their dead by resurrection. Some were tortured
Hebrews11:36 Others suffered mocking and scourging
Hebrews11:37 They were stoned
Hebrews11:38 of whom the world was not worthy—wandering over deserts and mountains
Hebrews11:39 And all these
Hebrews11:40 since God had foreseen something better for us
Hebrews12:1 Therefore
Hebrews12:2 looking to Jesus the pioneer and perfecter of our faith
Hebrews12:3 Consider him who endured from sinners such hostility against himself
Hebrews12:4 In your struggle against sin you have not yet resisted to the point of shedding your blood.
Hebrews12:5 And have you forgotten the exhortation which addresses you as sons?— “My son
Hebrews12:6 For the Lord disciplines him whom he loves
Hebrews12:7 It is for discipline that you have to endure. God is treating you as sons; for what son is there whom his father does not discipline?
Hebrews12:8 If you are left without discipline
Hebrews12:9 Besides this
Hebrews12:10 For they disciplined us for a short time at their pleasure
Hebrews12:11 For the moment all discipline seems painful rather than pleasant; later it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness to those who have been trained by it.
Hebrews12:12 Therefore lift your drooping hands and strengthen your weak knees
Hebrews12:13 and make straight paths for your feet
Hebrews12:14 Strive for peace with all men
Hebrews12:15 See to it that no one fail to obtain the grace of God; that no “root of bitterness” spring up and cause trouble
Hebrews12:16 that no one be immoral or irreligious like Esau
Hebrews12:17 For you know that afterward
Hebrews12:18 For you have not come to what may be touched
Hebrews12:19 and the sound of a trumpet
Hebrews12:20 For they could not endure the order that was given
Hebrews12:21 Indeed
Hebrews12:22 But you have come to Mount Zion and to the city of the living God
Hebrews12:23 and to the assembly of the first-born who are enrolled in heaven
Hebrews12:24 and to Jesus
Hebrews12:25 See that you do not refuse him who is speaking. For if they did not escape when they refused him who warned them on earth
Hebrews12:26 His voice then shook the earth; but now he has promised
Hebrews12:27 This phrase
Hebrews12:28 Therefore let us be grateful for receiving a kingdom that cannot be shaken
Hebrews12:29 for our God is a consuming fire.

Hebrews13:2 Do not neglect to show hospitality to strangers
Hebrews13:3 Remember those who are in prison
Hebrews13:4 Let marriage be held in honor among all
Hebrews13:5 Keep your life free from love of money
Hebrews13:6 Hence we can confidently say
Hebrews13:7 Remember your leaders
Hebrews13:8 Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and for ever.
Hebrews13:9 Do not be led away by diverse and strange teachings; for it is well that the heart be strengthened by grace
Hebrews13:10 We have an altar from which those who serve the tent have no right to eat.
Hebrews13:11 For the bodies of those animals whose blood is brought into the sanctuary by the high priest as a sacrifice for sin are burned outside the camp.
Hebrews13:12 So Jesus also suffered outside the gate in order to sanctify the people through his own blood.
Hebrews13:13 Therefore let us go forth to him outside the camp
Hebrews13:14 For here we have no lasting city
Hebrews13:15 Through him then let us continually offer up a sacrifice of praise to God
Hebrews13:16 Do not neglect to do good and to share what you have
Hebrews13:17 Obey your leaders and submit to them; for they are keeping watch over your souls

Hebrews13:19 I urge you the more earnestly to do this in order that I may be restored to you the sooner.

Hebrews13:21 equip you with everything good that you may do his will
Hebrews13:22 I appeal to you
Hebrews13:23 You should understand that our brother Timothy has been released
Hebrews13:24 Greet all your leaders and all the saints. Those who come from Italy send you greetings.
Hebrews13:25 Grace be with all of you. Amen.

James1:8

James1:14 but each person is tempted when he is lured and enticed by his own desire.
James1:15 Then desire when it has conceived gives birth to sin; and sin when it is full-grown brings forth death.
James1:16 Do not be deceived
James1:17 Every good endowment and every perfect gift is from above
James1:18 Of his own will he brought us forth by the word of truth that we should be a kind of first fruits of his creatures.

James1:20 for the anger of man does not work the righteousness of God.

James3:1 Let not many of you become teachers
James3:2 For we all make many mistakes
James3:3 If we put bits into the mouths of horses that they may obey us
James3:4 Look at the ships also; though they are so great and are driven by strong winds
James3:5 So the tongue is a little member and boasts of great things. How great a forest is set ablaze by a small fire!
James3:6 And the tongue is a fire. The tongue is an unrighteous world among our members
James3:7 For every kind of beast and bird
James3:8 but no human being can tame the tongue—a restless evil

James3:16 For where jealousy and selfish ambition exist
James3:17 But the wisdom from above is first pure
James3:18 And the harvest of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.

2Peter1:4 by which he has granted to us his precious and very great promises
2Peter1:5 For this very reason make every effort to supplement your faith with virtue
2Peter1:6 and knowledge with self-control
2Peter1:7 and godliness with brotherly affection
2Peter1:8 For if these things are yours and abound

2Peter1:10 Therefore
2Peter1:11 so there will be richly provided for you an entrance into the eternal kingdom of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ.
2Peter1:12 Therefore I intend always to remind you of these things
2Peter1:13 I think it right
2Peter1:14 since I know that the putting off of my body will be soon
2Peter1:15 And I will see to it that after my departure you may be able at any time to recall these things.

2Peter1:21 because no prophecy ever came by the impulse of man

2Peter3:1 This is now the second letter that I have written to you
2Peter3:2 that you should remember the predictions of the holy prophets and the commandment of the Lord and Savior through your apostles.
2Peter3:3 First of all you must understand this
2Peter3:4 and saying
2Peter3:5 They deliberately ignore this fact
2Peter3:6 through which the world that then existed was deluged with water and perished.
2Peter3:7 But by the same word the heavens and earth that now exist have been stored up for fire
2Peter3:8 But do not ignore this one fact
2Peter3:9 The Lord is not slow about his promise as some count slowness
2Peter3:10 But the day of the Lord will come like a thief
2Peter3:11 Since all these things are thus to be dissolved
2Peter3:12 waiting for and hastening the coming of the day of God
2Peter3:13 But according to his promise we wait for new heavens and a new earth in which righteousness dwells.
2Peter3:14 Therefore
2Peter3:15 And count the forbearance of our Lord as salvation. So also our beloved brother Paul wrote to you according to the wisdom given him
2Peter3:16 speaking of this as he does in all his letters. There are some things in them hard to understand
2Peter3:17 You therefore
2Peter3:18 But grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ. To him be the glory both now and to the day of eternity. Amen.
